title: Hostal las Vegas

Even with the hi parking charge we still came out Ok because the room was reasonably priced.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"veggieb2016","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QcLvGDdMkm_athQbRQtTYCQxjNUnlrxzopfUA8yGN0YYBZmB4uuNayh0HxTR0N7aNnuMg=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The Hostal is located next to a kebab shop in a street corner about 30 min by foot from the city center, we didn't mind walking as there's a lot to see on the way.||Once we got there the lady at the reception was very friendly and the room was simple but clean, we had a private bathroom which was very clean and they had daily housekeeping.||They said there's a common kitchen but we never went there.||Its a nice place to stay if you're travelling in a group or just want to stay somewhere cheap while you explore the city.||The only big issue we had was the noise from the street, walls and windows are very thin so there was a lot of noise coming from the street at 3 am and during most of the night, apart from that its a small place, not very easy to find if you dont have a GPS but very close to LIDL and restaurants.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"LoryJitariu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QdVsxDWqDDLG62Q3M3dmLlHORJs5jz0NWRJO3cP21KtRNPfosO7RpofjnbUgpyNjOTDew=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"good one night stay at this hostal, we booked a triple room with bathroom, aircon, small but very clean room, 10 min walk from Nuevos ministerios metro station and 2 min walk from cuatro caminos metro station, many stores nearby, lidl and a good restaurant Nebraska.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"ATTIQ REHMAN","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKa-LvRhPXMdJDteZBCiMJeqJ_gz8V1j7uHqgbUPb28_R7_JA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Economical place to stay in the heart of Madrid, cooperative staff","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: DEBOD HOSTEL
address: Calle del Buen Suceso, 23, Moncloa - Aravaca, 28008 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Марія Ластівка","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWeKXd0e6_fiTYeIcOby4a90BEvt5EBnYyIjBZI8an_IYLd6CjZCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Highly recommended!

We arrived in a very difficult situation — in the middle of the night, after the apartment we had booked stopped responding and left us without accommodation. The staff of this hostel welcomed us immediately and truly saved our trip.

The staff is always willing to help with anything — calling a taxi, giving advice, or solving any issue. This is a place run by people with big hearts.

title: HostelEstrella20
address: C. de la Estrella, 20, Centro, 28004 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jonathan Garry","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJIfw5vGjSHShSTWQ8wjH3a7d0HdmVpdcMtPiKo1x3IKK61mg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I’ve stayed in hostels all over the world — South America, Europe, Australia, Canada, the US — and Estrella Hostel in Madrid is by far the worst experience I’ve ever had.

From the moment I checked in, it felt dirty, poorly maintained, and honestly a bit creepy. The entire place had a terrible smell, and the cleanliness of the sheets and blankets was questionable. The “blanket” wasn’t even a proper blanket — it felt like a thin, plastic, artificial material that didn’t seem washed at all.

Throughout the rooms, the lights would flicker on and off constantly, which was disturbing and would be especially dangerous for anyone with photosensitivity or epilepsy. The noise level was also awful — my room faced the street and it was loud all night, making sleep nearly impossible.

For what they charge, the quality is shockingly poor. It’s not cheap, and yet the facilities are worse than hostels I stayed in for a quarter of the price. The kitchen is basically non-existent: a stool, a microwave thrown into a corner, plastic cups, and no proper place to cook or sit. Completely impractical and misleading.

The lockers are essentially pointless. Instead of actual lockers, you’re given a code, which doesn’t appear to be changed regularly. Anyone who memorized a previous guest’s code would have access to people’s belongings. That did not feel secure at all.

When I asked for a towel — given how bad the experience already was — I was told I’d have to pay for it, which added insult to injury.

The atmosphere was unsocial, uncomfortable, and unsettling. Nothing about it felt welcoming or safe.
And honestly, after staying there, I find it hard to believe many of the very positive reviews online. My experience was so extremely negative that I’m skeptical about how those ratings came to be.

Overall: absolutely the worst hostel I’ve ever stayed in. I cannot recommend it to anyone. Avoid this place.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marie Testa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKqb1qdvpRgaUAnCCjzC7a-NIayJJJqOc9oIAaUljwToETHVQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I’ve stayed in plenty of hostels, and this was by far one of the worst experiences I’ve had. The moment I walked in, the place smelled bad and felt dark and neglected. The “common area” was anything but social — just a small, gloomy space with a light that kept flickering, which made it uncomfortable to sit in for even a few minutes.

Cleanliness was clearly not a priority. The kitchen was barely functional: only a microwave, plastic cups and cutlery, and nothing you could actually cook with. It felt more like an afterthought than a usable space.

My room wasn’t any better. Getting into the top bunk was awkward and borderline unsafe because of how difficult it was to access. I could hear constant noise from the kitchen through the walls, and even the small reading light at my bed was flickering nonstop. The bedding felt cheap and unpleasant — the sheets had a plastic-like texture, and the “blanket” was basically something you’d throw on a couch, not something appropriate for a guest bed. I genuinely doubt it gets washed regularly.

Security wasn’t great either. The lockers use a simple four-digit PIN provided by the hostel, and it’s easy to imagine other guests knowing or keeping the same code if staff don’t reset it properly between stays. It didn’t feel secure at all.

Overall, the place felt dirty, smelly, and poorly maintained. I wouldn’t recommend this hostel to anyone. There are far better options in Madrid for the same price or even cheaper.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Miglė Grigalevičiūtė","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKMoBD0_TIHl1CXaCMba8JtOz4XZzIKn2xMVKMS0eETgwFfkg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"One star is generous for the price we paid

Unfortunately, this hostel was one of the worst we've stayed in, especially considering the high price. We've stayed in many hostels—some for the same price or even less—and the conditions there were five times better.

A few important things to note:

Shower conditions are very poor—very small space, uncomfortable and locker is broken so you can't lock.

Toilets, however, were clean and well-kept.

In the women’s room, the beds are extremely short. If you're taller than 165 cm, you literally won't be able to stretch your legs.

A male cleaning staff member everyday enter the room unannounced, which was shocking and unacceptable. I paid extra to stay in a female-only room for privacy, not to worry about someone walking in while I'm changing.

For sleeping, you're given a mattress cover and pillow cover, but the blankets are reused by several people without any cover. That’s unhygienic and quite disturbing—especially knowing they’re not washed between guests.

The kitchen is poorly equipped: only a few thick plastic plates, the rest are all cheap plastic cups and utensils. Not even a proper knife available.

To be honest, in these conditions, I wouldn’t stay here unless it was free or maybe €10 a night at most. The current rating on the booking site is way too high and doesn’t reflect the real experience.
I don't recommend or they have to lower the price.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mariel Camargo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBHFOsLScdkLs430-n4vwZ96vmMiOsRBm_MI4c_xtX9B4GwtgrzA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The only slay thing about this hostel is its location. Literally the rooms are horrible. First they smell of mildew and have zero air circulation. Second, the bedsheets they provide you with are thin as freak. I don’t know what the thread count is but it is literally giving they bought them for one euro.  Also the comforter is not even a comforter it’s a literal throw blanket. 😂 The locker situation is so annoying too bc it’s small and it’s deep rather than up and down. They do have curtains which helps, but that is probably only thing that is okay.

Showers are okay but definitely silly and open air & right by the kitchen which I found weird too. Idk not my favorite. Very constrained too. The bathrooms were ok. Kitchen was horrible. You cannot cook & they only have a toaster and a microwave. Refrigerator was full and dirty. Also not a proper dishwashing station. The sink is a handwashing station. So flop ultimately 😂😂. Please save yourself the trouble and book with another hostel. Maybe go to the neighboring neighborhoods or even lavapies- please you will thank me.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Patchy Blonde","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI-VVUZJU2CljWHOTsQSWxWDxS94_Lwgr17kNL1TQ39LGBUHQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Our stay at this Hostel was extremely unpleasant and unprofessional. Me and my other female friend were both in the mixed dorm, and had two men making inappropriate comments, staring and generally creating an uncomfortable atmosphere in the room. It came to a point where one of the men had opened my friend’s sleep curtain whilst she had been sleeping, and hanging around in-front of my bed whilst I was in there. I know this isn’t the hostel’s fault, however when we brought it to the attention of the manager, he simply claimed “he’s just annoying” and that there wasn’t anything they can do about it. No refund given, no room swap. They also defended him because he always stayed there and so brought money in. So we left immediately because we no longer felt it was a safe space to stay. As a manager of a hostel, you have a duty to take care of your residents, especially female or any minority residents who are most at risk of assault/harm. I don’t recommend anyone to stay here for this reason, I would hate for anything worse to happen to someone else. title: Cubik Rooms

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Handan Tek","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV-89KN0F4Got8aPElvEtJIlENHDnVaOjTR3ymgMqLuHpBWw1qBiQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Kübik Hostel is the best hostel I’ve ever seen. The cubicles provide people with private space, and they even include towels and a small air conditioner. The kitchen area, bathrooms, and toilets are spotless and very well-maintained. I was amazed by how much care and attention this hostel has received. It’s very cool inside, and the automatic air freshener makes the whole place smell wonderful. The housekeeper is a warm, friendly woman with great work discipline. I feel both valued and safe here. Your belongings are also secure thanks to the cubicle doors and locks. I also really liked the management system of this hostel — everything is online and automated.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bogdan Margarit","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLH6Al_5zzrdi_zA4ks5BvK7ELDdI6yebn26UjqZ-y56mTPPw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I needed to stay one night in Madrid, so I though this place was a good option. I have contacted them on WhatsApp about this issue, and they have assured me that they will send someone to rectify the problem. It's needless to say that nobody came to check anything.

My piece of advice for anyone who's thinking to book a cubical here: don't.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sagarika Bhattacharjee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVaA0UOtTjab1tiThd0HO8D2ZroWXIPYpffJc-5eYb70-MhkNKCoA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Don't visit during summers. title: Safestay Madrid Central
address: C. de Sagasta, 22, Centro, 28004 Madrid
descriptions: Casual dorms & private rooms in a low-key hostel with an industrial-style cafe & a rooftop bar.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Daniyar Zhumatayev","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPxkM4W-eHCyNFiD4ZFBrRN-t-Pj2-qGTBjchCm-KZz7gUnQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Longest check-in possible
It is crazy that only one person is working at front-desk when they know they are fully booked
Spent around 40minutes only to get the keys, considering I checked in online

Rooms though are quite nice. Very bright and spacious. Bed was comfortable. Everyone has it's one small locker in the room. However you have to bring the lock with you.

Toilets were clean, but having only one toilet on the whole floor for males, is quite strange.

For luggage room unfortunately you have to pay using bounce application, around 7EUR.

In general mixed feelings, but for this price it's okay.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kareem D","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVR9PWyI5KttB9VxU3TYvIbeNPaBKVK1nCCdeJrZj5sfbxdMfPz=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The hostel is well located, and I was happy to get a front 4-bed room with a balcony. The bed was clean and comfortable, the bathrooms were clean, and breakfast was basic but good for the price.

The front desk staff at check-in were kind, although the process was a bit slow. There’s also a €3 charge for luggage storage, which is good to know in advance.

Unfortunately, I had to check out two days early because my friend was placed in a room with no windows, which was filthy and had a strong sewer odor. When he asked for help, the management refused to switch rooms, saying none were available. They even sent someone to “inspect” the room, but instead of addressing the issue, they simply asked the other guests to shower — which was really disappointing and felt disrespectful.No resolution was offered by management

Because of that, my friend couldn’t stay, and we left two nights early. It was a shame, because our first night had actually been very good.

As a suggestion, I recommend lubricating all door hinges and, most importantly, showing more empathy toward guests when issues like this come up. The odor problem and poor handling by management really ruined what could have been a nice stay.

Overall, our first impression was positive, but the experience that followed turned into a bad experience. I hope management takes this feedback seriously — the location and potential are great, but guest care and cleanliness need improvement.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Saptak Bose","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI1jFUI1quFiGhNQNRSI0YkTh-aVhc8_egvS5fFYpSjH1e_37w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Only the location scores good for this place, thats it. The negatives :

1. WiFi - its a disaster and doesn't work at all. Worked for none of us in the rooms

2. The pillows are so uncomfortable that I couldn't sleep on both the nights I was there

3. Luggage storage- they mention that you could store luggage with them (at a fee ofcourse) but never mention that its a tiny room which runs out of capacity. On the morning of my departure I was told they can't keep my luggage so I need to look somewhere else or walk the city with it

4. Staffing is also very sad. At any given time there's only one person present at checkout or checkin, which makes the queues enormous. This, while other staff are roaming around but not helping with check in. Whenever I crossed yhe reception there was always a queue of atleast 45 mins of frustrated travelers in line. Some staff members arent very polite either

5. Its a very basic hostel with not so many amenities and the price : quality ratio is not reasonable enough for Madrid

6. Everything costs extra including bedsheets, towels, which also is an extension of point #5","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Angelo Saly","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0xKUfrAn4S-VfAPcHNw8g7Ueg806kqWobioQGCdWAlIevhpeM=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Its better to shut down this place. There is no basic cutlery like plate or spoon provided. Not even a fridge to put your food. The toilet smells and no cleaning. The rooms are with infused oil smell which does not let you have a good sleep. The lady at the reception when asked for cutlery said it under breqkfast dwpartment and she can not do anything. Please do not book this place andall websites should remive this hostel from their listing. Pathetic place. The rooms are shared with many old people 60+year and there is no air ciculation. The guy at the reception is too arrogant and does not allow to keep window bit open for the air to pass.the room smells like rotten rat.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAXkQkhacizGiGutcoHs9iN&fid=0x0:0x4cd829d07cb863eb"],"When":"2025-12-28"},{"Name":"Nekochi Neko","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLHyHqYDlf_J5HPfghv6VQMqdV4lzcsVWFT5Yuyo6tIlQ7ctg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"100% worth staying here. I DO recommend it. It's an awesome pace to stay in!!! The one improvement I would kindly suggest is to clarify in a pop-up message that 3 or 5 euros are requested as a refundable deposit for the key of the room, even though you're including it in terms and conditions, but almost nobody reads it.

Besides that, all good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rob Wynn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUqAKL0wlgr5jrVygQQcMwj5Iq801Mn6znfzpRxWuHLIfIkCCDZ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My stay was perfectly fine. Only stayed one night. What really impressed me was the woman who served me at breakfast. She was very considerate to make sure I had everything I needed.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"xavierrQ2654AQ","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qdxz9DVikvohXc65OX6mVR64CQ0nRiRrBQIjnQCKtPsKCujWRrdT0XmrY7V1jcjGNLs6Q=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The hostel did not feel "safe". The balcony doors in the dorm did not shut or lock. The balcony was shared with two other rooms. The lockers also did not lock. The bathrooms were somewhat exposed and the sinks area was mixed gender and open to the general corridor. There was a small area on my floor which was full of people on the last night having a gathering past midnight right outside my door. Hostel was not particularly clean or comfortable, the beds and pillows were not very good. No kitchen. Positioned on a very loud main road, made louder by the fact the window would not close. Nearby clubs went until past 4am, sleep was almost impossible. Would not recommend for solo travellers. Location was really good - the only good thing about this hostel","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mina Waston","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKt0nk9jpNoa1LEon1yN-BLMGD9PkooVQaAJqoolES27DHIag=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Unprofessional and a total joke. The staff has no idea how to handle guests and lacks transparency and basic politeness. They enforce rules and charges that were never disclosed during the booking process essentially trapping guests with surprise fees. Between the broken online check-in and the arrogant attitude at the desk, it’s clear this place has no interest in guest satisfaction. Avoid this place at all costs!

Update

I read the requirements previously and according to the info provided, you only require photo identification and a credit card nothing more! It seems you forgot to update the information on your pages. Refusing a refund due to unclear or outdated info is fraudulent. It is your responsibility to clarify the check-in rules since they aren't listed guests should not have to pay for errors made on your side.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAicmDCrHCakeWFgFYcngBz&fid=0x0:0x4cd829d07cb863eb"],"When":"2026-2-26"}]

title: H21 Rooms
address: C. de las Huertas, 21, Centro, 28014 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Andrew Whitelaw","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIQs0b2XZBTyDA7rzplyKRjWJhmnsxcOv3HGnK03jnZtV99Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Went through booking as seemed like a good place to stay.

No hot water, with push button start (similar to a beach shower).

Thin walls so we could hear everything between rooms and outside.

Reception didn’t seem to care, wouldn’t recommend booking.

Good location and the price was the only positive.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Aleksandra O","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHojZe2dxB1VsCvuN70M3aInoVaficwGURBiOEaPv5J_gIHZOE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"+ the location is quite okey as close to the center but that the only plus
- the young landy at reception looks like she’s working there as punishment. No help at all when requested and do not taking interest in her guests.
- no internet in the room as advertised. WiFi working only in reception. I reported that, but according to the lady from reception, WiFi in rooms is working, even though is not… no help given
- after first night I was bitten by bed bags.  Room 307 so be careful.
- the common showers and toilets are quite old and without privacy. The only closed part is small shower cabin itself but the doors are see-through anyway.
- the hairdryer is working only for few seconds because it’s overheating and it’s turning off itself. So quite useless.
- the air conditioning in the room has only option on and off so it’s hot because the room has no window or it’s extremely cold if the air conditioning is working.
- we spend there 4 night and the towels or sheets were not changed at all. It’s difficult to keep the towels dry in the room without window so I think that something this place can have a look at…
- the last but not least, the walls are thin as paper. You can hear literally everything. Also be advised to NOT come here with kids as every evening and morning you can hear the happy couples :) I do hope those are guests and not some private business run in this place.
In conclusion, I will be not coming back and I really do recommend to choose any other place rather then this while staying in the city.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Benjamin Schickel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUGSaobg0VGBQZFsekjv5LlD59jMESCKOdb7X-4_xFkT8dHDREs=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We had a private room with a shared bathroom. The bedroom is simple, the bed is comfortable, it was clean. We had a small window and a small balcony with a view on the street. The building inside looks kind of old and not in a very good condition, you can see it's on the cheap side. But it's fine to spend some nights in the city if you just need a place to sleep. The location within the city is great but mainly touristic. Two very small bathrooms were shared on one level by approx 10-15 rooms. They seem to be cleaned once or twice a day, so in the morning they look used after several persons taking a shower. Also toilet paper can be frequently empty, I had to ask once the receptionist to refill it in the morning. The bathrooms are not very well equiped, missing some space to put your clothes or utensils. All in all a good place with everything necessary in okay condition but not more than that. Staff was always friendly.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cesar Gomez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfDQV9OV0vV1PLm0THePG24ygkS0ZN6b_1yCYcUMYKsfJOu4b4gg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great quick little spot. Not bad, nothing amazing. Good room size. But they could've maybe added an extra blanket,  we just had one very thing small blanket. Maybe alil safe to store personal belongings if needed. We had a private room with private bathroom. We did enjoy the balcony very cute touch. Service was good, just ask and they can help you out with different things. Great location close to everything.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Niamh Coughlan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUEfDEAu59pQuzDk9Xj3iVCauLYMoa8O5P0yBSz9y1-MMCpZ1iS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We booked a couple months ago as the rooms looked spacious enough. We did chance it and try and ask for a room with a window when we first booked. When we arrived it’s literally like a prison cell, on our 3rd day we saw how much more spacious the other rooms are that were being cleaned and when we asked if we can move to one of them we were told they were booked. Our tiny cell of a room is also next door to a room with an en-suite where the shower is actually inside our room so whenever its turned on it’s like being in the shower with the person next door. If you’re in 210 god bless you because it’s not gunna be a comfortable stay. The walls are also so thin and you can hear everything so I would definitely bring earplugs. Definitely not a highlight to listen to someone wrench in the toilet all night. The cleanliness is good during the day but peak times of bathroom use expect hairs everywhere. The staff are friendly, just bad luck that they can’t rearrange us going into one of the empty rooms we saw getting cleaned ready for new guests. There’s also 0 ventilation in the room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEPSy0sXpuNiofw&fid=0x0:0xf68d321bfb8cc8e3"],"When":"2025-5-8"},{"Name":"黃莉媞","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCMWezGGST3xCjtwLsY-wSrjpu7HNRpPUHuz-8lUOFIPMhltE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The room is spacious, but it lacks basic amenities. One of the lights was broken, and although the staff said it would be fixed immediately, it never was. The bed was not clean, which is a big problem for people with sensitive skin. The shared bathroom was dirty, the shower system was very difficult to use, and there were no hooks inside the shower, meaning you have to hang your clothes outside the door and wait for no one to be around to open it. On top of that, in this freezing winter, there was no hot water! Additionally, the soundproofing was terrible. This place is only suitable for a short rest, and its only advantage is its central location.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gina Chen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUGeqpHOHVsmFnRcl_7KaxMMP1WPqui9J1TcYgDkX5Ehqj-1aIH=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The experience was so bad. The room is big but it’s so dirty. The sound-proofing is so terrible and one of the lights in room even is not working. As for the shared bathroom, I have to push the button to shower which means that the whole showering time I have to keep pushing the button to get the water. However, the water is cold. There’s no hot water!!! I am glad that I just stay one night there after this terrible experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_0JmnnQE&fid=0x0:0xf68d321bfb8cc8e3"],"When":"2025-1-20"},{"Name":"L Forss","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXixb_rIuiA1JLOX9xHpBMy6LHKFucPlL0Y8TpxS0skKdMNB80=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Had a room with toilet. The location is great, close both to Sol, Atocha and Retiro. Biggest draw back was that the sound proofing were really poor could hear every flush in the pipes and people talking in the hallway, doors closing and so on. But for such a great location and low cost I would for sure go back there again.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Very unpractical when arriving before 15:00, or leaving after 12:00. It made me consider another option. |- It would be simple to put lockers in the hallway, and enable the lock on the outer door during the day of arrival and departure. ||Communication at first very annoying.|- My simple repeated question by e-mail about that storage: no answer, only shoving off to a chatbot, or completely ignored. |- They NEVER answered the phone, only BS-message that 'their agents are busy'. Alternatively just walk to or from the Plaza de Espana. Convenient as I didn't use any buses, but the metro was a good way to get started or get back.|- Restaurants nearby. At least various fast-food and a cafeteria on the nearby square towards Plaza de Espana. Also consider VIPS for a decent meal.||Room|- Pretty new, neat, and clean. |- Needs some pegs on the walls.|- Major flaw: though the doors have a keypad, one can't use a code. One always has to take a phone out, or leave the door open. Even when just going to the toilet. One can't take a shower and leave the phone on a charger. And can't even properly put it somewhere in the bathroom. It would be simple to automatically assign a code by the hotel, or let the guest choose one. The locks even seem intended for the latter by a slide on the inside. I didn't get that working though. |- Second flaw. Rooms are quite noisy. Doors badly close, walls are thin. I don't want to hear what's happening on the toilet despite 2 doors in between, or hear my neighbour snoring, or worse. See the picture for the laughable doors.||Bathroom|- Shared. title: Hostal casa sofia
address: C. del Padre Rubio, 46, Tetuán, 28029 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"abdessamad DNIDEN","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJUqjmGhPksqUb6heiamsGHPzZoa2Ya26_O94uQQv9XT5oKwi5=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My Stay at This Hostel Was Absolutely the Worst Experience of My Life

This was, by far, the worst accommodation experience I have ever had. For the first time in my life, I stayed in a hostel where the kitchen closes just one hour after midnight, and all the stove burners and cooking utensils are removed. What’s even more frustrating is that if you order food from outside, there’s literally nowhere to eat it because even the tables are cleared away. This is truly bizarre and completely unjustified.

Beyond the kitchen issues, the entire hostel—with both floors—shares a single bathroom. The unpleasant odors are everywhere, making the environment extremely uncomfortable. On top of that, even though Madrid is currently experiencing a severe cold wave, the hostel provides only very thin blankets that are suitable for summer or very hot days, which makes sleeping almost impossible.

Overall, I would strongly advise against staying here unless you want a highly inconvenient and unpleasant experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAh8Nhu1j1ME-ubwubiACYw&fid=0x0:0x1a9951223f9c69ad"],"When":"2025-12-8"},{"Name":"Anass Anass","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ2h4QGdNp4-gg_v4jgIu1RYMPSMyR_HO1FYB-GX6fXrJJ8=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The hostel has a very unpleasant smell, and the condition of the toilets is extremely poor. The room was extremely dirty with a horrible smell, and I even found bugs in the blanket. It felt completely unclean and unhygienic. There was no control over who entered the room—people kept coming in and out, slamming doors in the middle of the night, being loud and disruptive. There was no staff to monitor the situation. I couldn’t get any sleep at all and ended up with a terrible headache. I just wonder how they are running a business in the heart of Madrid—really? No one overseeing this? Definitely not recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQ2ezj9gE&fid=0x0:0x1a9951223f9c69ad"],"When":"2025-3-5"},{"Name":"Trip.com Member","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeCck1CfANpZm9bBk3PHtP97Kh9k25HZfvq9yAWlN3BaIyjSHUPEfWkhAJmuYiJsH-_6g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The price is good. The staff are very kind. The people renting in it who you will share the room were mean.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cathal Ó Murchú","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo6hf8ZK2wcs4mDHtnqkr1N0gtQVgcMtj5GFaUNk7pt5J36dEkXw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not stay here.
The location is quiet, and close to Chamartin and Ventilla metro stops. It was also cheap.
The mattresses are inflatables which make a loud crinkling sound when you move. Mine was leaking constantly, I swapped it to a non leaking one by myself. The room had a window which did not close meaning it was freezing cold most nights. You are provided with a thin blanket and a pillow, that is all.
The staff have almost no English, are unhelpful and seem to want to make you as uncomfortable as possible.
There are no cooking facilities: one microwave and a kettle. These are only available 8 am to 3 pm and 5 pm to 10 pm. During these cooking times the staff would clean the floors telling everyone to leave the kitchen or the entrance area, there is literally nowhere else to go in the building. Every morning all the windows would be opened to the 2C winter air ensuring the public spaces were freezing cold constantly.
The upstairs toilets; only one had a toilet seat, which included access to a terrace littered in construction waste and cigarette butts.
The ceiling was flaking and crumbling in my partner's dorm, falling on to her bed daily.
I arrived back one evening to find all the beds and lockers in my room had been moved, meaning I had to hunt down my bed and locker.  The same lockers have no security on them.
The front door is regularly left in secured so anyone from the street can enter the building at will. You will not feel safe here.
The hostel also offers monthly rates, and the people who are living in the hostel long term behave as if they own the place.

You cannot tell the staff apart from the other guests so you never know if someone will turn around and tell you that whatever you are doing is not allowed.
During our stay the WiFi also failed for about 2 days.
An absolute nightmare start to finish.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Hotel Colombia
address: C. de Sagasta, 20, Centro, 28004 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"abir rhaouti","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ7BFwICiODrXuk9Fzt0dEdNmFgt-4awI8M15g4AE2dOQPdJg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I recently stayed at Futurotel Dreams in Granada and had a mixed experience. While we were initially welcomed by a friendly staff member, several issues marred our stay. The room was not cleaned during our visit, and the "futuristic technology" in the hotel had problems, such as the water temperature reaching up to 40 degrees Celsius and malfunctioning electronics, including curtains that opened on their own.

The most troubling experience involved a female staff member on the morning of our checkout. She was unprofessional, loudly knocking on our door and demanding we leave because we were slightly late for the 11 AM checkout. Even after I informed her that I was coming to open the door, she continued to shout and tried to open it herself, which was distressing, especially for our baby.

Additionally, a prepaid reservation for a return stay was canceled by the hotel, and upon arrival, we were told they were fully booked. We have not received a refund for this booking.

Despite these issues, the hotel has potential, particularly due to its location. There is a nearby restaurant with extremely friendly staff and amazing dishes, which enhanced our overall experience in the area. I hope the hotel makes the necessary improvements to both staff conduct and technological features, as it could be a great place to stay.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marta Peláez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ7qxdcMTdWdcAT6CXYZVUITzdeAJxfHxE4X4AwlXsB9MJzlwE=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Hemos venido a pasar una sola noche y, aunque la ubicación es buena, no lo recomiendo. Incluso tiene tropezones de a saber qué. Por no hablar del cojín que tiene unas manchas blancas un tanto sospechosas. Pero bueno,como hotel está bien y es muy cómodo, xo si vendes  ese tipo de hotel tan digital, debería d funcionar todo. Otra cosa es el parking;  dicen q hay q reservarlo x 18€ y cuando llegas ayi resulta q no hay,q tienes q ir a uno público x 19 q tienen ellos hablado.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Star Man","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVtiMpIQV21oqi6ev32pxgDY-rdPsLcscj7h1T7pk38f7-thoC2=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Il rapporto qualità-prezzo per una notte di passaggio è nel complesso accettabile, ma ci sono alcuni dettagli che, con un piccolo sforzo, migliorerebbero notevolmente l’esperienza.

Apprezzabile l’idea di offrire una scelta tra diversi tipi di cuscino, peccato però che le federe non li coprissero completamente: dormire con il viso a contatto diretto con il cuscino usato da altri non è il massimo in termini di igiene.

La pulizia della stanza lascia spazio a miglioramenti: abbiamo trovato un cerotto usato per terra e un asciugamano sporco dimenticato nell’armadio. Sono dettagli che danno subito una cattiva impressione.

Anche la manutenzione andrebbe curata di più: una tenda rotta che penzola non è certo un bel biglietto da visita.

Nel complesso l’esperienza non è stata negativa, ma basterebbe davvero poco per alzare il livello e rendere il soggiorno molto più piacevole.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBJV7KvFM-TOGbVCiZJKITW&fid=0x0:0x3a38cc3a88bdc953"],"When":"2025-6-21"},{"Name":"María Pérez Meneses","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWUOsoyhyPFdJzBaUHJdrMJ-RMPEpmpy6jIDknzgy1wyZPdAlU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"No recomiendo para nada el hotel.
La limpieza diremos que escasa, por decirlo de alguna manera, las sabanas pequeñas para el tamaño del colchón (hemos tenido que dormir sin sábanas), no ha pasado limpieza en dos días por la habitación (cuando lo pregunto me informan que la limpieza hay que solicitarla expresamente sin que nadie nos avisara de ello en el check-in)...
La parte tecnologica se vuelve completamente loca. El WC tira agua de la cisterna cuando quiere, la TV no se enciende, las luces se encienden y se apagan por sorpresa y, sobre todo, el termostato de la temperatura del agua de la ducha no funciona. Nos hemos tenido que duchar con el agua a 56°C y sin ninguna solución... Que llamarán a un técnico dicen. El problema es que no sabemos cuándo.
Y todo esto si tienes la suerte de encontrar a alguien en recepción (tienen más hoteles y a las empleadas las tienen de uno para otro para recortar personal).
Otro punto en contra, la puerta de fuera se abre con un código que a veces funciona y a veces no, y por ello la puerta casi siempre la encuentras abierta. Mucha seguridad del futuro.
Por último, tened cuidado con no traer mucho equipaje ha que el ascensor está ocupado durante todo el día por los productos de limpieza, parece ser que ese metro cuadrado lo usan también como almacén.
Ahorraos la estancia porque no es para nada agradable.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alberto Cenoz Elorz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI5hcr-ZYMD2y7mpdZjjgn37pxRvWOKkYVh8cLPT4w_1625Kg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"La experiencia mas lamentable, bochornosa, irritante y vergonzosa que hemos sufrido en ningun hotel de los que hemos estado hasta ahora.
Despues de que la recepcionista intentara configurar la tarjeta de entrar de la calle al hotel y despues de otros mas de 8 intentos de configurar la trajeta de entrada a la habitacion preguntamos a la recepcionista a ver si habia una persona en la recepcion durante toda la noche debido a que era imposible acceder al hotel, y despues de tener que preguntarselo mas de 6 veces porque en todo momento esquivaba la pregunta nos dijo que el horario de recepción era hasta las 22:00 y como era evidente que no podriamos entrar al hotel le dijimos que por favor nos devolviera el dinero por algo tan sencillo como no poder entrar a la habitacion a la noche, a lo que ella nos respondio que tenian un convenio con un hotel que hay al lado y que no nos preocuparamos que si llamabamos ahi nos atenderian y podrian ir a abrirnos, debido a lo extraño de la situacion decidi llamar al hotel que me dijo la recepcionista y para mi sorpresa, la recepcionista del otro hotel me dijo que no tenian absolutamente nada que ver y que no entendia nada de lo que nos estaba ocurriendo, despues de una hora de discusiones y con un gran desprecio y mala educacion por parte de la recepcionista de Futurotel le exigimos que por favor nos devolviera el dinero para irnos a otro hotel a lo que se nego en rotundo con gran indiferencia y con una soberbia desmedida e insultante. Por lo cual, mañana mismo acudiremos a la asociación de consumidores y usuarios a denunciar los hechos
Ademas de todo esto, habitaciones sucias con pelos en las camas.
Por lo tanto, si no quereis vivir una situacion lamentable, bochornosa, irritante y vergonzosa no os alojeis en este hotel.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Judith Martín Ortega","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLtiwD3CneLAokPy1sizvYuz6tBNf6Ad-Nyw21nPIiKBIIrfA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Reservamos únicamente una noche de hotel, y mi experiencia ha sido realmente gratificante. Si que es cierto que iba con dudas al principio debido a las malas reseñas de otros usuarios, pero no puedo estar más contenta con la experiencia. La habitación estaba muy limpia, la cama y las almohadas muy cómodas, tienes aire acondicionado, luz normal y luz ambiente (led), las cortinas se regulan de forma domotizadas y lo mejor fue probar ¡el vater japonés! Una pasada. En la ducha hay varios tipos de champús y de geles, y la televisión ofrece varias plataformas de series y películas como Netflix, Prime o Twitch. En cuanto a la recepción, me atendieron dos muchachas súper agradables. En resumen, el hotel funciona perfectamente, todos los aparatos electrónicos van a la perfección, lo que pasa es que hay que pararse a aprender a usarlos correctamente. ¡Repetiremos!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"juanjo fernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUoHwF9Is8_DHPXzCmsjwFyyjFIAvSokgP6tS6T11MC1t2Ke_w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Estuvimos mi pareja y yo hace una semana y la verdad que fue genial, quitando lo de que algunas cosas no funcionaban. user_reviews:

2. The heating was full on max. It was around 35-40 degrees. I asked the employee to help and he suggested a fan mentioning that he could not relocate me to another room since they are fully booked. Right in front of me i opened Booking.com
app and there was in fact a vacant room with a window. Only after me showing that to him he agreed to relocate me.
3. Since such an experience I would include every other thing that i rate low: weak wifi, hair in my bed sheets and i was provided with zero towels. After asking for one, they gave me a wet one.

After that I checked the hotel on the internet again - it seems that this hotel is available under three different names. Positives: It is not too expensive. We arrived early and the staff kindly let us drop our bags and were polite.

Cons: The dorm we were in has a toilet right by the beds (no door) so you can hear and smell people using them. There is no communal area whatsoever which feels mean. I saw a lot of cleaning staff around so it’s not that it’s filthy but I don’t know how to describe the smells that naturally you smell when you’re sleeping right by a toilet.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Steve Lin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI9ssMbwfyDGdEDjfn4aAseJgtkZhT13H-75wAlpijvfmCW1A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Madrid Adventure Hostel offers a mixed experience. My first room felt incredibly tight—hallways, showers, everything was snug. But on the second day, I lucked out with a spacious room featuring lovely street views, so it really depends on which room you get. The beds are comfortable and come with privacy curtains, plus each has its own light and power outlet, which is a nice touch. There’s no kitchen, and the common area is quite small, so socializing or cooking is limited. However, breakfast from the restaurant next door is a highlight—beautifully presented and reasonably priced.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eva S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKYhWYVqY3dEl8qzcSl5yFQ5Hgdp96Doy0nKdGt99LfuOe2KQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The hostel is in a great position and beds are comfortable. Do not expect an hostel to socialize because its has just a small coworking area but not a common one. The staff is nice but they do not share any additional tip on extra activities. There are some noises during movida days, our room was just above trash bins and bars apparently throw there the glass bottles. Bunk beds are very comfortable but my curtain was broken as well as the shower that they proptly repaired when shared.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkck5Z7BHuYAwODZ2IAAq8&fid=0x0:0x1810a503cff115d3"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Sophie Aronson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJUgrKop7Ph7sjrccknO4yBGBoc7BgqjZ5qIG_kblfEV3BlDcI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Good location and staff, but it was pretty gross. There was a blood stain on the pillowcase they gave me and a bunch of crusty stuff smeared on the curtain for my bunk, which was broken and barely hanging on, not offering me much privacy. The bathroom is not closed off from the room so you can hear and smell everything. The showers are also so tiny you can’t even reach down to pick up something if you drop it. That being said, it’s a really convenient location and if you have earplugs you should be fine.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vincent P","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXaejkFDfiQjxXWIQaW2rn5up6hWSO0G1wfVJpzQp6xkts_Rdvn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"This Hostel is very well located near the heart of Madrid center. The rooms are clean and have curtains on every bed.
The bathroom and shower are inside the room, which can lead to some noise if people use it on differente time.

The biggest issue of this hostel, is that there is no common room. There is no place for us to eat, drink, or just seat, except one sofa at the front desk.

This place is a good deal for a night in Madrid, but do not expected to meet people there because there is not appropriate place for this.

Rooms where cleans and staff was available.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kris S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX1QRns3zBli61NKiyGTisRvmpMjr1PN0wwfClsZSe4I6m3DklH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A well-located and budget-friendly hostel, offering great value with breakfast included. The location is incredibly convenient, making it easy to explore the city.

The reception area is clean and minimalistic, and the staff is very friendly. I stayed in the 8 bed shared room for ladies, which had a lovely balcony with a beautiful view. Underbed storage is available, though you will need your own lock (or you can buy one at reception, along with phone chargers). If needed, additional lockers are available at reception for an extra cost.

Overall, a solid choice for budget travelers looking for a good and comfortable stay!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBhKy-R_pSOGtV7qVdFp3yb&fid=0x0:0x1810a503cff115d3"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Ayoub El yousfi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUo3mH1JUbPhOT47po27SiwqkYqDaSccKL7jY6rLIbXoHaR1NRm=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"⚠️ ATTENTION ⚠️
To all tourists coming to Madrid: be very careful.

This hostel charges a €15 deposit and then looks for any excuse not to return it. In my case, because I checked out 20 minutes late.

The morning receptionist is rude, arrogant and unprofessional. He clearly doesn’t know how to speak to customers or handle situations politely.
Cheap prices are just bait. You think you’re saving money, but you end up losing the deposit.
My advice: Madrid has many hostels. It’s better to pay a little more than let them “manage” your €15 their own way.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAN2uBq4xIGNaLFX2tZBnn3&fid=0x0:0x1810a503cff115d3"],"When":"2026-1-15"}]

​To make matters worse, the bedding was completely torn and in rags. The room design is a safety hazard there were no ladders to reach the top bunks, making it nearly impossible and dangerous to get into bed.

user_reviews:

I’ve stayed in many hostels in my life, but this is definitely the worst one. There is no ventilation in the room, the bathroom and shower is shared with man, so as a woman I was really uncomfortable, especially that the showers don’t have any space for the towel and change of clothes. The toilets are really disgusting, they don’t seem to be cleaned daily and there was always pee all over them. And then the smell everywhere is just pure bad. I was suppose to be staying for two nights, but cut the stay shorter. It’s a pure robbery, not sure where the good reviews are coming from. And honestly the cherry on top was a man pleasuring himself very loudly in the shower at 7 am. Not a safe place.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sammy Chan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU6ukpBfhkVbZfYd4gA2C1GrgqhTLxRQPkA2QUPnd5ezS1a0E_3=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":""Unprofessional Staff”
My check-in date was on 30 Sept 2025 for a four-night accommodation. I arrived at the hostel around 10:00 pm that day, only to be told by the front counter staff that the hostel was overbooked and no bed could be offered to me. I was told to come back the next morning to check in for the rest of my booking, with no other solution provided. This was extremely shocking, especially since I had booked months in advance. I waited until around 9:00 am for the morning shift staff, but the situation only got worse. The staff member was impatient, slammed the door, yelled at customers, and initially claimed he could not speak English—though he later did. He grabbed my phone to check my booking, then threw it back on the table and refused to check me in, asking me to leave. He shouted that the hostel had not received payment and blamed the travel agency platform. His behavior was unprofessional and made me feel unsafe. The atmosphere at the front counter was tense, and other guests were also clearly uncomfortable. Ultimately, I gave up on my booking and requested a refund from the agency. I had nowhere to sleep the first night in Madrid and had to find another hostel for the remaining nights. This was a very disappointing and distressing experience.

07 Oct 2025","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rudy Adair","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKz1kciobrTNP48cnCtQgXXm81Bz8ZiBFQLq2C9v6UNV0CJAA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not stay here, especially as a woman. There were no other women in the hostel whatsoever, just older men. The shower/toilet was not clean and overall a very strange and dodgy atmosphere. Don't stay here, find another cheap place, and don't believe the 5 star reviews.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sofia Prade","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKa13hDwyUoi6zwJBlDQOYsJHFqL7pnTV-sIjPRqseImDzstcLb=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"WORST hostel I've ever stayed at.
I don't recommend it to anyone, especially women traveling alone. Most of the guests were older men, and on my second night, I was the only woman in a room full of extremely weird men. Everything was disgusting. The sheets and pillowcases were clearly dirty, the bathroom smelled terrible, and the room was hot and stuffy. The shower stall didn’t even have a lock, so literally anyone could open it while you were showering. The shower door was made of frosted glass, so you could still see others inside, zero privacy. I didn’t feel safe for even a second. On top of all that, I had to wait alone outside for an hour because there was no one at the front desk.
Worst experience ever. STAY AWAY!!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"The Godfather","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWdTZh7uEzSlVQ4zWuX8euUD5FnUZJaFUFfaJOBZjneoVfwPa6w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Listen to the bad reviews. The beds were dirty, the lights in the room keep turning on and off, the curtains were so thin, the bald receptionist was rude. Overall hated this place and would never come back.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Wanderdüne 22","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKj7NkxwaE2ck820n-66H3drZmgmAHNhfMJETMgoHO3oPLupg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Maybe the worst hostel I've ever had: The rooms were terribly dirty, you won't know the number of roommates until you move in (booked via Booking.com) and then it will be 11 other guys and girls beeing unbelievable loud at night and having the main light turned on all night long.

The bed is definitely shorter than 1,90 m so that larger guys/girls will have problems with stretching out or even sleeping in that uncomfortable soft bed...

The hostel itself looks like it is not finally built because there are cables hanging around in the room, in the toilet and so on while everything looks improvised (see images). In the breakfast room you will find just a few chairs in front of some large tables so that 5-8 people can sit there while eating the white toast which is the only "bread" served next to cornflakes. The breakfast time is that chaotic around one small table with the food that it looks like feeding animals... a terrible atmosphere :(

I will never ever visit that hostel again and I don't recommend it for anybody else! Most people I have meet there were thinking the same and were just convinced by some nice looking photos which don't even show the main building  from outside like the gray stone house which is just a house nearby!!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_qtm0UQ&fid=0x0:0xc22734c752ffe9b0"],"When":"2025-1-23"},{"Name":"Laura Golding","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKFSTqW_x6LHDXCVp_EpVrYRtrzCgnTLluw6qNfyeqTCDLaoEE=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"My partner and I stayed in the Madriz Hostel for a couple of nights. When arrived no one was at reception so had to call staff who said someone would be there shortly. Happen to wait a good 15 - 20 minutes for someone to come. By this stage a few others were waiting around to check in also. The room and bathroom were clean. The beds also had curtains which was nice. The bathrooms could have a few ammenities added e.g. hand dryer. It had far too many bunk beds for the room without any ventilation which meant it got warm and noisy at times. There was an annoying automated light too which keeps going off throughout the early night. Worst experience in 15 years of travelling

By far after 15 years of travelling in Europe, this is the worst hostel we have stayed. Pros: clean & centrally located. Cons: curtains missing requested and ignored. mattress is sunking. •	Overcharging Issue: I was charged an extra €10 for overstaying by one hour past the 11 AM checkout. The reason? My phone didn’t update automatically to Lisbon’s time zone, but they still insisted on the charge, even though it was an honest mistake.
•	Bunk Beds: The bunk beds in the 4-person room are very low and uncomfortable.
•	Room Privacy: Rooms are separated by thin dividers or cloth, so you can hear everything from neighboring rooms—even in private 4-pax bookings.
•	Bathrooms: Toilets are poorly maintained—smelly, stained, and without bidets.
•	Wi-Fi: The Wi-Fi doesn’t reach the 4-person room, making it very inconvenient.
•	Lockers: No lockers in the room, so you have to go in and out to access your belongings. While it’s better for security, it’s very inconvenient.

Good
•	The Dutch guy at the reception is super helpful and friendly.
•	Great location, within walking distance of the metro and tourist spots.
•	Hallway cleaners deserve recognition for keeping the common areas clean.
•	They offer useful traveler items, but keep in mind they’re not complimentary—expect to pay extra.

Overall:
For €20, this place doesn’t feel worth it. I’d rate it around €10 for what you get. Wouldn’t recommend unless you’re desperate for the location.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDvvZr8RA&fid=0x0:0x14fcc1f707621d31"],"When":"2024-12-23"},{"Name":"Jackson Rule","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWRkFtS0go5M_QNuXfiJRsbinjGBSgNV6wtFqPhLjJfsqXRSozQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Doesn’t accept card payment which is extremely unusual for Madrid / Spain, so expect an additional fee for getting cash out at one of the local BBVA or Santander bank ATMs. The facilities are extremely basic, with the dorm rooms having no controllable ceiling lights. The room we stayed in also had no window meaning that it was dark 24hours except during routine housekeeping at 10am-12pm when the team switched on the lights.

No lockers in the rooms, they are in the common entrance area. There is also minimal storage space (under beds only) for luggage in the dorms. Shower facilities were cramped but fine with hot water. Wifi was fine as I stayed in a room with access, but other reviews say wifi may not reach the room. The separate toilet facilities are cramped and often bad smelling.

It’s a very basic hostel so uncomfortability is to be expected, but there were several people sleeping on couches in the common room each night and I don’t understand why these people aren’t in beds if they’re paying guests.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sarah","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK-YsRIG-I-oxUQ3L5FYFRn390VvWIe9UktlG6lP5cn8gys5A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Wouldn't recommend staying longer than 1 or 2 nights. [{"Name":"夏眠","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQAXqGFLb2DADeVOTc9r5VxNcZQiHlwdZkQae2vHB679Ph=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Pros:
About a 20–25 minute walk to the city center, which is acceptable.
Each bed has a curtain, providing a basic level of privacy.
A microwave and an electric kettle are available, which is convenient.

Cons:
The hostel has two separate buildings. I was assigned to the secondary building, where there is no reception staff for long periods of time. Any issue requires walking back to the main building, which is very inconvenient.
I stayed in an 8-bed mixed dorm. The room was extremely small and cramped, with very limited space to move.
The lights in both the room and bathroom are motion-sensor based, but they turn off extremely quickly. Eventually, the room light stopped working completely and did not turn on at all.
There is only one storage locker under the bed. It is very small and completely insufficient for normal luggage or personal belongings.
I stayed during late December (New Year period). There was no heating at all in the hostel, including both common areas and the room. It was extremely cold, and the blanket provided was very thin. I was literally shivering while trying to sleep.
Several long-term residents were staying in my room. They occupied a large amount of shared space with their personal belongings, the room smelled bad, and their behavior was very poor. They talked loudly between 1–2 a.m., and on New Year’s Eve, the noise continued until almost 4 a.m. This was extremely disturbing for me as a short-term guest who had to check out at 10 a.m. the next morning. They were also very unwelcoming. I strongly believe short-term guests should not be placed in the same room as long-term residents.
Reception staff were cold and unwelcoming, and the check-in process was slow and inefficient.

Overall:
Poor management and uncomfortable conditions, especially in winter. The lack of heating, bad room allocation, and weak staff service made the stay very unpleasant. I would not stay here again and do not recommend it, particularly for winter or short-term travelers.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kyle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIl3MBmqy1Yfk9uwS5ZnZUucdLJwwpMP1yuIsDVkmz__9hYjRM=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I stayed in an 8-bed dorm, and it turned out to be one of the worst hostel experiences I’ve ever had.

There was no curtain on my bed. The staff gave no explanation at all, and they barely spoke English, and didn’t provide any proper information about the hostel.
The lights are motion-sensor, which means they keep turning on every time someone on the top bunk moves. The window faces a noisy street so you can hear every car and every person passing by. There’s a ceiling fan that keeps turning on and off loudly throughout the night.

The mattress is thin and uncomfortable, and whenever someone in another room closes a door, the wall shakes. The blanket looks old and dirty, and the room becomes cold in the morning because the window must stay open. The top bunk feels unsafe, with no guard rail. if you fall, you’ll get seriously hurt.

Before coming here, I thought the price was cheap, but after staying, I realized it’s not even worth that. I’ve been traveling across Europe for more than a month and stayed in many hostels, but this one is by far the worst.

Some guests seem to live here long-term, not travelers. The old woman on the bed below kept complaining about every little noise, even about me closing the window or the bed creaking slightly. I woke up three or four times during the night and ended up sleeping barely two hours. When people smoke outside, the smell comes directly into the room, and with the window open, it’s both cold and stuffy.

Overall, this place feels like a trap rather than a hostel. It’s loud, dirty, uncomfortable, and badly managed. You literally feel like you’re paying money to be tortured. I strongly recommend avoiding this place. It may seem cheap, it’s absolutely not worth it.
I rarely write negative reviews, but I felt it was necessary to share this experience so others can be aware.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB48QleDep_o58QSUdv8fym&fid=0x0:0x65c563d9bf5518f1"],"When":"2025-10-26"},{"Name":"Nisha Marie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMAATVneXmfjqFhk1J1CORbUlSiVBVJ8NlUCdXEe8F4426hm4R=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I very rarely leave negative reviews but I feel people should be warned. It was absolutely disgusting. Filthy dirty, black bags full of laundry and garbage all over the hallway, people shouting on their phones in the dorms, which smelled like feet. Fecal stains in the toilet, hair all over the bathroom and a dirty mop bucket with water that looked like it had been there since the place opened. And that was supposedly after it had been cleaned. Don’t go there.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sam","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIjFVvfn0i-fM0-OxCXYS12syoRv5aZA2by-am1dFJyDPvddg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Firstly, price was high for the services: 50 Euros for one night. Check-in is not clear, you need to do it in advance online, they send you a key generator link that doesn’t work, so you have to do it in person at the reception.

The atmosphere is sad and not social, rooms are small and bathrooms with basic tools (the hairdryer just works for 1 minute, then it emits a strange burnt smell). Beds are essential with low-quality pillows and blankets, you need to rent towels and lockers.

It seems that non-tourist people actually live there, which is not tourist-friendly as an approach. I would pay particular attention in case of solo female travelers going there, it seems unsafe, especially the street just outside the entrance, which is full of drunk people at night.
Making  the place better would't be a big effort: more safety and Easy check in, Better pillows and blanquet, painting the walls, set up the common areas... could bring also other kinds of travelers..","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"inventorman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfndCAq-uxCGOZMI7LGh3BMpWGEbPhcwmoTu71Rum6FWmIVCdty-rocNNYz1q8SL9qX-Q=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"This place is an absolute nightmare and should be shut down immediately. This hostel is a scam, completely unprofessional, and should be avoided at all costs.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mohammad Rajaei","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIVYio-IQJHwQC2CbXCY2ScUUBm3-47t__-fTlB5m7PEIwc9OC=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Avoid this place!
MYD Madriz Hostel & Hostel MYD La Latina is a complete disaster. When I arrived, there was no one at the property, no response to calls, and I had to find another hotel that night. This hostel gives a horrible reputation to European hostels. Someone was sick in our room, throwing their dirty tissue on top of the common space. Showers were MOLDY, bathrooms NOT CLEAN. Borderline shelter. Be vigilant and careful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBtNf7Vtnj6cy9OeyJdpNOe&fid=0x0:0x65c563d9bf5518f1"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Brooke Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUeqbg5j2DqE8OT54znZycbgBZrH9QvvfL8WXKBG1YaM6-03I9h=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not stay here. It’s not worth it. Horrible horrible horrible.

1. They advertise air conditioning. They don’t have it at all. They’ll claim they do, but have fans in every room with the window open??? And it was extremely hot, you won’t be able to sleep comfortably.

2. It was extremely dirty. The floors, bathrooms (had no hand soap either), shower, walls, bedding, literally everything was nasty and dirty.

3. The staff is rude and unhelpful. The short guy with brown hair and glasses at the front desk is the absolute worst.

I have been backpacking Europe for 7 weeks at this point and have yet to run into a place as heinous as this one.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSvkHGfTV3QO6ijoepsRW2&fid=0x0:0x65c563d9bf5518f1"],"When":"2025-6-21"}]

title: Modular Rooms Hotels
address: Calle de la Espada, 4, Centro, 28012 Madrid
descriptions: Simple dorms & compact rooms in an unpretentious hostel offering breakfast & a shared kitchenette.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"andyibz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QdrOaBwvzVCVZGa_JCxmRob18-RTGR2NAc2ehM2ioIFQuUJBtaO6jr7GHqX15LV00VYsw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"This does exactly what it says on the tin , I had a great nights sleep here . The room was super clean , the bed comfortable . The communal spaces are adequate for a hostal. ||People respected the rules and kept quiet whilst around the pods ! Obviously as with any hostal a lot depends on the other people sharing the spaces as to the quality of sleep you get , but despite it being a Saturday night it was all very quiet and everyone was very respectful when they came home ! ||The location is great in the centre of Madrid and for the price it was all good .","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"colleengQ7719PL","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QejddW0yoGeT8LnCjh-OGQgsf6YKqhof3HZA5-um4yCkQej85oCdMUmKPJmnowcu5Ag5w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"1-Star Review: Modular Nightmare, Not a Dream|I love the concept of unique, pod-style stays and had extremely high hopes for this place (my 6th hostel on a month-long trip). Unfortunately, this stay was a disaster marked by poor communication, non-existent service, and serious maintenance issues.||The Nightmare Check-In Process|The problems started long before I arrived and created a chaotic check-in:||Payment & Communication: My credit card company flagged the payment transmission as suspicious. Emailed questions took multiple attempts to get a single response—a major red flag.||Non-Functional Pre-Check-In: They require pre-check-in as there is no front desk staff (another red flag), but the website kept glitching, demanding I add a second person despite being a solo traveler.||Arrival Chaos: I was denied entry after ringing the doorbell because they couldn't find my reservation. After 5 minutes outside, I was let in, only to realize the "check-in" is done via a camera and a maintenance worker—not face-to-face.||I was hot, tired, and this complicated process was ridiculous and frustrating.||The "Mini-Double" Room Issues|I booked the mini-double, which was 90% as pictured (minus some decor). The bed was comfortable, but the flaws made it almost unbearable:||Zero Ventilation: There is no direct ventilation with the AC-cooled hallway. My vents lead into the next unit, meaning I was entirely dependent on their air circulation. The room felt immediately stiff and hot.||No Blanket: Just a folded sheet, though the heat from the lack of ventilation made a blanket unnecessary.||Mismatched Switches: The back wall is covered in unlabeled switches. They aren't connected, so turning a light off at the bedside means you can't turn it on at the door until you flip the bedside switch again.||Poor Security: In Area 4, the lock on the modular unit door was completely non-functional. You could just pull the handle down, and the door opened.||Hostel Identity & Maintenance Failure|This property is a place with a lost identity—it is neither a functional hotel nor a cozy hostel.||Communal Areas: The kitchen is basic, only offering coffee/tea, and is not a cozy communal space. The downstairs lounge is small and suffers from the same lack of soundproofing as the rooms.||The Bathroom Smell: The bathroom was genuinely awful. The smell changed but consistently resembled a NYC sewer. One of the three sinks was broken, and the shower doors were flimsy.||Overall Maintenance: The place lacked basic upkeep: peeled/ripped wallpaper, bleeding paint lines, holes in the walls, and the internet was poor.||No Activities: Unlike a true hostel, they offer no planned activities.||Verdict: The location was great, but this place is severely overpriced for the chaotic, poorly maintained, and non-serviced experience you actually receive. I would not stay here again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Redactora Tachi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIYf_L4hTlskY6lkFaF6QoeI25mqY8E6EH7JEV8ZWEdSwuB7Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This accommodation is unprofessional and disrespectful. After booking a couple of nights, I tried to contact them several times by phone, email and most recently, WhatsApp (following an email sent advertising to contact by WhatsApp). It took them a week to answer a simple question about the size of the luggage storage space. They provided inaccurate information and didn't even apologise. A poor service usually comes with no guarantee of quality.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDqWkueyYltIAhZ95Z1WF2S&fid=0x0:0x33901a6f341b8ae3"],"When":"2026-1-17"},{"Name":"spacebug30","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVADbOvWcRpqRw8B_o_1KmHU-nfvzVkQ2XsXe9b7NDdyybdBkKC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great capsule hotel in the heart of Madrid. The reception is open 24h and everyone I encountered was super kind and helpful. The capsule was spacious, with different lights (reading, ambient, etc), A/C and several power outlets. The shared bathrooms and kitchen were clean. Spacious locker, easily fits a large suitcase. My suitcase in the picture is hand luggage size. The place felt very safe because everything was locked and had to be opened with your unique pin code: front door, room door, capsule door, locker. Of course it's still a hotel so you'll hear some noise as others come and go in your room, but that's expected. Close to the metro station. Neighborhood felt safe too, also at night as a female solo traveller. Perfect place to visit Madrid solo, especially on a budget.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSe8UATri8ud7DwsnV9562&fid=0x0:0x33901a6f341b8ae3"],"When":"2025-8-25"},{"Name":"Senan Abusnaina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZQnHVZExyWZtQCS_dRd3GWJQgUT6ZrXQVAjC9Zj4Fr-5dMfs=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"here only the location is good, near to metro Station and nearby the SOL
but the room is very tiny and small
there is no rest area,
the bathroom in different floor,
yhe kitchen was very dirty,
there is no facilites nor coffee machine
for me i didnt like it at all","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"stephaniepK5459HN","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeSe9ibNVY65MpgeQh70I370foM0-7tEPyVc9CIfSq5Ekli093sZJVPBoLD5d5qPK_2rw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"We had a great stay at Modular Rooms in Madrid. These rooms are beds in cubicles / pods, with a secure door and a locker for your luggage. Our room even had a large TV. The showers, albeit small, are new and beat most Airbnb’s. As a couple, we stayed one night, but could have done a few if we weren’t moving on. Madrid is great and this spot is safe, well-run, clean in the right location. Tuvieron la amabilidad de darnos la habitación antes de la hora de check in, que siempre se agradece para dejar las maletas e instalarse antes de salir a pasear. La habitación estaba muy limpia, y también nos guardaron las maletas después del check out.

Pero me parece un precio desorbitado para lo que ofrece el alojamiento (aunque fue de lo mas económico que vimos), excepto por la ubicación. Está en la planta 3 y 4 de un edificio residencial, una casona antigua, que al parecer comparte con "Hostal Abril" (no se muy bien como es el asunto). El caso es que en Booking se contempla como un alojamiento de 3 "estrellas" (cuadraditos en este caso al no ser un hotel) y al llegar tienen el cartel de Pensión de 1 estrella en la puerta.

En nuestra habitación había una cama de matrimonio y una cama supletoria que menos mal que no necesitamos, porque el colchón chirriaba como ningún otro que hayamos visto. Tuvimos la mala suerte de que el huésped de la habitación de al lado se marchó de madrugada y se duchó a las 3 y media, con el consiguiente ruido de tuberías y agua cayendo, ya que no hay prácticamente nada de insonorización y se escuchaba como si estuviera en nuestro baño. Además, a eso de las 4 y media de la mañana unos impresentables empezaron a dar voces, no sabemos si venía de otra habitación, otra planta del edificio o del exterior, pero estuvieron casi hasta que amaneció, así que no pegamos ojo en toda la noche.

Por otro lado, el baño de la habitación tiene el típico alicatado de cristal transparente para dejar pasar algo de luz, sin embargo en lugar de hacerlo de mitad hacia arriba coge todo el alto de la pared, con lo que quien esté acostado en la cama ve perfectamente a quien está sentado en el inodoro. Resulta algo incómodo, la verdad. En resumidas cuentas, para más de 150€ la noche, no lo termino de ver.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEKHem8aQ8f3N6AE&fid=0x0:0x9126ce9bddd74ecd"],"When":"2025-5-13"},{"Name":"Nosferatu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWGFZObqx-2dH3oxWfeqFBuHJnwXJALyWKS04jTFRo4zn-wX80=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Fuimos un finde semana,puntos positivos?
