title: Multi Hostel Lavapies
address: C. de Lavapiés, 17, Centro, 28012 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Efe Ceylan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2LI9TPCMbeih2Xn5e20opFj7K_Y7fx4Aol9XTd6e4vt6Cn6I=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I can sum it up in one word: terrible. Do not stay here. I don’t even know where to begin. First of all, communicating with the receptionist was extremely difficult; he didn’t speak English, was yelling at me, and didn’t even know Spanish. There was a receptionist who made uncomfortable eye contact with female guests, which felt very unsettling. The reception is not available 24/7, and there is no security. Your safety may be at risk—keep that in mind.

As soon as I entered, I was hit by a strong, unpleasant smell. The rooms were damp, and the beds were crammed together. The bunk beds had awful curtains, and the bedding was not clean. My suitcase didn’t even fit into the lockers provided. I also saw some rooms without doors—bunk beds were placed directly in the hallway.

One of the biggest issues was the bathrooms. There was nowhere to put personal items—no table, no shelf. I even struggled to remove my contact lenses. Our room had no natural light, and noise from outside was constant, making it impossible to sleep.

There were no proper common areas, and overall, the facilities were terrible. When I asked about the kitchen, I was simply pointed to a microwave. Do not trust the photos on Booking.com—the reality is completely different.

The building is single-story, and sound echoes throughout. Hygiene is nonexistent. While the location is central, the surrounding area felt unsafe, especially at night. At one point, we were even approached aggressively while walking back to the hostel.

Please do not stay here—I wouldn’t recommend it to anyone.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Zara J","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJK2DiQ5g7fVR8SFV0a7kQkQBn4xTzNT2AKuZAIOtBJmML1DA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The hostel was good, room had aircon and fans to battle the hot weather. My one thing I didn’t like was that the tiled ramp that leads to the door had been hosed off so it was wet, I didn’t see that the floor was wet and there was no sign posted. I ended up falling over hurting my already damaged knee and aggravating my ankle that I broke a few months prior to staying here, this also caused me to slam the back of my head into the ground shattering my claw clip that was in my hair. Attached is the photo of my claw clip after my head slammed on the ground. Now I will recognise that yes I didn’t see the floor was wet but this being said, there should have been a sign or something.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMR6Cg8kQS1ac34bwkCvZK&fid=0x0:0x25f43dc4359a3658"],"When":"2025-6-20"},{"Name":"Aña Montaña","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUy-mt-URcQ-2yerzcTipd3E7joCUMZTVrvfXcXLYnl067qgXLN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I do not recommend this hostel – terrible living conditions

I stayed at this hostel in Madrid for 4 days, and it was a complete nightmare. To begin with, the amenities list includes air conditioning, but it only operates for a few hours from 9:30 PM. During the day, the room is unbearably hot – it’s suffocating, especially with the temperature outside at +40°C.

Additionally, for three out of the four days, there was no cold water in the tap – only boiling water, so the only way to wash your hands was in the shower. Living in such conditions is simply impossible. The hairdryer was present but didn’t work.

The window in our room was broken and wouldn’t close, so we had to listen to the noise of Madrid’s nightlife all night long. But that’s not all – there’s a horrible sewage smell at the entrance to the hostel that’s impossible to ignore.

The bathroom had no shower enclosure, so all the water after showering spread across the entire bathroom floor, creating additional discomfort. The floor in the room was disgustingly sticky, and the bedside lamp was broken and just lying on the bed. I had to ask for tape to stick it to the wall.

The only plus of this hostel is its convenient location, but that doesn’t justify the terrible living conditions. I strongly advise against staying here.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7zMfiiwE&fid=0x0:0x25f43dc4359a3658"],"When":"2024-8-13"},{"Name":"tlodv","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUNSPRNQJpFADwE1euomHMWjZlr-lZmvnShFm6VQ-HIGy_s8eyP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"How should I describe it? Entering our rooms, we luckily had two windows so we could get fresh air, even tho the windows were directly next to the playground which can be noisy. The beds aren’t comfortable, the mattress is thin, also the bedspread was weird. The lamps of the beds were sometimes alright, the others were broken down.

Bathroom wasn’t clean at all. So many papers laying on the floor, shower cover broken down so that the bathroom will be flooded. Also why was there a big bin upside down in the shower room?

No room for hanging out as a group in the hostel.

I would avoid the hostels in the future!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tanja Swinkels","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWvSDathdBqGKvbAQYT7xso3HNc6i41zpA6IMEBXOaZoEDDXow4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I didn’t expect much from the hostel but honestly it was okay! La habitación no es muy grande, pero suficiente para dos personas. Cuenta con toalla, manta y calefactor. La cama chirría un pelín pero no demasiado. La habitación en la que nos hospedamos tenía el baño compartido, pero no ha supuesto ningún problema.

El personal, tanto la dueña como la chica de la limpieza y las llaves, muy amable, educado y dulce.

Sin embargo, ha habido un par de cosas que no nos han gustado demasiado. El personal ha sido amable y atento. Además, la habitación está limpia, es cómoda y tiene lo necesario para resguardarse ahí: televisión, armario, calefactor, toallas, cama y mesas de noche. El baño es compartido, pero eso no ha supuesto ningún problema porque está muy limpio. El lugar lo hemos considerado amplio a pesar de que nuestra habitación haya sido un poco pequeña (pero acogedora).

Algunos puntos a recalcar son la ubicación, que al anochecer está solitaria y nos dió mala sensación, pero la boca de metro más cercana está a tres minutos.

Además de esto, no hay microondas, cocina o nevera, se escucha el ruido de la calle, las camas y ventanas (cuando las abres) chirrían un poco y cuando amanece entra un poco de luz por ellas.

De todas formas, nosotras hemos salido con muy buena sensación. Lo recomendamos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICLndPvpAE&fid=0x0:0x3faf99f1fefff0c1"],"When":"2024-6-22"},{"Name":"Naila SM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLA5DJngUyq-pZujBSA_wE6rC9CwJ5ARPACo0CotEwa37VRzDDh=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Para pasar una noche express, decente… A pesar de que hay carteles de no fumar, desde que entras por la puerta del hospedaje, huele todo a tabaco, la habitación, incluso las sabanas… (pedimos que las cambiaran y a la mañana siguiente nos las cambiaron). title: Santo - Coworking - Coliving Madrid
address: Cuesta de Sto. Domingo, 24, Centro, 28013 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Ivan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLwtB5Y-k1SM3Zq9T-b9c2UVa8G_MxSUx3pig-yRI1wzJO4YQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This place was so bad I feel like I need to warn people.

First of all — BEDBUGS.
I can handle noise, mess, questionable smells, whatever… but BEDBUGS are where I draw the line. I woke up covered in bites.

There’s no reception, which means literally anyone who knows the door code can walk in. And people DO — random strangers come in and sleep in the shared area like it’s a train station. Felt extremely unsafe.

Cleanliness? Nonexistent. Everything is dirty, sticky, and smells like it hasn’t been cleaned properly in ages.

You also have to pay extra for a locker, and you just get the cheapest flimsy padlock attached to a shelf that can be opened without effort. So basically: there is nowhere safe to put your belongings.

I’ve stayed in many budget hostels, but this one doesn’t even meet the minimum standard of hygiene or safety","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Daniel Reis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIR7selrwzp49_XlyBXZV4-3FFmzdD-4tGBOcznyFsUuxXQqQ_0Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I was going to live a good review. Although I don’t think it’s practical, there is no space to store the clothes when you shower or hangers to dry towels.
But the worst thing of all, we are terrified by it. They don’t change the bedsheets!!! As soon as we were leaving they make the bed and spray it with a perfume cleaner. That is just disgusting.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Beth Miles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIOlXG33dx5sEg0u-vHr8DvABvIqJSI9gIySnMJgvd5kvSeHQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"BEDBUGS!!!! CHINCHES!!!!! DO NOT STAY HERE This hostel unfortunately has a very misleading 7 stars on booking, which means a lot of people are booking a place full of bedbugs and having a horrible time! Contrary to the last review posted, they have NOT improved! After my first night, I woke up with bites everywhere. I assumed it was mosquitos even though no-one in my room had been bitten and no-one had heard a single mosquito in the hostel. The next day, I woke up with 27 bites! I didn't sleep for 3 days. When I requested a room change, they did it quickly, but they didn't care at all that I had to wash all my clothes, that I hadn't slept for 3 nights, or that I'd wasted an entire day of my holiday sorting this out, and I was exhausted on the rest of the days because of lack of sleep due to scratching the bed bug itches all night. The lack of a physical reception means they are slow to respond as soon as problems come up and they don't care about their guests. The day after I moved rooms, I saw a guy from another room also covered in bites everywhere. His bed also had bedbugs. 2 bedbug cases in 2 different rooms over the space of 2 days. They clearly have not improved. They have NOT sorted the bedbugs out. The only reason people stay here is because they go off the reviews on booking.com and don't check google. They try to blame the guests when it clearly them. Just look how many reviews about bedbugs there are! I have stayed in over 50 hostels and have never had a single case of bedbugs. This was the worst hostel of my life. They should be shut down for health reasons.

title: MadVibes Hostel
address: C. de Mesonero Romanos, Centro, 28013 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Mohamed Hesham (ETSH)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWIUkD4b7RJcMidBKF8LToHOMVusGJ9fizD2Aj_XneYsHR6tas=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My stay at this hostel was extremely disappointing and far below acceptable standards. From the moment of arrival, the check-in process was not ready on time and lacked basic organization and professionalism.

The overall cleanliness and maintenance were poor. There was an unpleasant smell throughout the property. The beds were uncomfortable, not properly clean, and had no curtains for privacy. The lockers did not lock properly, which raised serious concerns about security.

The bathroom facilities were inadequate — there were no functional locks on the doors, no hooks to hang personal belongings, and no trash bin in the room. These are basic necessities that should be standard in any accommodation.

Additionally, there was no kitchen and not even a microwave available, which is generally considered a basic hostel facility.

During my stay, guests were required to check out and check in again without clear justification, and I was asked to wait outside before being allowed to re-enter. This caused unnecessary inconvenience.

The written policy stated that card payments were not accepted, yet a mandatory €15 deposit was required by card. This inconsistency raises concerns regarding transparency.

It was also very difficult to leave a review on Hostelworld.com , which limits transparency and prevents potential guests from easily seeing honest feedback from others.

Overall, the management, organization, hygiene, and guest handling were below acceptable hospitality standards. Based on my experience, I would not recommend this property","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQtMD_QqZVnIeuvVm1j0Tc&fid=0x0:0x6e7eba099e8c441a"],"When":"2026-2-14"},{"Name":"Brayan Palace","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ0ZNNUE6S5ZKeCt2988HrV2nzP-uTwSYir0yQzxO_zcf9yOw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"In the evening shift, there was a staff member from Venezuela (I don’t remember his name). title: Multi Hostel Calvario
address: C. del Calvario, 19, Centro, 28012 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Egbert Scherman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_5wRHDGj_eE5W1UefgbZUu2ONRulAvlxUkcym6tKwqy4vu6IG=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I had the unfortunate situation where I booked my accommodation 1st before booking my train ticket. When I booked the only train ticket I get find, it meant I would arrive in Madrid at 12 midnight.

Trying to contact the Hostel to make arrangements accordingly proved to be challenging (Since checkin is from 3pm till 11:30pm). After a Google search for a different number, I spoke with a manager/owner who suggested they will wait for me as long as I keep them informed on my arrival in Madrid and as I'm making way to the Hostel. Train arrival was scheduled at 00:00 and it would take 30min to get to the Hostel.

I thought all is in order. An arrangement was made. However- while I was on the train to Madrid I received a call from the Hostel requesting my arrival time. When I responded that I would be there at 00:30 the person on the other end got upset and kept on saying that I can't checkin after 00:00
I received another call, which sounded like a different person, who started fighting with me in Spanish. I kept on saying that I don't understand Spanish.
He then whatsapped me, fighting, and said I have to make sure I'm there before 11:30. I replied that I'm on a train, and obviously can't make the train go faster, and that I have made arrangements at the time of booking with them. He kept on fighting, stating that it's unacceptable.
ALL HIS MESSAGES WAS IN CAPITALS.

I tried to stay calm, and not respond to his messages as I feel the arrangement was made. I informed them on my arrival in Chamartin, and when I got onto the metro.
This unset him more as he started going on (in capitals) that I should have taken a taxi cause the metro takes an hour.
Truth is metro takes 40 min, and a taxi would have taken me 35 min. Metro cost me a few euros, where a taxi would have cost alot. I would expect the person at the hostel should know this, but obviously being very abusive.

When I arrived (at 00:35), the receptionist was friendly, and said his boss is the one who messaged me. And I had to pay €10 for checkin after 11:30 (uhm).

When I made the booking I requested the bottom bed as I'm a left lower leg amputee, and struggle to get up to the top bed. When I remove the metal leg, it's even worse. They replied that it's in order.

When I arrived, guess which bed did I receive? When I queried it, the receptionist seemingly can't do anything about it.

Rigat (I hope the spelling is correct) the receptionist, did come to save the day, and moved me the following day to the bottom bed.

I realized that their wifi isn't working, and they ignored my message of enquiry - twice. Once in the morning and later in the afternoon. When Rigat came to sort my bed, I informed him on the wifi, which he then got sorted as well.

It is obvious that this place will disregard any pre-arrangements or complaint you might have. Treat you like you're an idiot for not falling into their momentarily preferences. 1 Bonus point for Rigat though to safe the day.

Everything else of this place is very much in comparison to other places in the same price range. Out of season, this place's price doesn't drop much, where you will find very nice Hostels who's prices dropped to the same.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Arafath Shikh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLCc20znq-Oq2pTG-klx5_wup_RI-wMLbfO7opkkydheFvAJd0=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nice hotel","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rubén Fernández Aguilar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUUvIbRcyIy8ciUFwAorF3GJ_xmwz_OubICkXlB7ngSC0R4wV2z=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"La comunicación es deficiente al no hablar buen castellano el personal.

Las camas y almohadas estaban llenas de pelos, arena y restos de migas, aparte de que llegas a hacer el check-in y tú mismo te tienes que hacer la cama!?!? De locos vaya.

Tanto la habitación como los baños estaban sucios, pelos por todas partes, y sin acceso a gel de baño o champú, deprimente.

Al segundo día de estancia alguien entró en nuestra habitación (nos quedamos un amigo y yo en una habitación privada con dos camas, nadie tendría que tener acceso a la misma ya que solo teníamos los códigos él y yo) nos movió los enseres de lugar y aparte nos deshizo las camas, al llamar a recepción no te dan solución ni respuesta a quien ha sido, les da totalmente igual, como si no hubiera pasado nada o fuera algo común. Otra vez a hacerte la cama tú mismo, genial.

Aparte el día del check-out te llaman a las puerta 20 minutos antes de malas maneras para que te largues, si el check-out es a las 11, nadie tiene porqué molestar ni dar golpes en la puerta antes de esa hora, de vergüenza.

Sensación de seguridad y de privacidad cero, la limpieza brilla por su ausencia y el trato no es cercano ni resolutivo.

La ubicación que es relativamente céntrica es lo único a destacar.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"V G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-eQPyfr86YAmhi5cFsJP_HD7O-Karfe9mSB4clG7g5aYf5A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"No pienso volver!!
Cama incómoda, habitación a pie de calle, compañeros de habitación que viven allí de larga estancia (parece un piso patera, los compañeros se han hecho los amos de la habitación) armario y lavabo muy sucio, solo dos lavabos, la cama te la tienes que hacer tú, un desastre.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Laura del Val","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUzyi-mBVMdHpAAsd2Ce2c124asc4o9xU0ZtJYHsuUBOv9ZeKfk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Estoy encantada con la estancia en este hostal, ha sido un gran descubrimiento! Muy contenta con todo y 100% recomendable!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jaime Rodríguez Aguilar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVKN-Eq3Pma_XInohddm6faQvZrsVOMPZHA4Lbi3O3-3Yf--_kxeA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Lamentable atención. Pros: room cleaning services, 24-hr front desk, nice staff, room is clean, bathroom is clean, free towels. The bed is very comfortable. There is a hand wash basin in the twin bed room. A dining room in the basement with a microwave and a water cattle. There are quite a lot of choice of food. Right next to a metro station. Closed to supermarket. The heating is very good, not too hot nor too dry, I'd say it's better than most of the hotels. Guess that 1-stareans something.

Cons: very narrow hallway, they maximize the room size. Poor sound isolation, you can hear the sound of television from the next room. There is no hot water in the basin, so no use in winter. The neighborhood is not the most beautiful one, but I should say it's safe.  There is no water in the dining room which is not convenient, you can't wash your hands. The smart TV turns on automatically and never figured out how it works. But it works perfectly in the next room, so my problem.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J J","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIl9jx3A6Vr1nya15gaBT-CKLD9xjNjYGpi2OCyJKFOynYkQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Hostel is very modern and staff is friendly. My double room was just 2 twin beds pushed together. The beds were kind of hard but for two nights, it was fine. The "gym" had old basic equipment.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDF4OPhLw&fid=0x0:0x949a64cd3973db11"],"When":"2023-11-3"},{"Name":"Elena Olsh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVG_pEkNIjFGoF3gJpQPfd_ietU3Qyybv2C0Adg-WZ1wz2_C5Hd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very nice place and staff. Had a great stay thanks to Maika and her team which spend an incredible amount of energy in taking care of the guests and looking after the facility. Hostel experiences can depend on other guests you share the room with but what concerns the management of the structure nothing to complain of. The service, cleanliness and availability of the staff result in a fantastic offer. Comfortable beds and plenty of common areas.
Surely recommend","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chloe Man","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUBqZpBe_LTn1AviBCvsNJVmDx54PbNm2e1VzYJ9eMOmjvW-RhU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We booked a private room, which allowed us to have more privacy. I love the decor and the bedding. Bed is comfortable as well.

The ladies at the front desk are wonderful and welcoming. Great amenities. The location is convenient and we didn’t need to call a car at all and were able to walk to every tourist attractions in Madrid.

The only complaint is the noise. We had the room by the street, so we can hear everything that’s happening on the street. Granted the street is relatively quiet, it could still get loud from time to time. The wall is also not soundproof, we could hear everything outside in the hallway (people dragging their luggage / opening lock pad ).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA29FLSKOpuP92LlTyvSkg1&fid=0x0:0x82550338db99b356"],"When":"2025-12-5"},{"Name":"Kat A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV-14qq48hckC2M4KTyUmfML2teREuH6DWrY4txKozK4vY6pPAR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I booked a room for my last night in Madrid while returning from a round trip in Spain. My review is based on this.

Pros:
The hostel is in comfortable walking distance from Atocha, even with luggage, one can walk there in 15 mins max. Also it is close to the centre and sights in the city.
Supermarkets are closeby if you want to buy food, also pubs, small tavernas.
They have a 24-hour reception so no matter what time you arrive, really early, really late, you will be able to check in.
The facilities are nicely designed with good taste, everything was clean. The beds have privacy curtains - this is super!
The shower rooms have enough space and nice and hot water, which is also excellent, there are hair dryers to use.
There are shared spaces for co-working (with conference cabins), dining room, hangout room, fitness room. There is a kitchen area and fridges to use as well. Plenty of rooms everywhere.
The beds are comfortable and has a little light, electric plug and shelve to put your things. Under the bed (extra spacious) storage, hook on the wall for your towel and coat.
Extra luggage lockers are also available on the corridors.
I booked my space in an 8-bed mixed dormitory which was the best value option and added breakfast as well.

Neutral:
This is a cashless hostel, only card payments are accepted.
The bathroom downstairs is cleaned between 8-9am, and it's closed for that time, one has to go to the upper bathroom in that time.
To get towels are €4 - non-refundable.
The doors are operated via GoKey app.

Cons:
The area surrounding the hostel has some rougher parts.
In the morning, I passed a few groups who were drunk or high, loud and generally just loitering.
Inside the hostel, some guests were also interesting.
Without wanting to make the hostel look bad, I just want to draw your attention to this: if you would like a quiet night, book the private room or the "ladies only" room if you are a girl.
In my mixed dorm, the stench of feet was so intense that it woke me up by 3am. Then someone sprayed men's deodorant in the air, which made everything worse. The other guests threw all of their clothes and shoes on the floor, in an untidy manner.
Two people were snoring like hell, someone had their reading light on for the whole night.
Others pointed out also, there are motion sensor activated floor lights in the rooms. These did not bother me, but they do stay on for long. I don't like darkness, so I was fine with it.
In my room, just two things were broken: The underbed storage that belonged to my bed (could not close fully) and the hook that was meant to be mine. No issues, I took another hook and still could place my luggage.
The breakfast did not worth for me personally. It has one coffe, one pastry, a glass of orange juice and one piece of fruit.

While eating breakfast, I witnessed one of the guests harrassing the receptionist girl. It was a disturbing scene, I felt sorry for the girl.

Summary:
Based on my experiences, there are two types of hostels. One type is small, old, maybe a bit outdated too, giving out an alternative/hippie vibe. These attract an amazing community, and have a welcoming, friendly atmosphere despite their less shiny appearance. Don't expect this here.

The other type, like The Loft House are "new concept" hostels, maybe we could describe them as cheap hotel alternatives, with beautiful design, large capacity but they feel alienating.
If you are on a budget, it's still a good choice in a city where accommodation tends to be expensive.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Roos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVre1aDHQ4kUD8PlBfIPzKfJJQp0DRuLv8a32vPabokVa_iwmY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"For a schooltrip we booked a whole room at the hostel: I really enjoyed our stay.
The (bath)rooms were super clean, i felt really safe & the receptionist were so kind.
The metrostation and supermarket are nearby & the city centre is walking distance.
Lavapiés is a cool place to stay as well: if you look for it, you can find lovely (tapas) restaurants & bars (definitely not too expensive)","Images":null,"When":""}]

I booked through Agoda and was sent a link by the hotel which my web browser flagged as unsafe/insecure.  I got in touch with the hotel and got a new link via whatsapp, but this took some time and wasn't the most reassuring procedure.||Because everything happens via whatsapp and electronic codes, you've always got to have your phone charged, and you've got to be patient enough for the person on the other end of the whatsapp to reply.  For me it took about 2 to 3 minutes once I'd sent the first message, but it could be longer.  It was all just a bit funny and awkward.||The room and everything else was fine. Comfortable bed, good location, etc etc.||Just you know, how much do you want to have to rely on your phone always having battery, always having data and a good connection?","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"AneZRZR","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfjHmZg8gr7jpDgKhrh5KsR_Mq7L6pkVedvpKQz-f3LMiOd3OjI90CK4uLtF3wE59zZgA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Please management-owner consider having one member of staff at least in reception: for safety reasons and fairness on the neighbours.||||There is not reception, not personal of staff that you could see, contact in case of an emergency or in case of needing something. The caretaker (conserje) of the building, is the one helping guests, informing them about amenities or where to eat or to go. The entrance to the building as well as the main door where the rooms are located is with codes. Anyone could follow me and go into the building. It is not saved. Not only regarding the guests, but essentially for the neighbours. There is not an emergency exit; the smoke alarm seems not to have light, not sure if it works. I was given with a mini-room, too tiny although I asked for a bedroom with bath. The corridors are too tiny. Towels too small, also. It is stated that you could leave a suitcase in a store room, but there is not control of who leaves what.  There is not provided with a hairdryer, also it is stated. And the toiletries are not good (gel). No kettle facilities. TV small. Old deco and furniture. Green and dark colours, depressing. Balcony useless, not space to sit.||||||The checkin is done by a camera (CCTV). You do not see anyone I believe this could be against the DATA protection Act. There are no signs of informing that there are cameras (at least I saw one), potentially recording. I went to eat breakfast to the coffee shops downstairs, and I came back to my room to rest, when at 10:30 am one of the cleaners opened my door. She told me that she was informed that I left. Obviously it was not her fault, I said do not worry. Astonishing, so they saw me through the camera leaving and they assumed that I left the room. As stated, firstly I do not think that it is a secure place; things can happened, emergencies, etc. one old couple got confused with the codes and they could not access the main door to the bedrooms, I just arrived (around 9 pm) and help them. But, usually there is nobody there. Again, not sure that this place meets the health and safety regulation. But, essentially the Data protection Act. Finally, it is not fair on the neighbours. I am sorry, but I cannot recommend this place.||||||||The location, just in Plaza Santa Bárbara where the Metro Alonso Martínez is located (from the Airport T4 to Nuevos Ministerios and then L10 two stops, approximately 30 minutes overall. Make sure you follow the signs in the Metro to Plaza Santa Bárbara, otherwise you may get confused). The wifi is strong. There are shops and restaurants nearby. Walking distance to Gran Via (15 minutes) and other interesting locations. Bathroom size fair.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"helen x","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWvCtCWJQEundq2yFnS1KINvvIbN4tDjeyefoVLEDinsOGMb_3odQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Location is convenient. Would give three stars because communication could be better. Since it is all virtual, it takes longer for someone to respond on the other end. And you’re left waiting. But for the price, it’s ok. Room is nice.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Onefam Madrid Hostel
address: C. del Montserrat, 9, Centro, 28015 Madrid
descriptions: Basic dorms & private rooms in a casual hostel offering free dinner, a shared kitchen & a courtyard.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"evita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4ef_IEdSE70avy13k9lJdJUvrXKpzh6uwmDzNP3_HHjIUAu7S=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"My overall impression of this hostel is very good- super duper clean (even in the corners and under the bed!), spacious common areas and sunlight. Generous free breakfast and sometimes dinner events.

I love the fact that they hold events almost daily to welcome and connect the residents. The vibe is superb.

Kitchen and dinning areas are well designed and clean. Love that they organized it daily to keep it comfortable and welcoming.

The highlight is the walking tour which is based on donations. Very budget friendly. I appreciate my tour guide Melissa who shares her passion and knowledge during the tour. She was patient to help out one of the tour members to find his lost property.

Only downside is the limited common toilet and shower, only one shower and toilet together in shared dorm hence in some situations you need to wait for a while for toilet/shower usage. Some noises from outside that can be heard inside the room at night time even after around 12:00 am, but a good pair of ear plugs helps.

I would very much love to stay in the hostel again and I love it so much that I even joined their membership.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDDu4iOL1chytPGmfGGfiuN&fid=0x0:0xc827ac0a57f89537"],"When":"2026-2-24"},{"Name":"AH.A","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKzlv8K1SLD5XL-9DoxtWTeqXITWSGvtBLCxP43eCCaWujoBg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was my second time staying in a onefam hostel and I cant recommend it enough. The location is great, its clean and theres always something to do. The staff are so friendly and are willing to help with anything. I'd like to give a special mention to our tour guide Melissa. She was an amazing tour guide and our experience was great and memorable thanks to her. She was full of knowledge and answered all of our questions whilst also staying funny and interesting to keep up engaged. Thank you and I hope to stay at a onefam hostel again soon!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marta Szcześniak","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL3-tIyWoDgUCNOuOnOvXnjVl294pAIL_wgSw6K13IP2Nahqg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I stayed at Onefam Hostel in Madrid two times and I would definitaly book it once again in the future. I was amazed by how great and helpfull is the Staff. I was travelling alone as a woman and I felt really safe there. The rooms and bathrooms are clean. There are events organized each evening to eat together or leave for some party. It is perfect option to meet new people and make friends especially when travelling alone. The location is close to many turistic places where you can easily go by walk. It is also very close to Metro station so it is very easy to get there from the airport. I strongly recommend !","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mister Mister","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLHMrS_GZIJOmg6AQX04iYNpnv3GWUsBTylkcQzQj9dRuQbtQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I stayed at this place while studying in Madrid, and the experience was great. The location is excellent - right in the Centro district, close to major attractions, shops, and cafés. The area feels safe, and the metro station is just a short walk away.

Communication was smooth. They replied much faster to emails written in Spanish, but in general the staff were polite and helpful.

The check-in process was clear and convenient. The staff understand English, so there were no issues when I arrived.

The facilities were clean, quiet, and well-equipped. Everything matched the description. I lived on the third floor, where there is a comfortable study area, which was very useful for my stay.

The value for money is definitely good. I especially liked the social activities, the free breakfast in the morning, the family dinners or tapas and pubcrawls in the evenings, and the different tours they organize. The atmosphere is cozy, and the people staying there were friendly and sociable. There is also an inner courtyard with chairs and a ping-pong table, plus a shared kitchen.

Overall, I definitely recommend this place. I usually came back only in the evenings after my classes, and it always felt nice to return to such a comfortable and welcoming environment.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC0JJ_TYZ4Z23nbOCPJQRKz&fid=0x0:0xc827ac0a57f89537"],"When":"2025-11-23"},{"Name":"Ashley McFolland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVMW6HBBWDM81JW-uedGqP_hW_kbA4RTa_nnoSBiR-mrCCIbQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved my stay at One Fam in Madrid. Spent the weekend there and had the most amazing time. The events were well put together and a really fun opportunity to meet new people. Would definitely stay here again when I come back to Madrid. Special shout out to Adri who made my stay even more memorable :)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Keaton Hughes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLoFpg99EuFEsJNAjXbsddBhuodTvYR6UauoSWkUJjxpp_qEA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An excellent stay, as always. The highlight was unequivocally Melissa's Toledo tour; even torrential unexpected rain didn't sour the day out.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Yehonatan Koren","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLYPAuGIA_NTkevKt9WBLEmxfKGAPFrkQDD85LED_8vjOqzjFFK=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Ive been to some hostels in europa, and  this is definetly the best one so far.

title: Hostal Corazón de Madrid

descriptions: Individually decorated rooms with flat-screen TVs in a down-to-earth hostel offering free Wi-Fi.

title: Room Mate Macarena, Madrid Gran Vía
address: Gran Vía, 43, Centro, 28013 Madrid
descriptions: Stylish, retro-inspired hotel featuring city views, as well as continental breakfast.

The room was not properly cleaned daily and lacked the pleasant scent it had during previous stays.

The hotel now provides a refillable bottle with tap water that must be refilled in the lobby, whereas sealed bottled water was previously offered. I find this change inconvenient.

The front desk staff was not particularly friendly, and I recommend reviewing the final bill carefully, as I was charged for breakfasts I did not consume.

While the terrace is still nice, it has lost some of its charm, and the entrance and elevator used to feel more welcoming. Madrid offers many other great hotel options, although prices have become extremely high.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Caroline McKechnie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU9tjrG-c1C_p5dhTFr5tLpkm3VdO4QZrnWjpyK1hvNCtLyDk_S=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Staff super attentive, rooms very modern, beds comfortable, hotel in all super clean.

The rooftop was incredible and especially the view over Gran Via was amazing. It was the best thing to have this view while being in the pool and eating breakfast. The rooftop area had everything and when the sun is there, it was the best place to relax and have a cold drink. It was a really nice room too, with great aircondition and it really helped a lot that you couldn't hear anything from Gran Via when closing the windows. Overall, the very best stay at a hotel we've ever had, as they had really much too offer and did it perfectly. I definitely recommend this to every other tourist going to Madrid, no matter for how long. For this price it is the best hotel in the city.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Iraida Fernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV-8Uua1eppf-oi2NoV6hIy1RVsAm1n-Xj2-xKUHv9dNr5VbB8=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nice hotel. Thank you for your great service.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Thomas McVeigh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJEEk8SgmtgnzFxH-aLVIp9fCACYAdmZRNbO2hbAA5iPz-ktw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great location, close to many tourists areas with a short walk. Although a little bit worn here and there, overall clean and decent, and to a good standard.
Rooms to the rear are quiet.
Good breakfast, but watch out for the old school dinner ladies running breakfast; try not to get your knuckles rapped for how you use the toaster.
Recomendable.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sebsdad","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeFH6dzZjd_ZFLhXHZKibEzXEy8hWzFB4Qa7PutLe3CnO2G7yVfjd4VSabiCQwQYo5W-g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Was not expecting much from what is essentially a budget hotel in Madrid. I was very pleasantly surprised. Very pleasant and polite check in experience, spacious room with a Juliet balcony, bathroom with powerful shower, small desk, safe, ok breakfast. |Everything was pretty basic, but it had everything I needed for my two night stay.|The location is ideal with plenty of bars and restaurants on the doorstep and a couple of metro stations within a 10 min walk in any direction. |I probably will stay here again as I don’t think I’d be able to get much better for the price.||There are a couple of opportunities for improvement:|There were shutters over the windows and no curtains, so if you’re sensitive to the light, you may get woken up earlier than you want to be by the sunlight. (Didn’t bother me as I had to be up early anyway, but I did notice it so I would suggest bringing an eye mask)|The shower gel dispenser was empty (didn’t matter to me as I had my own toiletries, but it does point to a potential lack of attention to detail.)||I think considering the location, I can happily recommend. (Already have, actually)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"James Briscoe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWDuChcGtNl4DlM-Xn3BUJp6Su6NGCmJhZhUvuAMU2xIYbfYVUu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The hostel seems nice in the lobby and the location is good and that is about as good as it gets.  The rooms were clean which was a good start but numerous things pulled up to make this the worst and most expensive place I stayed in Spain after 4 weeks.  The first thing is this place is loud, the people staying up late yelling, talking loud, banging doors but also the walls don't have any kind of sound insulation of any kind and you can hear every single thing in the rooms next to you,I could literally hear the phone vibrating on silent in the next door room.  The plumbing is also ridiculously loud, every toilet flush you can hear the sewage going down the pipe, when someone turns on water the water pipe makes a loud noise as well.  The bed is a nice firm mattress but it has one crazy 3 foot long hard pillow that is difficult to sleep with and also you don't have a second pillow to cover your head to block all the noise.  The bathroom is very small, which is expected but what's not expected is the 4 foot long walk-in shower with the glass on the wrong side so you have to squeeze between the sink and the glass and hang on to the glass for dear life.  This setup also makes water puddle all over the floor because, let's be honest, you can't have a 4 ft long walk-in shower because the water doesn't stop falling in 4 ft. The hot water or lack of hot water was another issue, I thought maybe it was timing and tried different times and nope, you only get a cold to somewhat warm shower here.  The front desk has a rule where you have to turn in your room key every time you leave and get it back when you come in like you are 5 years old.  I also was told about the breakfast buffet but not that it costs €14 till I had a plate full of food the next morning.  It's not worth €14, there's many places to get way better food for that price.  The staff at the front were nice.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDIysv_kwE&fid=0x0:0xfaa57cfd997c0f4b"],"When":"2025-4-8"}]

title: Hostal Nersan
address: C. de la Flor Baja, 5, Centro, 28013 Madrid
descriptions: Streamlined rooms with a funky vibe in an unpretentious budget hotel offering complimentary Wi-Fi.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Esteve Soria","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQTjd85HgO_0Zf7smgt1EZwNHdOJ6V0fGeWf4trK9l0L_dYaVxqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"UPDATE: 3 months later they have not refunded the money yet.

I booked for two nights and when I got there the room (nº 1) had black mold on the bathroom ceiling and water in the floor.
I called the contact phone and I was informed that they knew about the problem but there was no other room available. They offered me a change to another room for the day after but I should stay in that room for the day. That was not acceptable as that room should have not  been offered at all in the state it was.

I contacted the booking platform and they offered an alternative so we went somewhere else","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDtwMPV9gE&fid=0x0:0x27f0438aab25dfc1"],"When":"2024-2-1"},{"Name":"travellerine","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeqQESQs2CRf2VRyln1Raq0k2FSdB19mGKme5Xh6nHXcs2V7pTrOayJAdxC-qWdu9VyTA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"We booked this hostel with no high expectations, knowing what kind of horrific hostel accomodation central Madrid can offer. This was a pleasant surprise! We arrived at midnight and had no problem getting in, there is always someone there to answer the door and the phone. We were 3 girls and got a room with 3 separate beds at the end of the hallway, incl. night tables, cupboard for clothes and a mirror, also a ceiling fan which was a huge relief during hot summer nights. The window opened to the side street, which was a bit noisy (being right in the very HEART of the city) but we all had earplugs, so sleeping was not a problem. There were 2 toilets and 2 showers on the hallway, toilets always had paper and were cleaned every day. Clean towels were given for no extra charge and were changed upon request. We stayed for a long weekend (3nights) and paid about 14euros each/night, peanuts! The hostel is located right next to Gran Via, 2 min from metro stop Plaza de España, about 5-10min walk from SOL. For this price we honestly could not have asked for more. It was easy to drop off shopping bags and go right back out, without waisting any time. There is a great Grill restaurant right aroung the corner where you get a whole load of meat on "parilla" cold wine and nice salads for about 20€ per person, loved it! Would def stay at this hostel again as there was really nothing to complain about! And most importantly - NO CUCARACHAS seen during our stay, very unusual! :)))","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"littlemissfez1983","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qc1kNGO8LfCIUkNiXrsfeYG3onj8HWsGiBwC71th43hKBnE5GlvQyCYmZ1-b-eE7ReWRw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Admittedly it is not fancy but I found it to be clean, cool and completely adequate for our needs. We had a double room with a private bathroom which was directly opposite the reception so I was worried it might be noisy but we didn't hear anybody else while in the room. The location is great being very close to Gran Via and Plaza de Espana and the staff were all very nice. The only issue we had was the door to the bathroom scraped across the floor and made an unpleasant noise but we once we worked out that lifting the door a little avoided this it was all good.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"TravellerDreamerMore","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QeCck1CfANpZm9bBk3PHtP97Kh9k25HZfvq9yAWlN3BaIyjSHUPEfWkhAJmuYiJsH-_6g=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"The room could be fine but was dirty and full of hair everywhere and on the floor in every corner. |The shower was dark inside and the toilette product were very poor. |My tarif included a welcome drink but I forgot it and they didn't give me anything.|When I reported it at the check out the lady said they she was not there and just took note.|The rooms are on a floor of a private building which as no lights and in the evening is not nice to enter because it is open to everyone (No key, No code).||Avoid it!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"imogenc421","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QfndCAq-uxCGOZMI7LGh3BMpWGEbPhcwmoTu71Rum6FWmIVCdty-rocNNYz1q8SL9qX-Q=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Stayed this weekend and this place must have been totally redone since the previous reviews. We stayed in a twin room, but had a peek into other rooms as they weren't all occupied and they are all modern and nicely decorated and each has their own bathroom. It is located on the fourth floor of an apartment building on a quiet side street between two Japanese restaurants, you just buzz up to the hotel when you arrive. The owner speaks no English but was extremely patient with our terrible Spanish and always very friendly. You are given keys and can come and go as you please. There is free wifi which came in handy. The only downsides were the following:||- The check out time is 12 and check in was from 12.30. Unsurprisingly they were still cleaning the room when we arrived promptly at 12.30, but they let us freshen up and when we came back from lunch at 2 it was all ready for us||- There was only 1 plug socket in the room which was not by the mirror, but there were 2 in the bathroom which we also used||- There was a rail to hang some clothes on but no drawers or wardrobe||||Overall the price was brilliant for such a lovely, comfortable, centrally located room and I would definitely stay here again and recommend it to friends.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"lolam459","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qc1kNGO8LfCIUkNiXrsfeYG3onj8HWsGiBwC71th43hKBnE5GlvQyCYmZ1-b-eE7ReWRw=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Quiet place and not expensive at all. title: HOSTAL ACAPULCO
address: C. de la Salud, 13, 4º, Centro, 28013 Madrid
descriptions: Unpretentious rooms in a modest hotel offering a guest lounge, plus parking & Wi-Fi.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"J. Hong","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJY60uC1RIs3Uof78tcAIoeFdkxw2oLTlU6oG8o_nqJi1LzWA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stayed five nights plus one more night here. Excellent location. Close to Sol and Gran Via metro stations. Walkable to Prado and royal palace. A good Chinese restaurant across the street, and another good Si Chuan restaurant in 4 minutes.

Room not big, but well designed and functional. Our room was facing inside and was very quiet, except for some noisy neighbor sometimes. Ear plugs helped in those cases.

Friendly staff and owner. Hotel front desk staffed 24 hours. Since we stayed for more than 4 nights, we had a free laundry done by the hotel staff, which was very convenient to us and of great value. Owner also kindly agreed to hold a guidebook of Prado museum for us to pick up when we return at the end of our trip before our flight out.

We would stay here again for our future Madrid trip.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmela","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU0fMo9dfgoIdzM2ubLaaQbwM8pLTlataTCj911lIETO6s-ITE_=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our family of 5 adults loved our stay at Acapulco. Thank you Javier and the rest of the team.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Sayers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIbVrewtxaEoVEHjZcBeOi0hgFRgy2gE3KpzkBFDF5vtECVHQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We've stayed here several times since 2009 and it is always a pleasure. The staff were very helpful and friendly,  A comfortable place to sleep and rest while enjoying Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"barbaralB174OP","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0Qdvd3mFTmnQslJajBtyC7hGXBv5KKEN_7z5ZnXByEfDkAAFDmgEV3HW1P9_iXApXyf1XA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"I was most fortunate in booking Hostal Acapulco for my first visit to Madrid; the reason being that it’s location on a vast open square halfway between Puerta Del Sol and Gran Via set me up for accessing the city in any direction that I needed to go. Taxis service the adjacent curbside, which meant that I could be dropped off from Barajas airport right at the hostal’s doorstep. Taking the elevator to the fourth floor entrance led me to Javier, the manager, who could not have been more welcoming and helpful. Rooms are well-outfitted and comfortable, either facing a quiet courtyard or a vibrant, well-lit urban plaza. For my next visit to Madrid, I will return to the Acapulco without hesitation, because it feels like my home away from home!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bilal Qureshi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4u2eKpnN1MySM5pqCuBemRQVWpN8kLSzgN5U5klbrkHgqRKkq=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I came across recommendations for this hotel due to best location chose it.

Our experience was fantastic. We toured everything by foot, no hassle with taxis or public transport. Most of the well-known historic places and top museums are within walking distance - wonderful!

Moreover, there are great options to eat, shop etc nearby. This small family-run hotel is perfectly located for tourists in Madrid.

Front desk is available 24/7 and very helpful with typical tourist questions.

Complimentary coffee, collection of teas is available; also a fridge in the room and a common microwave to warm up stuff. Additional drinks or small snacks like chocolates can be purchased anytime.

Highly recommended for its location, nice-helpful staff, clean and functional room - great value for money!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"mollytrip123","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QftlnsLB8VReEIa8q3x-FUIWL9jAVnw9OiPzFml0xLx7k1DporKgZdMXGALflbNzntF3w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Fabulous small hotel. Room was exceptional and staff were very helpful. Quiet room, balcony, great facilities, great beds. Very clean and well maintained, highly recommended. No stairs, lift access. Also good value for money, superb location. Close to Gran Via metro and Plaza Major just a short walk. Safe area/security building with 24/7 front desk","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: LATROUPE Prado Hostel Madrid
address: Paseo de las Delicias, 1, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid
descriptions: Informal dorms & private rooms, some with balconies, in a casual hostel with a restaurant & a bar.
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Kristine Sweigart","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPMb8htMWyn5DAMQIxAfTg86Ods4ss-n7L3mM5q2AuTQkEK9PL=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stayed here in a private room for two nights after arriving in Madrid because we wanted to have easy access to the train station which was pretty much across the street. The room had an great skylight that you can open with a view of the agriculture ministry which was lit up at night. The room was clean and was really nicely decorated. The bed was comfortable but the shower let water go everywhere. Breakfast was OK but not worth the $10. We paid for it because we wanted the convenience of not trying to find food before the early morning trains since most restaurants are closed that early. Hindsight being 20/20, we could have grabbed something at the train station. The room itself was slightly expensive but worth it for the location.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rachel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLPbf8J22_QFUlyPTg4VjAm94FX3SJ5zCJRw29afkbke_mOTg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I stayed in this hostel for one night and had a fantastic experience! Right from check in, the staff were so friendly and helpful. Though they knew I spoke English, they asked/offered to speak in Spanish to help me practice if I wanted, which I did! I arrived early and was able to use the storage lockers. I stayed in a 4 bed all female dorm. The room was clean, spacious and had large locker drawers for each person. I participated in the Saturday night pub crawl - so much fun and met awesome people!!! The location is great, not right in the center so its quieter, but still close enough to walk everywhere and super close to the Atocha station. Breakfast was amazing. I will definitely stay here again! breakfast was OK.
if I have to point something negative is that I would like a kettle in the private room for make a tea at least. and the heater was locked and was impossible for me setting it a bit more up. in general lines I recommend it.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"mjrc2025","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QftlnsLB8VReEIa8q3x-FUIWL9jAVnw9OiPzFml0xLx7k1DporKgZdMXGALflbNzntF3w=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"Check in was good|Location good|Atmosphere is good|Room was small for family of four. The restaurant POLAR OPPOSITE. They looked annoyed to see us during happy hour as if we did not spend a decent amount of money there. Everything went very smooth, the rooms are cleaned, and towels are changed etc. It's in one of the most central locations (so close to puerta del sol!) and yet the rooms are actually quiet. I would definitely recommend staying here. Lots of nice bars, restaurants and places to go around. Also very close to nice cafes, supermercado and metro.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Steven Coutinho","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVifniue4XULmWWDW8LX0jKESbqaOv11yfHfv6Lf4pmxxOLAQ_6=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It started bad, then got worse...
1. You have to go to a different address (5-10 min away) to check in and get the key. Then you (with your luggage) walk back to the room location.
2. At the room location there is NO front desk, NO concierge, NO luggage storage, NOTHING. Just a locked door and an elevator.
3. The only way to communicate with them is via WhatsApp, or walk back to the other location. But don't waste your time, the receptionist won't do anything.
4. The water was shut off in the middle of the night, and never came on again. Had to go to a nearby bar to use the bathroom. Asked about compensation for this mess: no response.
5. After 11 days, multiple messages and a visit to their office, I finally got a response: as compensation they offered 5% DISCOUNT on my NEXT stay. Unbelievable.
6. Not sure, but they seemed to use a bot for the last communication. You never get to speak with "the boss", he/she may not exist.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ricky Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIMLr2d_VSGyMXF1rbNXhzxBjHkLRg1wWTaWpjYIzFXYdm9icdk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Hostal La Bañezana

BAD;
The single room is ridiculously small. I could not open the luggage in the room, and go to the other side of the room.
The bathroom is soo small, I could brush, shower and pee without moving. In the same spot.
It is sol small, I actually was not able to sit in the toilet....

Extremely small room. A d ridiculously small bathroom.

I was inside the bathroom and I was actually impress, thinking how they got a shower in there

There is ONLY one plug in the room.
The walls are very thin you can hear the people talk in the hall and the other rooms.

No lamps. Only ONE light as well.

The heat was also super highm really hot rooms. And if you open the windows the noise is also bad.

GOOD:
The location is really good and the service was great as well. The place is clean.
BUT make sure you stay in a double room. Avoid at all cost the single room.","Images":null,"When":""}]

The staff is also super friendly and helpful.

The 8-bed dormitory is a bit small, but you still get plenty of privacy thanks to the curtains and the bed-cube design. Overall, a great stay. Highly recommended!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Abigail","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXcagJbpYzSiai4uRwSL3la62SeSaeUm9Nj7Y3zqaH_KHyJVqIHVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This hostel is run by AI there are no humans in this hostel. You enter by putting your sensitive information in a kiosk outside of the hostel, so make sure you have data/wifi. I did this and arrived after checkout time (4 pm), yet the system would not give me my key card, saying that “the bed wasn’t ready”. So I clicked the help button, which connects to a call center in a far away foreign country.  The first time, a woman picked up and said she’s “helping other customers” and she hung up on me, it took 20 more minutes for anyone to answer. A man picked up the call and when I said hello he said “ you better be nice or else I’m not going to help you” I was too stunned to speak, it was pouring rain and all I said was hola. Eventually he realized the mistake and fixed it, I was able to check in. Well it got worse. When you enter the building you have to go down stairs, and there is no elevator, so be prepared if you have heavy bags. I was fortunate to get put in a female dorm, however when I opened the door there was a broken full glass mirror all over the floor. Glass shards were everywhere. I immediately used WhatsApp to tell the ai chat bot, who told me it will transfer it to a human. One hour later, they told me they will not fix anything as “it’s not their fault” . I begged them saying there is glass in the bathroom, shower, everywhere, that this cannot be safe. I asked for compensation and they refused. Well after begging and pleading they sent a human to clean up the glass.

Outside of this tumultuous entry, it seems they have humans clean the rooms every few hours. It is clean, and whatever material they use for the floors dries water quite quickly so the showers and bathroom was fine. They came in about 4 times a day to do random things like change key cards and clean the common bathroom. Because my dorm only had 2-3 people at a time, it was manageable but I would not stay here if it was fully booked, because the walkway is quite narrow and there are no windows if it gets too hot. They do give a free towel (most hostels dont so this was a nice touch), and there is no need to bring a padlock, because your keycard will do it for you.There is no luggage storage. With all this considered, I want you to take a good look at yourself and say is this something I want to do. This is not a social hostel, it’s honestly more of a capsule hotel. I think they should market it like that, and make it more apparent when booking it is run by AI.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Daniela W. Pelton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzIv0w90n6f0lR-jeOkKts_Z5wmOnn7h5XKAmO6b_4Jk8Y1tqM-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I don't understand why this place has high reviews. I've stayed in MANY hostels in many different countries over the last 15 years. But never did I cut my stay short and just forgo the money I paid. But that is how poorly I slept my first night in this hostel. What I don't understand is how you can stuff 8 people into a tiny room with no open window and absolutely no air conditioning at night. I felt like I was suffocating in a coffin. Mind you, this is mid October when there was lovely fresh air at night, but there was no way to open a window because all the rooms are underground. So even though it was cool and crisp outside, it was absolutely BOILING inside the room the entire night. I maybe slept 2 out of the 8 hours I attempted. By early morning, I knew I had to go. I asked if they'd refund me for the 2nd night I paid for, but they refused. I highly DO NOT RECOMMEND unless you enjoy sleeping in sauna-like temperatures. The only pro is I liked the central location, and the Peruvian girl who greeted me at the door had a lovely personality.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Valentina Russano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL0zJqpvNRAkqnmc9cfZGJjCHaRuCu6o_zZeei85QS4VsunLw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"horrible experience, i got there after midnight because my flight got in late and you have to self check in (no reception) went into my room and there was someone sleeping on my bed, complained at reception the next morning and they didn’t even care. weird smells, beds not private enough, extremely small rooms.
don’t stay here, save your money","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maria joão Mascarenhas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUjHjs-Fvx02Iq_I6moPkS9ZrOO-Sb-fSry1I6mjoOXlYcd_Ycj9g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"This hostel is ok if you don’t plan on spending more than 2 nights in there. Even though the location is good and the beds are fairly comfortable, it is impossible to shower decently. The shower simply has no pressure, I could barely rinse the shower gel, let alone wash my hair. On my opinion the quality does not justify the price.","Images":null,"When":""}]

title: Monument to Quevedo
address: Gta. de Quevedo, 9, Chamberí, 28015 Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["Open 24 hours"],"Monday":["Open 24 hours"],"Saturday":["Open 24 hours"],"Sunday":["Open 24 hours"],"Thursday":["Open 24 hours"],"Tuesday":["Open 24 hours"],"Wednesday":["Open 24 hours"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una hermosa escultura dedicada a Francisco de Quevedo, uno de los más grandes escritores del Siglo de Oro español. Este monumento rinde merecido homenaje al genial poeta, novelista y político que marcó la literatura española con su ingenio y maestría verbal. Ubicado en la Glorieta de Quevedo que lleva su nombre, la estatua se encuentra en el corazón de Chamberí, uno de los barrios más emblemáticos de Madrid. La obra capta perfectamente el espíritu intelectual y la personalidad del escritor, siendo un punto de referencia tanto cultural como geográfico en la ciudad. El entorno de la glorieta, con su tránsito constante, refleja la vitalidad literaria que Quevedo representó. Es un lugar ideal para los amantes de la literatura clásica española y para quienes desean rendir tributo a este gigante de las letras hispanas. Una parada obligatoria para comprender el legado cultural de Madrid y de España.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"carloscharmy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWFdw1qpWaL-mYY5fLQw6J2ARy5ZrhgxYlqAtu7vjf2_TufjVo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Esta estatua, creada por el escultor Agustín Querol y erigida en 1902, rinde homenaje al escritor madrileño Francisco de Quevedo. Originalmente ubicada en la plaza de Santa Bárbara, fue trasladada en 1963 a su ubicación actual en la glorieta de Quevedo. El monumento se destaca por su pedestal ornamentado, aunque algunos elementos previstos no se llegaron a instalar. Rodeada de una fuente monumental, la estatua forma parte de la historia escultórica de Madrid, junto a otras obras de Querol en la ciudad. Ideal para una visita cultural en la zona.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDHlp7cjAE&fid=0x0:0x75995848fd71a015"],"When":"2024-9-15"},{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es obra del escultor Agustín Querol Subirats en 1902. La escultura es de cuerpo entero, representa a Quevedo vestido como caballero de la época con la cruz de Santiago en el pecho, espada y capa corta, con sus típicos anteojos, bigote y perilla. En su mano derecha porta una pluma y en la izquierda guarda en el cinto un billete.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICktc-bDQ&fid=0x0:0x75995848fd71a015"],"When":"2018-12-24"},{"Name":"Linoferho Fernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWhv0CAHZzDdPwjajSGRWcssSivfzgk_BZledfaZDmfOAopMdHv=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Monumento erigido en honor del escritor Francisco de Quevedo, uno de los genios literatos que colaboraron en dar brillo a las letras españolas del S. XVII.

La escultura es obra del escultor Agustín Querol, muy popular en el Madrid de la época, y fue inaugurada en 1902, con la presencia del Rey Alfonso XIII y del entonces alcalde de Madrid Alberto Aguilera, fué colocada en la que hoy es la plaza de Alonso Martinez, y trasladada, en los años 60, a su ubicación actual.

La figura es de mármol de Carrara, el pedestal de caliza tiene unas figuras alegóricas a la actividad literaria.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDm34mFjQE&fid=0x0:0x75995848fd71a015"],"When":"2022-2-13"},{"Name":"José Julio Melgares","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPlI3_WxZ-JbiFDSG6A1n_aKR_BFRpvuYb0hLgejNuccGQun5Z=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El monumento de Francisco Gómez de Quevedo Villegas y Santibáñez Cevallos (Madrid, 1580 - Villanueva de los Infantes, Ciudad Real, 1645), ilustre escritor del siglo de Oro, preside esta glorieta desde 1963, antes estaba situado en la Plaza de Alonso Martínez. Realizado en 1902 por el escultor catalán Agustín Querol Subirats (Tortosa, 1860 - Madrid, 1909).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE8-PGuQE&fid=0x0:0x75995848fd71a015"],"When":"2017-8-24"},{"Name":"daniele pedroni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnkaPKb1GmjVC8z_wjLbuqZ_-jhO2XAXa2yuEZIiK57ABxvoYO=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Quevedo o il Monumento a Quevedo è un esempio di arte pubblica a Madrid , Spagna. Un'opera di Agustín Querol , è dedicata a Francisco de Quevedo , illustre scrittore dell'era barocca .
Opera di Agustín Querol , fu inaugurata nella sua ubicazione originale in Plaza de Alonso Martínez  il 5 giugno 1902 (la cerimonia era stata più volte ritardata dal maltempo) come parte di una serie di inaugurazioni di sculture all'aperto a Madrid in occasione delle celebrazioni per il raggiungimento della maggiore età del re Alfonso XIII ,  alle quali parteciparono diverse personalità di spicco come, oltre a Quevedo, Agustín Argüelles , Lope de Vega , Juan Bravo Murillo , Eloy Gonzalo .
La statua in marmo di Carrara in piedi che rappresenta Quevedo è posta sulla sommità del monumento. Indossa il suo Pince-nez (chiamato quevedos in spagnolo dal suo nome) e la croce di San Giacomo sul petto. Quattro sculture allegoriche che rappresentano Satira , Poesia , Prosa e Storia abbracciano il basamento in pietra calcarea .
Il calcare di Novelda del piedistallo si è eroso negli anni '60 e il piedistallo è stato sostituito da una replica di pietra (meno porosa) di Atarce scolpita da Fernando Cruz Solís . Il monumento è stato spostato nella sua posizione attuale nella Glorieta de Quevedo  negli anni '60.
Nel 1999, sulla base del monumento è stata installata una fontana in granito di Badajoz.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Xoom Too","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLRzhuxFKh1jOBQiJLb1bjferwZ-xPRZ7v7ReqgRp2P_uVyh9Y4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The monument was created on June 5, 1902 and created by Agustín Querol, and Fernando Cruz Solís. The monument is also located in a roundabout area on the Fuencarral street and near the Quevedo metro station.

The monument is dedicated to Francisco del Quevedo who was a Spanish politican and writer known for being one of the most important writers of the Spanish baroque and known for books such as La cuna y la sepultura, Historia de la vida del Buscón, Los Sueños, and more.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICL1dz0iwE&fid=0x0:0x75995848fd71a015"],"When":"2024-6-21"},{"Name":"Javier Gonzalez-Soria","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU-bs7DdPBvaX0K1wRpct26TnEXl3DQYsxz29GO9vFz9f3Expitag=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El monumento a Francisco de Quevedo fue erigido a petición del alcalde Alberto Aguilera, por el escultor Agustín Querol e inaugurado, aún sin terminar, el 5 de enero de 1902, en la plaza de Santa Bárbara, actual Alonso Martínez y con la presencia del rey, de Alberto Aguilera y las principales autoridades y personajes de Madrid.

La glorieta de Quevedo ha sido una de tantas plazas madrileñas por las que fueron desfilando larga serie de monumentos, un tanto errabundos, que han tardado años en hallar su ubicación definitiva. Así, en 1902, el mismo año que se inaugura el monumento en la plaza de Santa Bárbara, se inaugura e instala en esta glorieta de Quevedo, la estatua de Lope de Vega, y años después, fue sustituida por los Héroes del Dos de mayo, de Aniceto Marinas. Más tarde, se instaló en este lugar, el monumento a Quevedo y en el que sigue actualmente.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Además, la glorieta está decorada con cuatro árboles con leds azules por navidad.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICB5Im7Iw&fid=0x0:0x7e2ec2610bfa189d"],"When":"2022-12-17"},{"Name":"Salvator Mundi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl3Y3brUOzzqe3dORyucP80GI88NIqEWYGdpx9_dHNvvi0kc5K=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Die Glorieta de Quevedo, dieser kleine Kreisverkehr im Herzen von Madrid, ist ein Ort, der gleichermaßen Geschichte, Alltagshektik und eine Prise Chaos in sich vereint.
Als ich mich ihr nähere, wird schnell klar, dass sie nicht mit majestätischen Statuen oder opulenten Brunnen prahlt.
Nein, die Glorieta de Quevedo ist eine echte Diva: unaufgeregt, ein wenig exzentrisch, und immer mitten im Geschehen.

In ihrer Mitte steht eine Statue von Francisco de Quevedo, dem berühmten Dichter und Satiriker des spanischen Siglo de Oro. Und wie könnte es anders sein – Quevedo, bekannt für seine scharfe Zunge und seinen beißenden Humor, steht da mit einem Blick, der zu sagen scheint: „So viele Autos, und keiner hat Zeit für ein gutes Sonett?“ Er hält eine Feder in der einen und ein Buch in der anderen Hand, als wolle er jedem vorbeieilenden Pendler einen Vers nachwerfen.
Doch die meisten beachten ihn kaum, zu sehr beschäftigt mit ihrem Tag – oder dem Versuch, diesen vertrackten Verkehrsknotenpunkt heil zu überstehen.

Die Glorieta selbst ist ein Wirbel aus städtischem Leben. Autos kreisen um sie herum wie auf einem Karussell, Motorräder schlängeln sich geschickt durch die engen Lücken, und Fußgänger kämpfen tapfer, um die Straße zu überqueren.
Die Ampeln hier haben offenbar ihren eigenen Zeitplan, der selten mit dem der Fußgänger übereinstimmt.
Es fühlt sich ein bisschen an wie ein Tanz, nur dass niemand den Takt kennt.

Rund um die Glorieta reihen sich Bars, Cafés und Geschäfte aneinander, die ihre ganz eigene Geschichte erzählen. Eine charmante Mischung aus hippen Lokalen und traditionellen Läden prägt das Viertel.
Es gibt eine Bäckerei, die so duftet, dass man fast vergisst, warum man hier überhaupt hergekommen ist, und ein paar kleine Buchläden, die vermutlich mehr Geschichten über Quevedo verkaufen, als er je selbst geschrieben hat.

Doch zurück zu unserem Dichter in der Mitte: Quevedo hat diesen Platz nicht immer für sich gehabt.
Die Glorieta wurde im 19. Jahrhundert angelegt, und wie so viele Orte in Madrid hat sie sich im Laufe der Zeit verändert.
Früher war sie ruhiger, weniger verkehrsreich – ein Ort, an dem sich Spaziergänger in Ruhe ihre Gedanken sammeln konnten.
Heute dagegen scheint es, als ob Quevedo selbst kaum mit dem Kopf aus seinen Papieren hochkommen würde, um das Durcheinander um ihn herum zu registrieren.

Trotzdem hat die Glorieta de Quevedo einen unbestreitbaren Charme. Sie ist lebendig, eigenwillig und ein bisschen chaotisch – genau wie die Stadt, in der sie sich befindet.
Während ich dort stehe und das bunte Treiben beobachte, stelle ich mir vor, wie Quevedo seine spitze Feder in die Hand nimmt und über diesen Ort schreiben würde. Wahrscheinlich wäre es eine bissige Satire über die Absurditäten des modernen Lebens.
Oder vielleicht würde er einfach schmunzeln, einen Schluck Wein aus einer nahen Taverne nehmen und denken: „Madrid bleibt Madrid – immer ein wenig verrückt, aber dafür umso liebenswerter.“

Stand: 17.11.2024

"Das Leben bewegt sich sehr, sehr schnell. Wenn man nicht gelegentlich anhält und sich umschaut, könnte man es verpassen"","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Juan Zuñiga","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWn3T98VZZ4vRWHU9ba8rgrEqZIU15U3Ri5euEnnSngdJ1ecbI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Chamberí es un distrito de Madrid formado por seis barrios: Almagro, Trafalgar, Arapiles, Gaztambide, Ríos Rosas y Vallehermoso. Su desarrollo urbanístico es fruto del Plan de Ensanche de Carlos María de Castro aprobado en 1860 cuando desapareció la Real Cerca de Felipe IV y ejecutado en las décadas siguientes hasta la consolidación de los años treinta del siglo pasado y las reconstrucciones tras la Guerra Civil. Aquí se encuentran los palacios y palacetes mas bellos de la ciudad –los supervivientes a la piqueta de los años 50, 60 y 70– y aquí vivieron personalidades como Benito Pérez Galdós, José Ortega y Gasset, Gregorio Marañón, Rubén Darío, Antonio Machado, Pablo Neruda, Gerardo Diego, Ramón Pérez de Ayala, Agustín Querol, Joaquín Sorolla, Mariano Benlliure y Miguel de Unamuno. Hoy pasearemos entre las esculturas públicas que se exhiben en este distrito.

La más importante es, quizás, la dedicada a Francisco de Quevedo en la glorieta con su nombre, obra de Agustín Querol (1860-1909), uno de los encargos promovidos por el alcalde Alberto Aguilera en 1902 como parte del programa escultórico conmemorativo de la mayoría de edad de Alfonso XIII. La escultura es bellísima pero el flujo de tráfico rodado apenas permite apreciarla. Tal vez habría que sustituirla por una réplica y llevar a un museo la original.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICbvt_MmQE&fid=0x0:0x7e2ec2610bfa189d"],"When":"2024-7-27"}]
