A display that leaves a great impression and I'm sorry that I didn't take pictures of the monument from more angles because I wasn't familiar with its history and meaning (hence the upper part was taken from one of the reviewers and I thank him on behalf of the community for the explanation). It is a pity that the monuments in Madrid do not have an explanation in English somewhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAzJKnF15hQkJ6Yg3_RaBVL&fid=0x0:0xb622eb7a727fc219"],"When":"2025-12-31"},{"Name":"macedonboy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL7obIFVFelZpGZFWErr47Wzzrz8xLtHVDOYNGdpIt459EbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This sculpture stands in tribute to the victims of the 1977 Atocha massacre, a part of neofascist terrorism during the Spanish transition to democracy, when right wing terror groups attempted return the country to dictatorship.

The monument is a nice appear to brotherhood and sisterhood, arm in arm and shoulder to shoulder standing against tyranny. Having said that, this is more for the locals than tourists. It's also well away from the main tourist areas, so difficult to justify coming to see.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE1L7iywE&fid=0x0:0xb622eb7a727fc219"],"When":"2019-4-22"},{"Name":"Geovanny","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWpba9fybFOGKARgiQWJJKLXixzY6BzcQ8Id_v0GFsgkaMSRfk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Atocha lawyers attack was a 1977 massacre in Madrid where right-wing extremists murdered five labor lawyers, aiming to destabilize Spain's democratic transition.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDIzNfT4AE&fid=0x0:0xb622eb7a727fc219"],"When":"2025-4-7"},{"Name":"Taylor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVgSFmr7SXwpWEv1WORlQOxsDwE1-uNaB_5cG3V1mUuXyyGIy38_Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Thankful to have visited the solemn sculpture.The Plaza de Antón Martín, between Lavapiés and the Barrio de las Letras literary quarter, is home to this sculpture that pays tribute to the labour lawyers murdered in their office at No. 55 Calle Atocha street. On 24 January 1977, a group of far-right extremists burst into their offices and gunned down five left-wing lawyers who were associated with the Comisiones Obreras trade union and the Spanish Communist Party, wounding four others. The monument was unveiled on 10th June 2003

Approved by Madrid City Council in 2002 and promoted by Comisiones Obreras, the monument is a three-dimensional adaptation of the painting “Amnesty” created by the Valencian painter Juan Genovés in 1976 and currently housed in the Reina Sofia Museum, Spain. It was used during the Spanish Transition to democracy to demand the release and amnesty of political prisoners. Genovés himself was in charge of turning it into a sculpture, the first one he made in his professional career. The piece, made of bronze, represents a circle of people united in a group hug; it is 3.5 m in diameter and 4 m in height, with a white stone base and anti-graffiti protection. The statue was cast at the foundry "Arte6".

On 24 January 2007, on the 30th anniversary of the massacre, a commemorative plaque was added to the bottom of the monument that reads: “To the lawyers of Atocha: if the echo of their voice weakens, we will perish”, an adaptation of an excerpt from a poem by Paul Éluard.

The painter Juan Genovés was born in Valencia in 1930, where he trained at the Royal Academy of San Carlos. A member of the groups Los Siete, Parpalló, Hondo and the ASAP (Association of Plastic Artists), he travelled to Paris in 1955, where he learned about informalism and new artistic currents. His work, with its strong political and social commitment, is represented in major museums and collections around the world. He received the National Plastic Arts Award in 1984.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lupex","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWmrxhKO4XmwSx6j6KEecO79zeqs2c0yfuZRw1HaPZz615KW4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"impressive statue right next to the metro exit","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCglp3GzAE&fid=0x0:0xb622eb7a727fc219"],"When":"2025-2-16"},{"Name":"Praba Pillai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVeB-RfEkYd3vfgATa15jmyCHJ3jthck5ik15xupreZ5nfXwWa6nQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Interesting sculpture","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDn_tmH8QE&fid=0x0:0xb622eb7a727fc219"],"When":"2024-10-6"},{"Name":"Ben Gray","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKAe-_HnCusJ4Zx1IMSps5w8OqGqQvCVETCblAETRORpKu6Ag=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Commemorates the murder of a group of lawyers in a terrorist attack.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nicolás La Bella","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMNJpBwmvLUCRVkYQe2vnMKk25fNYrRLHrvE4HGzirW6yPWq3w-w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Not so interesting","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Xoom Too","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLRzhuxFKh1jOBQiJLb1bjferwZ-xPRZ7v7ReqgRp2P_uVyh9Y4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Daoiz and Velarde monument is located in the ‘Plaza del Dos de Mayo’ right in the smack center. This sculpture is based off 2 people who were in the military Luiz Daoiz (1762-1808) and Pedro Velarde (1779-1808) that had brought the spirit of the National State to the army and other establishments for them to join them against the French.

The plaza where this monument is located is also based of their movement on the 2nd of May 1808 which was against the French troops. There’s also another monument of them in the plaza of Queen Victoria Eugenia in the province of Segovia not far off from Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICL9PafpgE&fid=0x0:0x37ce2ba9745bda73"],"When":"2024-6-15"},{"Name":"Gustav Ohlsson Hallgren","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU79vQ6t6vgf507p5oDCA_2Nub_vYv7B7czVsa6TTrZ5lFvUdcieQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great square with 3 different playgrounds. Good cafe around","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Julio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIWBBpBXlKk7PFYHuZWo-TDvrSDTT2o-LEU5Z_BZdCLGammIA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un monumento histórico en el corazón de Malasaña, dedicado a los capitanes Daoíz y Velarde, héroes del levantamiento del 2 de mayo de 1808 contra las tropas napoleónicas. La escultura, inaugurada en 1881, es una de las más representativas del barrio y un símbolo de la resistencia madrileña.
Dato curioso: originalmente se instaló en la Plaza del Dos de Mayo, lugar donde se alzaron los madrileños contra los franceses, convirtiéndose en un punto de encuentro cargado de memoria histórica. Hoy es un rincón perfecto para descansar, tomar algo en las terrazas y conectar con la historia del barrio más castizo y rebelde de Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD2myMe8cyMVSNJMOdHZ-7a&fid=0x0:0x37ce2ba9745bda73"],"When":"2025-8-23"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Impresionante monumento que conmemora a los héroes Daoiz y Velarde, figuras emblemáticas del levantamiento del 2 de mayo. La escultura es majestuosa y transmite el valor y el sacrificio de estos militares españoles. El conjunto escultórico está ejecutado con gran maestría y atención al detalle histórico. Ubicado en un lugar significativo que honra apropiadamente su memoria. Es un testimonio fundamental de la resistencia madrileña contra la invasión napoleónica. Una visita esencial para entender la historia de Madrid y su pueblo valiente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Doncelador","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXnewNEbYYaXdHD0epgVByIWzW2jKqBZMPTzU18-HYzM_sBuOGCqw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El grupo escultórico es de estilo neoclásico, sus autores son el escultor Antonio Solá y el arquitecto Francisco Jareño, data de 1822, está situada en el centro de la Plaza del Dos de Mayo. Representa a los dos militares de pie cogidos de la mano en señal de su juramento, apoyados en un cañón, vestidos con el uniforme de la época y cubiertos con túnicas. Detrás de la escultura se sitúa el Arco de  Monteleon.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICktZenSw&fid=0x0:0x37ce2ba9745bda73"],"When":"2018-12-20"},{"Name":"José Julio Melgares","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPlI3_WxZ-JbiFDSG6A1n_aKR_BFRpvuYb0hLgejNuccGQun5Z=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Situado a los pies del arco que fue la entrada del Cuartel de Monteleón, único vestigio que queda del acuartelamiento. Los capitanes Luis Daoiz y Torres (Sevilla, 1767 - Madrid 1808) y Pedro Velarde (Muriedas, Cantabria, 1779 - Madrid, 1808) oficiales del Cuartel de Monteleón, fueron parte de los muchos héroes en el levantamiento del 2 de mayo. El monumento es obra del escultor Antonio Solá (Barcelona, 1780 - Roma, 1861) realizada en 1830.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE84PV1gE&fid=0x0:0x37ce2ba9745bda73"],"When":"2018-7-29"},{"Name":"Jenny Rodríguez Pérez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUwINcz-fVm_SEioKQ1EfMBtwNs9nQVLjoWLB5Dja8LBHkopwdY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"En La Habana, en su municipio Cerro hay dos calles nombradas en honor a ellos. Las dos forman parte de la dirección de las casas mia y  la de mi esposo. Cuando lo ví me traslade automaticamente a mis origene a pesar que no se sabe mucho la historia de ellos en mi barrio. Una pena que el monumento esté maltratado a pesar de que organizan visitas con guías a este lugar y barrio.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Fatares","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXFtES0b0_d227NaRy9Njsxyrww1C2OW-DyIdif-ri93rA6ZSR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy buena escultura, en homenaje a los heróicos artilleros Daoiz y Velarde, que desde el cuartel de Monteleón capitanearon al no menos heroico pueblo de Madrid y se opusieron al invasor napoleónico. ¡Gloria y honor¡","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Anıl KARADAVUT","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX41QutsezWuUxEkWEGEh54GbviHVkeK6anDClkT0zTcMDuI1A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located in Plaza de España, this monument is a must-see for literature lovers. It honors Miguel de Cervantes, author of Don Quijote de la Mancha, and features the iconic duo: Don Quijote on horseback with his lance, and loyal Sancho Panza on a donkey beside him.
What’s particularly interesting is a local interpretation that the weapon a statue holds often hints at how that figure met their end. In this case, legend has it that Don Quijote, shown holding a lance, was killed by one—a poetic (and slightly dark) twist.
Madrid is full of symbolic statues like this, blending history, culture, and a bit of mystery in the open air.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEKKWp8-GjJ6TngE&fid=0x0:0xf9f72931f97af3b7"],"When":"2025-5-19"},{"Name":"Sveto Rakovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXELv1h6KNSxjLBZ4irfEkGKjzrypoUHg3izpRjZZ_yFLu388A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Famous literary characters of the great Spanish writer Cervantes.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkVwH0Bl6L_bOqqKFSk36b&fid=0x0:0xf9f72931f97af3b7"],"When":"2025-12-29"},{"Name":"Anri Erinin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLAh9vsD7Y0_CWAVAT3opYtBy4iU7ou127Bvqq_WAXN3_8oMQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The figures of Don Quixote and his squire Sancho Panza. user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lorenzo Berardinelli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQYZfs7aGmPhWV6GArevvBQrV3lL4dmCAQU_1872nRZD-NgA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I visited the Museum of Illusions in Madrid with my girlfriend, and it was a fun experience overall. However, I think it’s more enjoyable if you go in a group of more than two people, since many of the photo spots are designed for pairs or groups. Inside, there aren’t that many interactive activities, but the main issue is definitely the number of children running around — though of course, you can’t really ask them not to come! Other than that, we had a good time. I’d recommend going with friends or people you know you can have fun with anywhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDmml8-lSdI7MJDcz6dCc1F\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-10-27"},{"Name":"Darren Beck","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLRfTQ5lzSivZGEcb4EWCur2uNoffJUe10rBNwzOdNobY-mUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A little small but worth an hour or so if you want a break from the heat. Note that it definitely skews more toward a younger crowd, and there were a lot of families with little kids there. As a group of two adults though, it was still a fun experience. I’m sure the kids had a great time as well. Staff were great also. Give it a try if you need a break from the sightseeing and just want some fun relaxation and air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDZBKUvmAhb_LOAn8QK7LQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-13"},{"Name":"rated by maryam • food critic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2rFU889_uyIosz_7bpffSFpmpz0-gKcaVKqZvHb7h1qD_qhXV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely entertaining from start to finish! The Museum of Illusions in Madrid kept us all fully engaged — we could’ve easily spent two hours exploring. The installations are clever, interactive, and truly high quality. My kids had a blast, and honestly, so did I. Every room is picture perfect, and the staff made sure the flow was easy and fun. Whether you’re going with kids or just want to play with perspective and be wowed, this spot delivers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvIn2cbOwD1xlT2Bju8D5l\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-1"},{"Name":"Alex S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuqMxZHWW4Q3oWg1MozJ5EDSP48AY-vf9ImsmDzcUsLKEKjbGa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow! What a great museum! If you want to find where the magic is at in Madrid, look no further! Offering many illusions for you to try out and capture, this museum is incredibly fun for everyone of all ages except people who otherwise have vertigo, as some of the illusions you will have to walk through may not be good for people with vertigo.\nIf you are wanting a fun activity to do with your kids, it’s a great place! Depending on your pace, you could be at this museum for a while. My favourite illusion was the vortex, as it is a really cool experience and it really makes you feel like you are spinning in an actual vortex. If that’s your thing, it is the last illusion downstairs at the very end of the room. This museum is also not far from the train line, so you can travel here easily.\nIn sum, if you’re wanting something to do while you’re in Madrid, come here!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAiCzHJZBmXN2Y3VDQxPYol\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-7"},{"Name":"Michael Stockdale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIrWLzicQpgC3hAFUv5MqBQNE3eX1_sKxt7Pa3MR8iF60jyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was okay but l was hoping to see more than l did for the entry price.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoNl4gi2TxojBRMWk7xy-N\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-12-15"},{"Name":"Rebecca Sirois","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVZzU3H_Gmj---yW_cc8WSqFd5G5oCZ4-hT-20m9bPEiVF3i3R=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a fun experience with the kids. Definitely buy tickets in advance, they don't let you in early so show up right on time. Staff were helpful for picture taking otherwise a fun place to wander through at your leisure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS7-Y2SbFgXGvCL3e_1CBg\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"Joel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl5821tyzJrwoJ9WtZ4ckHFtm77vetb8svyUaCTvs_yjbDKrzH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had such a great time at the Museum of Illusions in Madrid! I went with my two kids and they absolutely loved it — from the mind-bending exhibits to all the fun photo ops, there was never a dull moment. A big shoutout to Roberto, who went above and beyond. He was incredibly kind and patient, explaining how the illusions worked in a way my kids could understand, and even helped us take some amazing photos. He showed us the best angles and made sure we got the most out of each exhibit. Definitely recommend for a fun, family-friendly outing. So much fun and lots of laughs!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA41UAbda0q4GHtxLgczpgq\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"David Sargent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNd7Vx1pxVZ2hd91K_Xk_NBWo4ZUDgz8hEItPBONm_A7r1mRme=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love finding unusual museums, and I read about this one on WikiTravel when I had some spare time on a recwnt business trip. This museum was an absolute delight! My only regret was that I was alone, as a lot of the displays are interactive and the couples and groups were having so much fun watching and photographing each other and srading places and such that I decided that someday I need to go back there with at least one other person who hasn't seen it before. Fingers crossed for another trip to Madrid one of these days!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Can","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVNslvhVRdHS5a2DPOldz7fU8nbA3R7JngSGWMd6SbjHvobHpr8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is the most amazing museum I've ever seen. Everyone working there is very polite. The museum is incredibly detailed and has particularly fantastic fossils. The price is very, very reasonable for a place like this. Definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMaaPLmAy-5IIsZKz9fBvM&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2026-2-11"},{"Name":"Syed Jilani","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXmWDI7kflGQbOE_B-gKNBzjNx8IJRO-qeeYZEawZymUa7PPmIV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Visiting Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales feels less like walking into a museum and more like stepping into a carefully curated adventure through time. From the very first hall, you’re surrounded by giants of the natural world — towering dinosaur skeletons, massive whale bones suspended overhead, and displays that instantly spark a childlike sense of wonder, no matter your age.

What makes this museum special is how it balances scale with storytelling. The fossil galleries don’t just show ancient creatures; they invite you to imagine their lives, their habitats, and the dramatic changes that shaped our planet. One moment you’re staring up at a prehistoric predator, and the next you’re face-to-face with meticulously preserved mammals, birds, and insects that highlight the incredible diversity of life on Earth.

The evolution and biodiversity sections are especially engaging. They gently guide you through complex scientific ideas without ever feeling heavy or overwhelming. Interactive elements and well-written explanations keep things lively, making it easy to lose track of time as you move from ecosystems to human evolution. There’s a quiet thrill in realizing how deeply connected everything is — from microscopic organisms to the largest animals that ever lived.

The building itself adds to the experience. Grand yet welcoming, it has that classic museum charm where history lingers in the air, but the exhibits feel fresh and thoughtfully updated. Families, solo travelers, and curious minds all seem equally at home here.

By the time you leave, you don’t just feel informed — you feel inspired. The Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales is an adventurous journey through nature’s past and present, one that reminds you how astonishing, fragile, and endlessly fascinating our world truly is.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Moses Jhon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVTMzTmKx317bL25VfaSo0yD1VlLLd7gxxf7uMu8Tzd1BCEUzU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"s there a better way to spark your curiosity than stepping into a world where dinosaurs roam and ancient ecosystems come alive? MNCN in Madrid is a treasure trove of natural wonders that had me hooked from the first fossil. The interactive exhibits, especially the human evolution display, make you feel like a scientist on a discovery mission. Pro tip: grab tickets online for €7 (free for kids under 4 or seniors over 65) to skip the queues, and start on the left side of the building for the best flow. Oh, and the African elephant in the lobby? It’s been standing tall since 1929, a silent guardian of the museum’s epic story. Bring comfy shoes and a water bottle—you’ll want to linger for hours","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCsBanB9aY1UYEiIrOQx7g_&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-7-15"},{"Name":"Summer A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWkStxgtyPwdVIJ1k6zDUe9ZikrcD65XzzmzgOE4A98xhEtpMT3xw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited the Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales in Madrid and was genuinely impressed. It’s the kind of place that strikes the right balance — fun and engaging for kids, but just as interesting for adults. There’s also a cafe just nearby to relax afterwards. Everything in the museum is written and explained both in Spanish and English so it’s very tourist-friendly.

One of the most striking things is getting to see life-size replicas of animals, from elephants to whales to dinosaurs. You can stare a tiger or a gorilla straight in the face — something you wouldn’t be able to do in the wild without putting yourself in serious danger. It really gives you a sense of their scale and presence.

There was a great exhibit on the history of dinosaurs. And one of my favorite parts was a photo exhibition showing animals in funny, human-like poses — funny and cleverly done. The exhibit explained how seeing animals this way can help people empathize with them, which in turn can raise awareness and motivate action to protect their habitats. Thoughtful, while also pretty entertaining.

Another interesting part of the museum is an exhibit dedicated to Santiago Ramón y Cajal, the Spanish neuroscientist who won a Nobel Prize for his work on the structure of the nervous system. The way his research is displayed feels almost like peeking into the early days of modern science — delicate drawings, old instruments, and lots of context. You can also see some of his photography - an interest he had on the side.

There’s also a beautiful old room that used to be part of a private natural history collection from a former mayor. It still has that “cabinet of curiosities” vibe — dark wood, antique display cases, and a mix of fossils, minerals, and preserved animals. It’s like stepping back in time, and it adds a really charming contrast to the more modern parts of the museum.

All in all, it’s a really worthwhile visit — part science, part history, and part wonder. Whether you’re with kids or just feeling curious yourself, there’s a lot to explore and enjoy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8fMKsFQsgujaIWQq8mUUR&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-7-16"},{"Name":"Michael Currin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWP39nsOd4LZSJSXbjuEyg2bRgo__eldMdZfh1xXPuWucYXmsTVVg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The fossil collection of dinosaurs and related animals was impressive.
Also enjoyed the section on dinosaurs to birds with the taxidermied birds.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBbDXTdlaDJF0yAUtAS2Se0&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2026-1-6"},{"Name":"Anthony Kraag","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIBVOrMEqTU4Ng7DSUYYRPbIEnGvv6fNYdSjvT3okp4F-Wuww=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales offers an impressive collection of stuffed animals, including an elephant, lion, and tiger, all beautifully preserved and displayed. The dinosaur fossils were another highlight—fascinating to see up close! Furthermore, there was a stunning photo exhibition showcasing beautiful images of animals, captured with incredible detail (by Tim Flach)
The exhibits are well-organized and educational, making it a great place for both kids and adults. The museum has a classic, old-school charm but still feels engaging. Plus, it's very affordable!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDAotX3zAE&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2025-2-7"},{"Name":"SM Othman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWITlDmHJnZaPhLNddQ8ra0b3AZAvQP2-PpjTBAW4RsjS-SoHxa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited the Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales in Madrid, and it was an incredible experience! The museum offers a fascinating journey through the natural world, with a wide range of exhibits covering everything from paleontology and geology to biodiversity and ecosystems.

The dinosaur skeletons and fossils were particularly impressive, captivating visitors of all ages. The displays were well-curated, informative, and engaging, making it easy to learn while enjoying the visuals. The museum is also very family-friendly, with interactive sections that are perfect for kids.

The building itself is beautiful, and the staff were welcoming and helpful throughout my visit. Whether you’re a science enthusiast or just looking for an educational and entertaining activity in Madrid, this museum is a must-visit. I highly recommend taking your time to explore all the exhibits—there’s so much to see and learn!

I’ll definitely be coming back next time I’m in Madrid!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDv8J68ygE&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2024-12-17"},{"Name":"Carlos Melgar Castillo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVK4eXDd_2Rd_9e64PgSMjfdygc-r_b0wbPtdjNNs3kcDK-fuCxFg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Entry fees: (August 2024)

- Free entry on Sundays from 17:00 -20:00 hours.
- Normal entry 7 euros.

Spectacular museum!!

Great for people who love science and biology and people who can appreciate biodiversity and care about endangered species.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr48qxKw&fid=0x0:0x141d274e9dbb7a8e"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An absolutely stunning museum explaining the history of Madrid. It is free admission for this museum and absolutely worth a visit. The collection is amazing and includes everything from paintings sculptures Drawings, models to clothing, fans, pottery, ceramics, and cartography. One of my favorite things was the model of the bullfighting Arena, which is incredibly detailed, including hundreds of miniature spectators. Some of the artists who have paintings include Velasquez and Madrazo. Some practicalities can help enjoying the museum even more - audio guide via headsets so that your hands are free. As otherwise you hold everything in your hands (not even a small bag is allowed inside) which is very inconvenient","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAxn3jfe8g1l_7U_yWQmKWA&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"eh m","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDc4S4npu6fVJbaYEi1ib1lumKNzCIEZg_ypimQordOgj_nw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cerralbo is one of those places where you can genuinely imagine how people once lived. A charming and atmospheric place;  highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBThl4J40qEvXdEKoJhUZ1d&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-11-29"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Eran Gordon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9TCfglhZAbghI0RPu-m-ZPBi-FZdLJGAKjRajtReHhhnskaMd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Nice place for kids, very gimicy but I think kids will like it, and The stuff at the tour was really nice.
They give a little gummy candy every room, and a small VR game at the end.
The price is 15€ per person.

I give 1 star cause they offered me a candy if I give them a good review.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQt1W2fArOblsDLJOasmnP&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-2"},{"Name":"Amanda Crook","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJI--3PEU6CVV2n1C01h-yKGKVCbVqSSw_3JMHuND5PXexbxg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Super cute and fun activity for kids. Takes about 30 minutes to complete. Take the slide down!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDTXKFaDAMvt_TxkkmUMJ5T&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-12-29"},{"Name":"Ana Reyes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWMwtoVpk1i3x_wiRJTSSM-Q4T6tR8QfucPFB3sfTx613SCfoKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"1/5 stars*

Visited The Sweet Space museum in Madrid with my family, and unfortunately, it was a disappointing experience. We were expecting a fun and engaging time, but what we got was quite the opposite.

Firstly, the duration of the experience was misleading. They claimed it would be around 40-50 minutes long, but in reality, we were done in less than 15 minutes. It felt like a rushed and overpriced experience.

The attractions themselves were also quite boring. My kids and I didn't find them engaging or entertaining, and it seemed like they were just going through the motions.

The only highlights of our visit were the toboggan and the pool full of plastic balls. My kids seemed to enjoy those parts, but it wasn't enough to make up for the rest of the experience. My kids started crying ad they were looking for more things to do but by that time we were already by the exit.

Overall, I wouldn't recommend The Sweet Space museum to others. It felt like a scam, and the lack of transparency about the experience duration was frustrating. Maybe they can work on improving their attractions and being more honest about what visitors can expect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCYATxwx6h_tORsyiHHhdMY&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Deborah Laks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3grBV_SHuXFTJao3Rutge7Qtph1-vhD5tPBYjbJL4SV7esFjt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A modern museum with a childhood twist. They give you sweets at every stop. When we went the entire once was decorated for Halloween. A nice place to enjoy with your kids.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPsDf66iV5GVDHHBkIymuV&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-28"},{"Name":"Maria Manduca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLp8c_vw_Rx8IXWepmHa2TGsRjR-x5UtvczrqYHPDzQrUiybA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We spent a different day. My youngest son enjoyed it the most, especially because there were many other children there and he was able to find some company. We had a good time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Péter Tircsi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVCRIZAxUz_FZObiv4KZppFU21hoNLseK-a-gh8Cb_X1Z9ISo_TyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Omg, it is such a great place to spend an hour or more. Each room is completely different, not only because of the art, but also the experience just to look at them, touch them, feel them, and have different activities, a lot to see and enjoy. They even decorated the whole place with Halloween decoration in October. We enjoyed the sweets as well which we got in the different rooms. The sour one was very sour! :) Highly recommended, don’t forget to wear clothes which have white writing/art on them because there are a lot of rooms where it is glowing in the dark :)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICXhYvSCg&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2024-10-18"},{"Name":"Susan Welsh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLI95JpjyBBVNTLcOjn5b433GELt5Zil0HtBBzWebLQRgjvGA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"A few random rooms with lights and painted walls and some decorations is meant to give feeling you are in space. You are given a total of 4 sweets 1 per room. Go down a slide with a small ball pit. Shop at the exit sells a few sweets and cheap toys. VR game is good but in a separate room after you’ve left. Nothing to do with sweets or space or a museum.
Really disappointing don’t waste your money!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alksjndrs F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKoOWDYxbjIE-rwV5dmL-TzkAaglNsNFj1MjdX8QuGJ54Nfi_E=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Quite a rip-off. Paid 40€ for 2 adults and a three year old. It’s not really a playground more like a wannabe instagram picture place. In everything the kids get sweets. Total tour takes you maybe 20 minutes. We had a group of 20 including kids which was way too many people for some of the rooms.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeCydmfjMefZ3TaCcEAfVN&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-6"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a fantastic building with some wonderful artworks. The ground floor has some student works many of true quality and talent. You descend one floor to see an exhibition of graphic arts from the early 1900s on. You descend to the main exhibition space which has a moving collection of work depiction the trauma of immigration and the victims of this dangerous journey via sea.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAKVHdLqOJeu4cq3_Ew79dC&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"S Bristol","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI0zVxZqdB273yxQV1VinjzNJsA0sZ28LlFSrl4rEY9BlkzSg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An excellent museum in a lively neighborhood with many independent cafés, bars and restaurants. A history of the early Spanish illustrated magazine  ' White and Black ', showing many covers and early cartoons in chronological order from 1901. Varied artistic illustrations showcasing the ' modern life', politics etc. Other exhibitions on more floors. We visited towards the end of the day,  and could have easily spent 2 hours there, plus visiting the cafe. Recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDzmOedbw&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-6-7"},{"Name":"Marc G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK2hJP8h7COFWvZMrwQPcpLDair4JLlEimwAnddDETKQNGIvV5s=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved the Museum and the (free!) guided tour. Wonderful to go from 1890ish all the way to 2000 in history through caricature, illustration and art… don’t miss out on visiting this place! I can confirm that very young & upwards will enjoy this colourful and fun exhibition. Most pieces are driven by action & personality of the subjects, or by the relatable situation of the historic moment they were created in.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDTkKuJOQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-5-21"},{"Name":"Will Finley","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVE9RXbySJxSR7Ize1IBsmiQkteh52ZtMoGsP7LqIwsVeMif9Er=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Not so much a museum as it is a book store. Which by itself is amazing. It is the largest selection of design books I've seen in one place. Very interesting stuff from all over the world with beautiful design. The building is also fantastic. The metal panel facade is striking and the underlit floor modules are really cool looking at night. They gate off the courtyard when it gets dark though. The museum is not exceptionally inspiring, but you should still go up to the second level to walk through the connector.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE_-TNHg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-6-14"},{"Name":"Elisabetta Borzini","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWW9OaJFHYeOlmWeyc9v25DLabeFhCxN7jRlbMj8RkcjNuLAtMf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So, the museum is actually closed but the exhibition on the ground floor is open (and free).
It’s an exhibition about transgender people, trans art and trans artists. I didn’t expect it and I’m so grateful we stumbled open it because it was so heartwarming and marvellous. Thank you for this space!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFjay90wE&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2023-10-30"},{"Name":"Christian Jimenez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJschGurXRAeIT0xo2IXQDdEoBMGg9nvZnG8j56pfgoYf7jg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Free Museum featuring Illustration work as well as some digital work. As of May 17, the top floor was Alfredo González Sánchez, with the bottom two floors being for Ana Juan.

Reader be warned, everything is in Spanish.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID4loD5fQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-5-17"},{"Name":"Shea Gallagher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo2wI3q1e5oC5KoDnAr91sqejKTG3sycFuqlF7lDZ1clIBeIg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's pay what you think (3euros recommended which is well worth it) and features some amazing artists of various late 20th century fashion paintings/ designs and at the minute there is a what I would describe as geometric art on the first floor.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE_dXyMQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2019-3-16"},{"Name":"Mariia Lozben","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWL49abyt_wP-fLEYIyYb8q3sxUdKHVFjw4m1aatuvE2XcV99N8-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great place for inspiration 👍 there are an exhibition of illustrations on -1 floor and Madrid's railway history exhibition on 2 floor. Very interesting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICc1vjNYg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2019-11-27"}]

This is a free museum and well worth the visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB6_0emJFfBwG1nH0EjiHyu&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"Axel Büse","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVHzcEZadA0iHs82-OfQwpRQBo7RCY9ew1ElIM-zNWvCgtaMGk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Several small exhibitions with interesting works. The centre is huge and in the underground cellar video installations were shown.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCOAOn0GmJwZlyeN30Y8fcl&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2025-9-20"},{"Name":"Sal FromCal","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXHmWCUsn3MgcrMooaO3-IG2gQO23Hgvg-mYxKjPX22z6X2qApTxA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Smaller exhibition but worth your time if you enjoy modern art. Not overly impressive though a few exhibits I found interesting. I took my time and ended up spending 2 hours there where the toy exhibit really caught my attention as a former engineer and the art piece made up of rifle stocks.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC1w-2iCA&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2024-1-2"},{"Name":"Aybüke Çoban","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJP0OnSBDD60IdPwXR0buAaLKXGKOvqpv47Jkw3GxtJzLfQVQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing collections, and easy location ❤️","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDC7rphyHDeldsyyiIYvisN&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2026-1-15"},{"Name":"Stephen B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIQprGwl20PsJdSsNNP_mfYTH8b87OJoY1KngZpH8uOQEvKgXb0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Don't expect a grand institution here or a huge collection but what's here is both exciting, beautiful and diverse. Turn left after you enter through security and walk to the far side of the courty to find the galleries which are on the top two levels.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChvICg8gE&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2023-2-7"},{"Name":"Dušan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJxGrDqZWtXb13K1GObmzL7pIQ7YWZbYaZ0Nbk8416b4UI8bg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Exposition was pretty small but nicely composed. I spend there around one hour.

If you want free entry, ask in tourist point when to visit museum for free, they will tell you everything.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChrZP7zgE&fid=0x0:0xad646714b0522e37"],"When":"2023-2-19"},{"Name":"Alvin Yap","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVpUBqzMpqYCmcX3p84S8hkazyznSR52kYyGweSIeMBwcxcxWMN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free entry, lots of amazing pieces to get lost in. [{"Name":"Dong Cai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVDzBsXATZ4chn3Go4FUKsm03lk7jcwsyJ89uTw1eNKA2ebF_c=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The experience in itself is good.  However, given the small size of the venue and things to do, definitely not worth the 68€ for a family of 5.  Everything could be done and seen in literally 15-20 mins.  Definitely felt like a tourist trap for this venue.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Stefan Nilles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcSebPbMGAP-j9hMDMpSkJM2OSZiScSw7Jd2G1MSWm5hmuLuIdjw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not visit this place unless your are a Spanish nationalist family with very young children!

The place is catering exclusively to children to teach them about the embroidery on the torrreros' uniform, the tradition of spanish tiles as an art from and other mundane details of Spanish culture that are presented as something Spanish kids should be proud of.

The glacially paced narrative of the projected information is in Spanish (for 3 year olds) and you can read the English translation projected to the wall.

Total waste of money and time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBxSdMF_EItVZ0NhATVpwun&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Chris Tomkins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMdh9K_6GffzOUidLsxnoeKD-7jw1e7YUvNARYkIb04iAytgI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good set up. Very engaging immersive experience for the artists. The bit at the end was just novel fun.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCHot09waWMZrBDGpS-w-Z3&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-28"},{"Name":"Jonas Montvidas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXykj1DBG41wM8onrJXAQ3usyMsllep8989BjgjWIqriCxo8UQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It is a hoax. You pay 18 euros to get into two rooms - one bigger and one smaller. The same story about The Last Supper by Davinci goes on a loop in the bigger room, and there are two/three activities for the children in the smaller room. Two short hallways with lights are the main attraction of this place. But even these hallways are just a cheap copy of other museums worldwide that offer better experiences. Not worth 18 euros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCoqoaZkAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-4-16"},{"Name":"Shalom N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXjmGFJHDuC19RcGGg0lG7mHAePo2CjbG7fd4Pk9sPEsG8DxAUj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It was cute for the 10 minutes it lasted for, you can tell that this place was made for influencers to come and take a quick video so others will feel the need to come.

It was one main exhibition room where they just projected some stuff on the wall, then another room where you get to view terrible VR and do some coloring, i got pretty pictures i guess but would i recommend? No because its not giving you value for your money

It is giving very much tourist trap because you leave feeling unsatisfied because it can literally take you 5 minutes to go through it all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQ58b0UQ&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-3-7"},{"Name":"Jose Maria Tijerina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUN2vlCdnJoVVzCMvczORJZNy6fp097DcqnE5mvO5JL7UyEJ4mK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The immersive experience is very interesting. When I visited, the experience was about the work of Klimt and Van Gogh. I went on a Monday so it was not full. The extra activities besides the immersive experience are more for kids than adults. There are a  virtual painting wall, last for 3 minutes, drawings for kids and virtual reality headsets that were not working when i attended. The narration on the immersive experience is ominous Spanish language, so if you cannot follow it then you would not get  part of the experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQHraYDrmqxt2VPIpl0y2E&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-13"},{"Name":"Ariannaly Pea Martínez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHlslcBXwCCEYwj-22n12iqaNzJJSfd6xSJOjFHvdkhRKV2mc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It’s a pretty nice and neat experience. Visuals and music were the best of it, although the interactive room was a bit mid imo. Either way I’d sit hours and hours just to enjoy the Spirit of Japan showcase","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC3vuqCmAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2024-11-6"},{"Name":"Doron Privé","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkdtQAXsQ4Kx4kWjv6_tOt_smbLwFWiIFkEVxSgS1lbwIt1iE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went there with two friends and it was very disappointing. The tour last maximum 10min and was very expansive. If you are not a Spanish speaker, it’s just a waste of money because they are projecting a kind of movie during the second part of the tour and the it’s 100% in Spanish.
The museum is a first corridor with lights and mirror on 10 meters (it’s the  only cool thing you will see), after that there’s the Spanish movie projected on walls, ground and ceiling and then it’s finished. I told you, it last 10 minutes and cost ~20€/person.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"Szabó Niki","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV84fSuMdL2CvUMYb8KTIx8o8N_acVCSyv6Jhzs_qi8PGzXEFft=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Too expensive for what it is. There’s only 2 room, and we thought there will be more to see. There was only a few people inside and wasn’t any line at the entrance.
I really liked the horror part though, that was pretty cool.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB_ebNOiQ5DETbdlRgz2VtP&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Elona Mendelovich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXAv8jvh5kGg_s9ACrPeqXHq8uDb5qWUTkl7ZQw69QCYwrK5-qNUw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The place is well-designed and nice with unique themes. A tourist has a little difficulty understanding some of the displays or characters, but I'm sure locals understand everything. Horror movie lovers have an impressive portion of the display.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDEnRRUHgJ1T6epDxqx-1OE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Ray Recce","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVaLUzLvo5cjXZS1ZzCMxys31WGA7W0ybQPm3fN8OEkc5vtYjM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We had a nice visit with family, we specially liked the horror part. A lot of famous people to see and the train was also a nice experience. We bought the tickets online but there was no queue at the museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7jsjMdA&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-15"},{"Name":"Mariangela de Gemmis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXdSMyx0obDpTCrQyAZqsYS8HR7RH0Kbw6FCjqH_fUIMVrn6l0OPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very nice experience, would recommend. In the ticket are included 3 "galleries" to visit, a small fun horror area upstairs and a "train of terror" ride, all inside of that building! I was surprised by how big in reality it is, they planned it very well.

The statues are incredibly detailed but some of them feel cramped and out of place? (Like Leonardo Di Caprio's statue in a modern suit in the Western area). But overall I loved the museum!

They also have an AR experience on their website which you can activate through the QR codes! They give additional information about the characters in display and activities that make you temporarely unlock filters for instagram and tiktok, it's very cute. Only flaw is that the website was very slow and because of that it took a lot of time for me to visit the exposition... almost 3 hrs! But you will take way less if you speed up or ignore the QR codes I guess. Your choice!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDbr93UBQ&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-10"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cera in Madrid is a fun and entertaining place for people of all ages. The museum showcases a wide variety of wax figures, including both Spanish and international celebrities, famous athletes, and historical personalities. The dedicated horror section adds a unique touch of excitement. Staff are helpful, and the experience is well organized, with easy access and no long queues. If you enjoy exploring cultural attractions or taking creative photos, this museum is an enjoyable stop during your visit to Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cherry Blossom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzTc_9PiI119Xy0q0bT8F1EkxZ5bBoeD_190WYbA3Wn9pk_BJ_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Only some of the attendants are helpful, the others would just stare at you instead of showing you the way to go (not marked otherwise).
The museum has got a majority of Spanish personalities/ celebrities. It's got a dedicated horror upstairs, although it all feels a bit creepy. 😄","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chryso Konstantinou","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVak3LGZKu-DY7RPzeR_-fFfSuk4zZ5ICrZySNLg5dSR4y4d1cZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"We visited the Wax Museum and were very disappointed with the top floor experience. It is supposed to be entertaining and fun, not something that scares visitors away. The staff member upstairs yesterday really frightened us — he chased us almost to the very end. I was with my 7-year-old son, and in the end we had to turn back and leave because we were both so terrified that it was no longer enjoyable.

There should be limits to this kind of performance. It’s fine to create a spooky atmosphere, but not to pursue visitors all the way through. That only ruins the experience and achieves the opposite of what it should.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Johnny Rodriguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWiul4NlavDOHRZ5rs97Ih05Ja0sL6bIWBFTlxJECvl0fKzmRGx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my recent visit to the wax museum in Madrid, I had the pleasure of being guided by Yini, an outstanding tour guide who truly enhanced my experience. Yini's knowledge and enthusiasm brought each wax figure to life, making the tour both educational and entertaining. Her engaging storytelling and passion for the museum's exhibits made the visit truly memorable. I highly recommend taking a tour with Yini for a fantastic and insightful journey through the world of wax figures. Thank you, Yini, for your wonderful guidance and for making my visit to the museum so special!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr_YfezAE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jaspal Singh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXKoK6lQQKruIpwS2QBDpwAj__9wnNd6RsyiWPI2xdMIy04VT-H=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"February 2026 5*

Visited the museum on Friday evening. I spent 3 hours and could not finish it half of it. This place is very amazing and beautiful collection. I really want to also shout out for staff who are excellent. I bought ticket online from GetYourGuide but did not get it. The staff issued me ticket on the spot.

There is big art collection from different era. There are 3 floors and covering different segments of different centuries. It is so interesting to see the collection. You can get audio tour from the museum.

One of the floor is closed which is covering Arabic art. You can see Hispanic and Roman arts.

The place is fully accessible and will need good time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAn_Df4pAfgFNOFyHtdqjEX&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-21"},{"Name":"Sophie Macfarland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkDSyC73O6U7wn67SRhdMV7tLfHakEHRayagFeeQe5pM9JzA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was a great stop!

The ticket is very cheap and the museum walks through Spanish history (starting with pre-history). The collection, while good, is not good enough to warrant a long visit. However, the signs and curation are phenomenal (English signage is included). The outline and explanation of history was great and I learned a ton. This was a perfect foundation for the rest of my trip to Spain, bouncing between Phoenician, Roman, Visigoithic, Islamic, and Catholic sites.

In particular the maps were amazing and I loved the late Roman mosaics.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA-lfZOKY30aOAJGmJQApKR&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-9"},{"Name":"Michael Offord","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCYcnQ9p_fF7bpz5p0dlxJtJ-6yRZkgizWzoZieS9pO2nu0Dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a quiet but very good museum detailing the early history of Spain, from pre-history to the Romans. There is also an excellent permanent exhibit on Egypt.

Some carvings are top quality. This is not the same size as the British Museum or the Louvre, but is very visitable and a notable collection of Roman emperor busts, though unlike many other former Roman provinces, Spain has not centralised their Roman archaeology, and much of it remains in regional museums.

Very easy to get in with no queue and extremely reasonably priced. Also modern facilities.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBDVxuKkhh1jH8Zit6CxnO-&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Chiahsun Liao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV4TDJ6SlyonvuEbjhTra3qvN1QpGzvmehP9hkS97J4t9BvqKFH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must-visit museum for history enthusiasts. The collection is highly informative and well presented throughout, covering major human civilizations across the historical timeline with impressive depth. It was surprising to see how lightly visited it was, given the world class quality of the exhibits. It is one of the few museums in Madrid that provides thorough and professional English explanations for every exhibit. The museum also offers very convenient amenities, including spacious restrooms on every floor and a self-help cloakroom that can fit in carry-on sized luggage, which can be a huge bonus for tourists. You can easily lose yourself in the river of history and spend an entire day exploring. A true hidden gem and, in my opinion, the best museum in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD8emR2DIgqvJHvvDaWlXcv&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Daria Khenkina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNRdxiUGq7jBgczsAL2sQjH8oqhoDG11AET9kNczYnHhWopYPx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best museums I've been to. Rich and diverse collection, very beautiful artifacts. Definitely recommend. Spent here about 2 hours,   but would prefer to spend more","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDsJm2IPD_TBFrx2jpnu8I2&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-14"},{"Name":"Bob Meadow","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJU2pLYy4QH7AEa64d-1yxozoqEo9N-RU-m9HK-wQ0knCCn3g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located next to the National Library, the National Museum of Archeology offers an amazing journey through the history of Spain from the Paleolithic to the 19th century. The collection includes artifacts dating back to prehistoric times, the Iberian peoples, Roman Hispania, the Visigothic kingdom, Al-Andalus, the Middle Ages, the Modern era and so on. On the other hand, the collection of the ancient Greek and Egyptian civilizations located on the second floor, is really enjoyable to explore thanks to the quality and quantity of the pieces. And all this for just 3€. Don't miss it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDbzyA8HM2TEYP5Hgye9jbx&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-18"},{"Name":"Elaine Huang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLbBOdrqJ3ChpJY9ADZrIwE3kyztaS4mN7ayUXI_HJtKjf7PA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I participated a guided tour and Sophia gave me a fantastic experience. She shared a lot of information and unknown stories beyond audio guide (the app is really helpful as well). This museum gives me a good reason to come back to Madrid and I would love to stay in the museum whole day by then ~","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBvp-6TJ5VCxKHIGZM1JcP1&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-17"},{"Name":"Sandra Gregan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAzKIkf4GjI17qG_PAe8kJGNxVGaF6ZI_6fNjp_9a6xaxwo508=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of my favorite experiences while in Madrid. Well thought out floor plan and displays. Easy to navigate and amazing exploration of history from the beginning of time. We purchased tickets online prior to entering. Very easy process and only €3/person which was a steal, I would have easily paid more. Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBQCFNJfjVT1IZuzPeu8Ejg&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-16"}]

Me ha gustado cómo está diseñado, la distribución de las salas por temas y las explicaciones de los paneles informativos. No es muy grande pero es muy completo. Ideal para pasar con niños un rato didáctico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manuel Gutierrez Hernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBhNgNShCgkXUUDaMsWqDfvucTT-e5pjyDFfDEBzL13Aw_47pu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy interesante, el staff muy amable, pequeño pero merece la pena","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhATsOKMdqiOvYznF-9XpxuY&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-7"},{"Name":"Lucía Fernández","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWo-7rJUvFax_U_UfEomhs-QAw3KDdofD-gwXZ6CPawE0ORnTuGVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El museo de Leonardo Da Vinci está muy bien, sobre todo cuando te lo explican. Las dos personas que nos hicieron la visita lo hicieron genial y así se disfruta mucho más.

Hay maquetas de máquinas e inventos en sus cuadernos de madera hechas a partir de los dibujos de Leonardo y también reproducciones de la Mona Lisa y La Última Cena.

Echo de menos que haya más pantallas o elementos interactivos para entender mejor cómo funcionan las máquinas. Aun así, la visita merece la pena.

Por otro lado pensábamos con tanto vinilo que estaban en obras, porque parecía que estaba cerrado y al abrir la puerta no vimos la taquilla.
Hay descuentos para niños y mayores.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6JgZvhlyijm5ZQpihWU4U&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-6"}]

Our guide was so fun and what's there is lovely. You never know what's just beneath the surface!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manuel Gonzalez Vazquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7DEtHZo8-9cH9OphhBsTFwF_pw91tcJyp1d4OGjmYWJ_mo1KK2w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de los Caños del Peral is inside Madrid's Ópera metro station, and has in display the archaeological remnants of the 16th and 17th-century Caños del Peral fountain and Arenal sewer.
This site offers a historical view of Madrid's water distribution and collection and the evolution of the city. It is free to metro users and offers guided tours.
Although rather small, due to bad management by historical institutions in the past that allowed the destruction of a big part after the discovery, the explanation and guided tour from the team in charge is outstanding.
Free entrance (Just need to pay the regular subway entrance to access the location), but you need to book a visit in the museum web page","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Melinda Merola","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVFngu06ulrW8u0ebmP25yp3ZtA_2hDyVGjpV0tNJZcO5Eqrw4O=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I wasn't sure what to expect from this museum that I'd passed so many times in the Opera metro station. As someone interested in medieval history of Madrid I loved it! It's very small but with original artifacts. The guide, Javier, went above and beyond to explain everything. It's also free, you just have to sign up in advance.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Victoria Nuflo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJYvduo7XewH5lolNq99cBCNiccuVcJr-2SmBVotLSieol4Fw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We were SO fascinated by this tour! [{"Name":"Daniel Lobo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWP7EtNH8nWsJ_7-rcmcycS2S2SFrJbzMn-Q7X9OwsbXNcNwMlS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fun historic visit to the decommissioned Chamberí Metro station. Wonderful care has been taken to offer the opportunity to access the old station. The free visit is somewhat brief but insightful. The first part is guided by knowledgeable staff, which adds robust historic context. And it ends with some time to linger and photograph.

Lifetime iconic as it can be seen from the passing trains of Metro Line 1, it was fun to visit after so many years and enjoy how well preserved it is. Reservations are best to ensure timed access with one of the small tour groups. But folks without a ticket can also wait in an open queue at the street entrance. And if the group is not full they are invited to join the slot.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDZnC13iSo3BOd_38wbjPSR&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Raluca Chirita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI520GJb2nosTLbyFObR6VpPrEVUChv4XKd7CDwcMIdrGFZkw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The station is in really great shape and is really interesting to see how metro stations looked in the beginning. The museum is free of charge and there is a free guide, but it is only in spanish. You cannot visit the station on your own and you have to wait for the tour guide. If you don't speak Spanish is quite boring to wait for the tour guide to speak. There is a qr code at the beginning with a brief information guide in English, but I feel like it did not contain everything that tour guide explained in person.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD44tZlXYHq7jGJf4sjj9Z4&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-11-29"},{"Name":"Rinor Zejna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUvvIekDoiakC0lcbOFvJQGN4mjdoSd_J6yW7G_q8jDxJDZekMP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was one of the best places to visit in Madrid, especially that is free and with a guide that tells you the history of the Metro (you need to speak and understand Spanish tho). But it is an amazing experience to try, make sure to reserve the spot in advance as there are only a small number of groups that can enter, although you may enter without a reservation but it is risky and the waiting time can be a lot.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDQb2_vTQ-T1qoP2HUrxwr9&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-11-6"},{"Name":"TravelingDave","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVPCrljDYhlOATfCbyYZ9A7VQbXiacTlndePDddXXJdRhME57CL=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Old station preserved. Highly recommend a visit. Only complaint is I wish it was opened more days.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD2cQnA3b5ms8af-bEwihpt&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Ruben Perfetto","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU2uggQ_5xKUD5-hkwA03izN3qimvpvvIWnQlkHkl1U6fgnf4Kcxg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Excellent guide tour, full of history and incredible work for the 1900th.
Free admission, last week of the month you can reserve your spot, what it is highly recommended. They reserved 25 spot and tree for walk ins.
Tour run from 4:00pm every 1/2 and hour.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEKnSrJLHyLSo7wE&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-5-24"},{"Name":"Ivo Driesser","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIciPniOzcbLK9IFEiJhdmfNrwnHpZemYdTRmQ68X9Aq62_JQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Nice opportunity to travel in time.
It’s a pity that there wasn’t an English tour. Nevertheless they offer information in PDF format in English.
Check online to reserve your tickets.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBVyiiYrgDC88WX2WxyF4Qi&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-10-20"},{"Name":"Navnish N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXefl5E1EmAwE-upSkIAStxun_gBvfSrsyE0-DGSA75Wn6aViCC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very unique experience. The guide speaks in Spanish but he does ask if anyone needs information in English. It was a good experience to see something which is preserved well. Book your tickets in advance we booked ours 2 weeks prior. A must visit if you are in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICZtuuH_QE&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2023-9-10"},{"Name":"Nina Liu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9hmUHjjBS1nrNNkzP024qy_M5YiDBJIbKFV4VaqoNOes6O0Fo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I made a reservation online beforehand (Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays) but I think you can also go there and wait in line to see if there are free spots for the upcoming tour. The whole tour - only in Spanish, lasted around 40 mins or so and you can have some time afterwards for photo taking. I liked it as it’s like going back in times and you can see still metros passing by the station!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAil-SnGuBoU4fZfyoT8t00&fid=0x0:0xc68f8606de845e2d"],"When":"2025-6-25"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Sidónio Sousa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkV_4xUfxZJNhW1rio2RuLNJ7VwEDjaWuJlHV3nHya9uXTSmw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"WOW! The interior is just magnific! A gem itself. Then you have a huge collection of so beautiful minerals and rocks. Not many tourists, more students so i really enjoyed the time here.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDQZd1Q4E5ra-XM9Cw7ndni&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Nay Nay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJQCUewEak1WfsulkdX5J5auXOGaksWgBmPLrQWigfapxhpOQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The building is spectacular! I love the way they have the mineral set up very easy to go through. They have some beautiful local minerals as well as international minerals. You need a few hours to really enjoy it. Highly recommend for children. It’s a great learning experience into the world of geology.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCKywUvmGEprbH7wc1UrAAF&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-8-24"},{"Name":"Tundra Caoduro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVI2Rm51a38V1f4cEnxrpoBjFpDziiLKdc5NnRN64BBZrhfnw8T=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free museum, really well kept and the collection is HUGE.
I love minerals and whenever I visit a new city I always go to Natural Science museums, because usually there are none dedicated only to minerals.
On the ground floor you can find fossils, minerals and rocks. 1rst floor is dedicated to fossils and the second floor displays minerals and rocks found in Spain, divided by each region.
The thing that I appreciated the most was that each mineral had it's own chemical composition written on the label, which was really cool and I've never found it before at any other museum I've visited!!
If minerals are your passion or you have a few hours to spare, you should definitely pay a visit!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAvmG6F7HzQ3IZaO0kMq2fs&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-8-26"},{"Name":"John Chan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKpohnOR1hE_wIXJZG3Ya9rbeQxjwThGDIa-38jsFRg_daS0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Would give it 10 star this place is amazing! So much information and so many gems and fossils to see! And it was free!!! Don’t forget to bring your passport to enter.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC3jpIwe8k1ifZJRK7cXnVt&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-10-3"},{"Name":"Alexandra Dejanovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXH99b1546rvNCbgmNKFiM9BFjVK09XRBF_MD8gVEGaAodbUg4qEQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The museum is free, but you need to show your ID at the entrance. No reservation needed. The collection of minerals is impressive! The building itself is beautiful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBPhPPZWEO5MBYbVoxHWFBS&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-11-19"},{"Name":"Stephanie C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2JxsBQxBaL1s9YhQjw9gvuremUuIkgGLeibn5hNIRwz7kkMbm=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This museum was an absolute gem (pun intended). There were thousands of fossils, rocks, and bones to marvel at spread out over several floors that were accessible via spiral staircases. The space itself was stunning, with a large open ground floor (that had a mastodon skull on display!), glass sunroof, and exhibits around the periphery. The descriptions were almost all in Spanish, but even without the descriptions, the items on display were impressive. Definitely a must-see if you're in the area.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCI5svuZA&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-4-1"},{"Name":"Will Gayle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV6RQl5zC19Zbiw5cAHGko5yk7r9GI5TwXDRmwSPrbhg_3Fbi0o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Gem of a place to visit. It rocked.

The workshop for kids (in Spanish) is great and the building is beautiful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAZxzNhvC266-Vc5p7T1PGD&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Jim McJim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVx1SVrTzDK2VplleC5LqOhHnk6_nFtRO6kAW8Q8YFF_czS7QGm=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow. Just wow.

We are hobbyist field collectors from UK and generally find mineral museums to be a bit of a jumbled mess, or missing the high quality systematic/locality based approach.

This museum was exceptional, great specimens of both fossils and minerals, lots of info and was free to enter. We spent a couple of hours and I could have spent more, but we were only visiting Madrid for the day.

Was great to see some specimens from our home turf, and one from a locality I have visited in the UK

Only gripe was the majority was not translated, but we can not expect this as visitors to another country.

Would highly recommend visiting!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICdydaD7wE&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2024-2-16"}]
