[{"Name":"Madeleine Dieleman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVFk4NupipoXLc_9SNsxAKpEIJSwWCFtnLYo0zADDh-j2O5HF_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is the spot if you like modern art or ceramics and old gentlemen house interior. It’s a perfect thing to do if you want a mix of history and new conceptual art. There was also a good collection of Bauhaus and some famous names likes Behrens, rietveld, etc. Dont miss this if you like musea and interior","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA4vnc7EHYTjbwR68oPe9Vs&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2026-1-23"},{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is another of Madrid‘s fabulous museums. Set in over 60 rooms and several floors this museum, displays paintings, pottery, glassware, silverware furniture, and other items found in well to do family homes. Though the 17th and 18th centuries are featured on the ground floor there are temporary exhibitions, which include Contemporary works. Such a wonderful place and free entry. It’s highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA_wwQhTlcwVWpc6N4_5qkw&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-9-25"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The pieces they have are great, but there are very few labels or descriptions of what the items are. For example, there are drawers in the textile room that you can open with beautiful pieces of textiles -- and not a single label. There's a small room with jewelry and other items, mostly clerical items, again, not a single label. The only things that are labeled are the larger pieces in glass cases, but even those are lacking in information. The temporary exhibits are usually much better explained. I really want to love this museum, as they do have wonderful pieces, but it appears that it is mostly designed for guided tours.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDSMMlHLLlq7sS-lX-2nRe&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"NYS Counselor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLwuzsNPG9_q0me8YL3lQpOMkuA9iChayY37Fr-rJdc4F3Jcw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The National Museum of Decorative Arts is a trip back in time, not only for the content of the displays, but for the way in which it is setup, which sometimes sadly reflects museum practice of an earlier era and no doubt a lack of funding to support upgrading the installations. While this is definitely not a museum designed for busloads of tourists, for those who love the decorative arts and have some knowledge of their history it is well worth a visit, particularly as it is very conveniently located only a short walk from the painting galleries of the famed Prado Museum.
We were there on a warm day which emphasized that the museum lacks air conditioning, presenting a hazard not only for the objects, but for staff and visitors as well. We were originally told that high temperatures did not allow opening the top floor, then, while were on the floor below, a staff member came over and graciously informed us there the top floor had been reopened on a very temporary basis. Fortunately, we moved with dispatch, as we had barely completed a circuit of the exhibitions upstairs before the heat forced it to be shut down again.
There are many fascinating pieces, some in vitrines, some in room-like displays, about which we would very much have liked to know more, as labels were minimal where they existed and largely of course in Spanish. Several fascinating pieces of furniture deserved far greater documentation, as did excellent displays of ceramics representing many centuries of Spanish skill and creativity and reflecting multiple different cultural influences. Noteworthy was a fine special exhibition devoted to glass, as well as a standing display of an historic kitchen featuring beautiful tile-work.
Although there were a handful of books on display in a case at the admissions desk, there is no gift shop and it isn't possible to preview their publications to determine if they may be of interest for purchase. In addition to historical displays, the Museum features changing special exhibitions, including while we were there one on fashion photography! Despite its limitations as a building, if time allows we will try to return on a cooler day to better explore the displays. There are certainly many fine works here that can't be found elsewhere, deserve more time, and will repay the attention of careful viewers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCk6SYwZQS0c9NrejYsJFBz&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Maria Deschamps","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVsIezNK7zPsJnkjEI5S3rRD5EheuCShZN3_AZMI-uAuo_w-rXZxw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic museum for interior or furniture designers - affordable entrance fee, easy to get around and good size.  Great selection of artifacts.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAFk7uibE2BctCM71UvAGnI&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-9-18"},{"Name":"Slex","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWbct4Aw-rcigEXdVAdlVF8WX_LBRo5joBinKWc1okbNC59YCsR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really adored this museum! I love baroque, rococo, and romantic era furniture so this place was a dream for me. They also had a temporary Versace fashion exhibit that was very fun to walk through. The best part? For whatever reason, the day we went it was free! (And it was a Saturday!)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICX65Ke8QE&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2024-10-20"},{"Name":"Itay Manor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLm3lTIQwxXHsHsVtGHc45jPJyC9jww5b-ehfTAohoE_zvJ0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A beautiful hidden museum! Located in a central location in the city! The entrance fee is ridiculous, the staff is so friendly and helpful! Inside the museum there is a great collection of arts & crafts from the early 20th century, combined with contemporary art! A recommended visit for design and aesthetics enthusiasts!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDI8brlmwE&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-4-10"},{"Name":"Liyi Z","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX03rtkx5M-0EbDuTkg4U7jHV7uqxUissG-GYmKapa9llLf964Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Tickets are cheap but they are free Thursdays 5-8 pm and Saturdays 2-3 pm. Be aware that exhibits are in Spanish or don't have much info. They also have some temporary exhibition space. I liked that the museum covered a good range of decorative arts from home goods to a whole replica kitchen or dining room. There's a Frank Lloyd Wright window (and chair), which is a shame to see not with light shining through it but a pleasant surprise anyway.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDI2aj1CA&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-4-11"}]

Beautifully presented pieces from all over Latin America in an extensive exhibition.\nI was warned that part of the exhibit is closed. Didn't feel like I missed much. And at 3 Euro entry not sure what others are complaining about. Wanted 60 cent back as a 20% discount?!\nSide note, had the best Porras con chocolate at a cart outside the hospital, 5 min up the hill.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBsp6cZ2M-5mrAHUqR8WJYs\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-9-7"},{"Name":"Daicai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHPWMWbA4YDyrOUIBiYgHsSryQ27wqTIK3_XUbNiI6ZuMDePaA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The museum is big so there are a lot of people allowed to visit, not too many people because admission is free on Sundays, I like the sofas inside because I would not feel good if I see too many spooky exhibits inside. Many exhibits are full of negative energy, so the pavilion puts a lot of sacred paintings or holy objects to check and balance them. Similar situations are more common in museums, so you can just appreciate their appearance. The staff didn't look too energetic guess it had something to do with the exhibits having too much energy. Very special museum and well worth exploring for those with strong energy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCoofqmTQ\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-4-17"},{"Name":"Neil Pearson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLoBoBg3nFw8kzBFVNuPr-Ns0V8K716E9E6tVoYbSh9cKnYjg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Avoid. Very grumpy staff who will start strongly warning you about closing 30 mins before closing.  15 minutes before they will start physically ushering you out.  Lots of friendlier places to go in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB0FFpn8At6dpBED4h5-Pn7\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-10-25"},{"Name":"Lissa Wolfe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMvYGaVYe9nQSkSToJer4qx3iRKXYYTycNR1svTzUW9NXtPLw7=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Good mix of pre and post colonial art. All signage in español.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDU4PE6le070ifTe-6Dhxk2\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2026-1-15"},{"Name":"Ioannes Paulus","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIbCPap65h4lqq34JTFIJ4n-pRF3uX4bVSTQNTvIlMnKjoqPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The place to see indian artifacts in Madrid, not a huge collection but an interesting one nonetheless. Worth seeing even if only for one of the three remaining mayan codices.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB6uEvDsdbcLQitsODypqJC\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-9-8"},{"Name":"Debbi Thornhill","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVyXY-1F9wWOvFxtNynr0JSkbJ6fjqbEC__tTokZYmx88r-g95RLg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Most beautiful museum I've ever been to","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAe4L6BMOs_cNeW_yEszhdf\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-11-24"},{"Name":"Guillermo Jose Aguirre Selvetti","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXZvDc7w7v7HGAl8KAtaupH5Gx9e14et398-CthFFTin5Qsv81t=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Beautiful museum with a great collection of pieces of art from prehispanic American cultures. Easy to get to, and quite cheap. The Quimbayas treasures are amazing, but also the Madrid Codex, also known as the Tro-Cortesianus Codex, a significant and unique pre-Columbian Maya manuscript. The only problem is that the display is old fashioned and it turns the exhibition a bit boring. It desperately needs a new way to display the pieces of art to make it more engaging and interesting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDYRTy6Q-yz71aDDRcU31ng\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Eduard Rudyk","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXF37PlPjeUpRE5RfujIYFS7tbfN8QeZyMujrX-AMRdJgol3gnI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing museum! Great collection! Really enjoyed visiting. Have several rare artifacts from pre-Columbian and later periods both from South and North America. For example, Borgia codex. We were during the weekend and it wasn't crowded. Unfortunately the museum closed at 3pm. Very recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7kq-3Bw\u0026fid=0x0:0xbf952a474d8e0423"],"When":"2024-8-14"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"vicetravellers","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqff5QMzzqdnjeGHbPvbc5EDj2433svwF5jcMJTM7qHU5ZLWM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Thyssen-Bornemisza National Museum is an exceptional museum that completes Madrid’s art triangle beautifully. The collection is incredibly well curated, offering a smooth journey through different periods, styles, and artistic movements without feeling overwhelming.

What makes the Thyssen stand out is its balance. From old masters to impressionism and modern works, everything is displayed in a way that feels coherent, calm, and engaging. It is a museum where you can truly take your time and enjoy the art rather than rush through it.

An essential visit in Madrid for anyone interested in painting, culture, and thoughtful curation. Elegant, accessible, and genuinely inspiring.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBdWidapaCnYGeYfb_ZWDy4&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-29"},{"Name":"Juliana C M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXYDWugloZlh79bbKifo16IVCS1CM67BsOBsP__7PPYAZBofac=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing museum that covers almost 700 years of paintings. If you go on Mondays entrance is free from 12:00 till 16:00.  The museum is huge and I would recommend going in different days if you have time. I will add some painting photos :) I used AI to stroll around and experience hand picked paintings and worked really well.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhANYkkmiZmYShE9LLYAzsEs&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"KP G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2Rg9DiQ29ajhUkI-XwqOIh23_chg3Bw9nylChO9JBI-09zvil=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fabulous collection! If you like impressionist and expressionist art, you’d likely enjoy this museum a lot.

Inclusive design, in the sense of having displays for touching, with braille and audio descriptions, for low/no vision visitors. I haven’t seen this attentive detail in any other museum. Bravo to the curators.

Website says Mondays are free. Another great feature of this museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAvf1BsJeyyrMDNaQwCaNCt&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-1-21"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum offers a very complete and enjoyable experience, both in terms of its collection and its overall atmosphere. The variety of artworks, ranging from classical pieces to modern and contemporary art, keeps the visit engaging without feeling overwhelming. The spaces are calm, well lit and easy to move through, allowing you to take your time and really connect with the works at your own pace.
A major highlight of the museum is the staff, who are genuinely excellent. In contrast to the Prado, the employees here are friendly, approachable and clearly willing to help, and they speak English comfortably, which makes a big difference for international visitors. Whether you need directions, information or simple assistance, the interaction feels relaxed and human rather than rushed or strict.
Another strong advantage is that photography is allowed inside the museum. This adds a lot to the overall experience, as you can capture the artworks that stand out to you and engage with them more personally. Compared to the Prado, where this is not permitted, it makes the visit feel more open and memorable. Overall, the Thyssen-Bornemisza combines quality, comfort and excellent service, making it a museum that feels both impressive and genuinely welcoming.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSdUxpp7tdukVt8c0Iwv9P&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"L BM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVkO6B8IbOnTAt2fcyX0LrY9llf-tZHiPSEaVTMZn9T1FP_WwnS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Beautiful collection! Had a wonderful time admiring the incredible artwork.
An extremely rude tour guide for the museum decided to stand in front of a Caravaggio for nearly 10 minutes as the room filled up with people waiting to see it. She wouldn't move, kept yapping. Even the people in her group realized they were in the way. Eventually someone gently approached her & asked if she could please move aside so that all the rest of the people waiting could view the popular Caravaggio. Her response was that she was with the museum & "there are 800 other works of art you can go look at". I don't care who you work for or how much art there is in the museum, the art is for all of us. Don't be rude.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAqp28PB5JQd5JghpHNSNju&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-11-17"},{"Name":"Dana Kay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVRATWmxn5F6aCV3p5nFeIAGUrPqu7BA-nlAp1x2cUG0B9GB8Bl=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The museum offers an impressive art collection spanning from the early Renaissance to contemporary works. It’s divided into two sections: the permanent exhibition on the first and second floors, and a loaned collection on the ground floor. The galleries are organized by artistic style, so similar works are displayed together, making it easy to appreciate the evolution of each movement. During my visit, I was fortunate to catch two exceptional pairings of exhibitions—Picasso with Klee, and Andy Warhol with Jackson Pollock. It was a rare treat to see these artists presented side by side.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD1xB8M03zFpuGHASrpQF1V&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"In the pocket Drum studio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIXESsegfBQB9BUlhcmYM2BYMAjmIaoxM6W7gpgRocovBrsDw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"totally more than i've expected!
there are not only classical paintings but also lots of pivotal modern painter's master pieces
really impressed by the collections..
stayed 3 hours for whole exibitions💕👍🏼","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmL4cz-ROreUe_82K3eIJh&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-2-16"},{"Name":"IAN Zhang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUy_zjCVruENW4GrUB8GggcDbeLevjim5b_QLMkM69oxp_5FS_h=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One ticket costs €14. There are many exhibits, and you’ll need to spend an entire afternoon slowly appreciating them. I also noticed a subtle Republic of China (Taiwan) flag.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAnZ4kJ3TPRfrCAYlixUPmB&fid=0x0:0x5a934a7882cb528f"],"When":"2026-2-21"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"James Brazier","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXExyHDqgeVtyTfRwdYDNjhh92rDitOTYOTqH7gNmxYJi_DMmB9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The collection is very diverse: paintings, sculptures, jewelry, weapons, decorative arts, and rare books. You can see works by Goya, El Greco, and many European artists.

The Leonardo da Vinci painting is worth a look. The Adolescent Saviour is claimed to be an original by the great man however perhaps it was a pupil from his workshop. Either way it’s has to be considered a Leonardesque.

Overall, it’s a peaceful, high-quality museum with lots of interesting objects.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhALZc-cMUzDXPXX-dK08vO6&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-11-30"},{"Name":"eh m","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDc4S4npu6fVJbaYEi1ib1lumKNzCIEZg_ypimQordOgj_nw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have visited the Museo Lázaro Galdiano twice, and it continues to impress me each time. The museum offers an excellent price-to-value experience, given the extraordinary range of works on display. The beautifully designed rooms and the architecture of the building alone are worth a visit.

It is truly remarkable what José Lázaro Galdiano achieved in his lifetime and the exceptional collection he assembled. If you are in Madrid, it is without doubt a must-see.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD59cElIrKw5ykWjnB0Raw-&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"Linda Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV13-ZLd5E8gbX7oVINRLkobXoW7aOrzbAdXFra4V8v2e0Q96I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Incredible museum with the collection of José Lázaro Galdiano. It is a varied and amazing collection from Goya's to swords, Bosch's to stunning ceilings, medieval art to an El Greco that took our breath away. We spent 2 hours here and could have stayed half a day.

There are lockers before ticket booth but they seemed not so secure to us.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCqp7OhhcM2qVuu-pdR4iWX&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-9-27"},{"Name":"Song","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhvoqfI6DYGerqYUa0bfmANUUJ7mMuHOF55sTOJDSNa_D1MQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic collection in a stunningly beautiful building. I am glad I didn’t miss this hidden treasure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBT85E3GPBi34tpXYdwOZar&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2026-2-4"},{"Name":"Anton N.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI4TJjJDuMPiDu-_95c8DFbSUKPJ_ercGHl_xr2ZKQp1F_CYA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I absolutely loved visiting the Lazaro Galdiano Museum! The atmosphere is truly authentic, allowing me to admire the exhibits up close without the hassle of crowded tourist areas. Each piece is beautifully presented, making the experience both relaxing and inspiring. The elegant interiors and friendly staff add to the charm, creating a perfect setting for art enthusiasts and casual visitors alike. Highly recommend for a peaceful and immersive cultural experience!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC_6MWTuQE&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-1-12"},{"Name":"Juliana C M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXYDWugloZlh79bbKifo16IVCS1CM67BsOBsP__7PPYAZBofac=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Well put together Museum with beautiful collection of art and objects. The palace is a piece of art by itself. There you will find dem medals, pottery from more then 2500 years ago to beautiful paintings from the XX century.  Note that the museum offers more then a visit but its agenda offers music, dance and poetry in the evenings.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCA7eqhqAE&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-2-3"},{"Name":"Robin Gadomski","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXPFpA3U8-ZNOJ94Ek-GmhSsuzPWPMZeKgjRqEiNRJi3B056XzU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A fantastic little museum with wonderful artifacts and fine works of art from great masters. Quite hidden and not the most obvious pick for a day trip, I highly recommend visiting this museum if you have a few hours to spare in Madrid. Not too busy, and definitely worth it if you are in the area.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDr-9PrggE&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2024-7-20"},{"Name":"Nikos Parastatidis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUnS1-zp1TI8IMsHpYLlnZR3bKBlTsuIQ1X-s_RKwPIaEFk-0mR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Small museum with an interesting collection of art and artefacts. Some masterpieces stand out but the whole collection is interesting. It took me an hour to wander through its rooms. The building itself is outstanding covered with wonderful frescoes full of colour.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICjgNWi4wE&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2024-4-16"}]

[{"Name":"Kelly Sidney","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVuUqfKbkpL015_LwC-awHUYq0K5---lp_AbgGyRQ5gkxchN7hR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a beautiful collection of Picasso, Salvator Dali, and Joan Miró pieces… amongst several other talented artists ! I preferred this museum significantly over the Prado; it had much better organization and ease to find pieces. The Guernica in person was magnificent. It is a very large museum which you can spend at least 2-3 hours inside. If you are on a time crunch, the temporary contemporary exhibitions were pleasant as well but less a standout compared to the permanent collection.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhALWZzEXq-0QAfRjRAOzQ64&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-6"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Reina Sofía is one of those museums that leaves a strong impression even if you’re not particularly into modern art. The space itself is beautiful, with plenty of natural light and a calm flow that makes the visit feel relaxed rather than tiring, even if you stay for a while. The mix between the historic building and the more contemporary elements works really well and fits the character of the exhibitions.
The main highlight is of course Picasso’s Guernica, which feels completely different in person and has a kind of intensity that makes you stop and take your time with it. Beyond that, the collection as a whole is quite engaging, with some works that make you think and others that you simply enjoy visually, without feeling the need to overanalyze them.
Overall, it’s definitely a museum worth visiting if you’re in Madrid. It doesn’t feel boring or cold, and you leave with the sense that you’ve experienced something meaningful, even if you don’t remember every artist or movement afterward. It’s also great for a more laid-back visit, without the pressure of having to see absolutely everything.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDqj6phukx3ZgfKwfFS17Oq&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Tata Kutaladze","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK0DVJn4bsWYsDSJlAwlQE2Cku3Sdvyo6gUgL7mt6PQUegQAg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My main reason for visiting the Reina Sofía Museum was Picasso and his Guernica, and it was absolutely worth it. Seeing Guernica in person is a powerful and emotional experience — the scale, details, and meaning are far more intense than in any book or photo.
Dali and Miro are  impressive as well.
A practical tip: buy your ticket online. The entrance line is much shorter compared to waiting in front of the ticket office, which saves a lot of time. Overall, a must-visit museum in Madrid, especially for anyone interested in modern art and Picasso’s legacy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDA4ckpcuwpD0Hvb1bUJ9Ky&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"I Berezner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVpuR88LJbO2mGM0lgRoSSgPom1qNaDTbZc_75JhdnFpfi3w3af7A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía stands out as Madrid's premier modern art destination, housing an exceptional collection that spans surrealism to abstract expressionism. Picasso's monumental Guernica commands the spotlight, its raw power amplified by surrounding works that contextualize the Spanish Civil War's devastation. Dalí's melting clocks, Miró's playful abstractions, and international gems like Rothko create a dynamic narrative of 20th-century creativity and turmoil. The renovated hospital building blends airy glass extensions with historic stone, offering light-filled galleries and serene courtyards perfect for reflection. Navigation is intuitive, though the sheer scale rewards a focused itinerary—prioritize floors 2 and 4 for highlights. Temporary exhibits often steal the show, adding fresh energy. Despite occasional crowds around Guernica, the museum's thoughtful spacing ensures an immersive experience. Reina Sofía isn't just art—it's a profound dialogue with history, making it indispensable for cultured travelers","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDB_o3oMjw8-sZEWxSHwF_s&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Asher Mendelsohn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKqRvYW-a_lSUaf5iTtmw6DBfVcf7TfVR491K28IHEfLs9SorY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful museum. Very easy to get in even though it was a crowded Sunday morning. You can pre-buy your art pass (covers 2 other museums as well) or buy a pass at the gate. The queue to buy the pass was longer to enter the museum, so I recommend pre buying the art pass online. The museum is quite big, and there are multiple floors, however when I visited only floors 1 and 2 were open. The world famous Guernica painting by Pablo Picasso is located on the second floor. I like how there’s a cloakroom where you can store all your valuables, and bags. There’s also lots of toilets around the museum. The museum contains a large amount of paintings and other work too, many of them being from Picasso, Dali, and more. But the most popular of all is Guernica, and that can be seen by the sheer amount of people standing around it. Overall museum is great and I enjoy the large collection of art.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAXUzvqZ48DFCyk6ib2H715&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Nicos Stathis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKc-rWmYLiJLBz4mtw01fjprIyd-zvV9OdX_F75kJp_dWx4RvY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I believe one of the most important museums in the world.

It is not just the Guernica, which is astonishing and pivotal indeed, the whole exhibition is a great story telling of how things evolved from late 1800s to mid 1900s, and many artworks and references of some of the most iconic modernism enthusiasts, for example Le Corbusier.

I visited on a Monday, I cannot say it was crowded.

The facade restoration keeps going, not affecting the visitor’s experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBd0vRqhvICgS52QH0HDzpT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Simona","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXIxl36BDvWwaZaehYUjB_o5qPqD55SL2EAL5iQDj96hxaCIkEj=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An amazing gallery, which concentrates on spanish art, but with some very interesting and valuable international links too.
I went on a sunday in the free hour 12:30-14:15 for which i would recommend being there 30 minute earlier since obviously many people do this🤭. Still despite the long queue, people were getting in pretty quickly.
When in, the permanent paintings, distributed in the halls by style (cubism, post cubism, surrealism, etc.) were on the second floor (3rd and 4th floor were temporarily closed).
On the first floor there was an amazing exhibit by Maruja Mallo (i’ll post photos of some of her paintings).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCbj5NOy8CEzlG6-hG8v7uT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-11-3"},{"Name":"Neda Pavlovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWa59zCZJWVxSL2SdtoKVSif7DYFnayRKCeB7JBshDyK-fQVdrC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Reina Sofía is a museum you shouldn’t skip in Madrid. While many go straight to the Prado, Reina Sofía offers a modern, refreshing art experience with masterpieces like Picasso’s Guernica. A big plus is that photography is allowed, unlike in the Prado, which makes the visit more relaxed and enjoyable. The museum café also serves great coffee, perfect for a quick break. Overall, a must-visit spot for art lovers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPVrVcWtyDWZpO40p6k-l5&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-8"}]

[{"Name":"Dean Hobson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIF7jS2r5GGbILesYau6uSZnbIOoTXKZy7V-ChylDM7fsDzkLU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Truly, one of the great galleries of the world. Allow a minimum of three hours to visit, and this is a quickly as you could possibly do it, and that's only giving you a brief look. Be there for the opening, it's advisable to buy your tickets in advance, but tickets are available on the day.... DO NOT BUY TICKETS FROM A SECONDARY WEBSITE, YOU DON'T NEED TO.

There are a number of cafes within the museum, so take a break and a deep breath. If you are wondering about a visit, just come, you won't regret it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBfy4PrrKd78BnlSzqNVrfx&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Sveto Rakovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXELv1h6KNSxjLBZ4irfEkGKjzrypoUHg3izpRjZZ_yFLu388A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The most famous, the most visited (more than a million people a year) and the largest museum in Madrid. I recommend buying tickets in advance so you don't spend too much on the entrance to the museum. The museum primarily contains paintings by famous Spanish painters (Velázquez, El Greco, Mannings, Goya, etc.) and international painters (such as Rubens). You need 2-3 hours for the tour. What irritates me the most is the forbidden photography (which is not even the case in the Vatican museums) and I will never be able to understand what is achieved by this.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDeMdVw6qZyZhdcvTRHuw7P&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Gabriel Radford","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVwcyb-dRAmO-1NM9nQfZHDz6GtwhkUAPGwtqhfxlDBGuIhY8AwKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A transformational experience. To see
Such greatness - the best humanity has to offer. Velásquez obviously a highlight. Caravaggio took my breath away. Audio guides very helpful. Staff good. Crowded yes, but still space to see the art. Pre buying tickets definitely a pro move but won’t help avoid a bit of a line.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA4JiJziSda31JjotVGzUNm&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Rae Zyon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG1QtdDeQPQLETzpCBgyDyGAAEvIdbUpAe1YDVy3QwmQLrwpSA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A magnificent museum, house to famous paintings especially Goya. Absolutely worth the visit, the paintings are beautiful and about three levels of the museum to explore. There's free entry to the museum two hours before closing everyday. Best to be there earlier as the queue gets very long.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDf2hW00MYwX2GfsElbPqd-&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-8"},{"Name":"Priya Nagda Omron","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX8HuThTfcNgDC2fnOs9xiSudL-yOJ_wR3gcNtGq2i6x6jtvnQf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful museum. We skipped the audio tour, but I’d highly recommend it—there’s so much to take in that it can feel overwhelming without guidance. I also learned that two versions of the Mona Lisa were created at the same time, and the earlier version is housed here (something I had no idea about!). It’s fascinating to see how it differs subtly from the version at the Louvre.

No photos are allowed so I will leave you with a picture of the outside of the museum 🙂","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAm3qp1CmId7FMItVlGChd7&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-20"},{"Name":"Gary H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVQ8GQfBLsQr8dF9mAzMMkyVfmOldXWUxiSLLn1GUJd55bjyy0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo del Prado is, quite simply, one of the greatest museums in Europe and an essential cultural experience in Madrid. Spread across six expansive floors, it houses an extraordinary collection that feels both monumental and deeply human, offering a powerful journey through the artistic and historical development of Spain.

What makes the Prado truly exceptional is how its collection reflects the evolution of Spanish identity. Through masterpieces spanning centuries, the museum traces Spain’s transformation from an Arab-ruled nation to one developing its own distinct culture, power, and artistic voice. This narrative unfolds seamlessly as you move through the galleries, giving context and meaning to each period rather than presenting art in isolation.

The breadth and quality of the collection are remarkable. Iconic works by Velázquez, Goya, El Greco, Zurbarán, and Ribera sit alongside masterpieces from other European centers, creating a rich dialogue between Spain and the wider artistic world. Each room feels thoughtfully curated, allowing visitors to appreciate both the technical brilliance and emotional depth of the works.

Despite its scale, the Prado never feels overwhelming. The layout is intuitive, the lighting superb, and the atmosphere calm and reverent, inviting visitors to linger, reflect, and truly absorb the art. It is a museum that rewards both first-time visitors and seasoned art lovers.

The Museo del Prado is not simply a collection of paintings; it is a visual history of a nation—its struggles, triumphs, and soul. Profound, moving, and endlessly fascinating, it stands confidently among Europe’s very finest museums.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8qo90W-d4Te3_RFBIFmFx&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2026-1-10"},{"Name":"Greg Alford","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVKjj-a8Q9L5aAdj7mt9CvLPiJWdOEMyG8vWgLI0KJjdb0Up4e8MQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Spectacular museum. If you go after six and I believe this is auto the majority museums in Spain admission is free the lines were pretty long but it went quickly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBgIJQ5xwinBeF-K95MTusI&fid=0x0:0x1094f07d93ad885a"],"When":"2025-12-21"},{"Name":"bermúdez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG7HzTtczUBS20m-REqDEHZhqoURjoNr2qW0_ZK8dQNkaGeSU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The “no pictures allowed” policy is quite irrational to me, but alright. descriptions: Museum relating city's history back to the Paleolithic period via artifacts, maps & paintings.
owner: {"id":"106333832438272038625","name":"Museo de San Isidro. Los Orígenes de Madrid (Owner)","link":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106333832438272038625"}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ A Hidden Gem of Madrid History – And It’s Free!

If you’re looking for a high-quality cultural experience in the heart of the city, Museo de San Isidro is a fantastic find. It’s a wonderful way to spend an hour or two, especially if you need a break from the Madrid sun in a cool, air-conditioned space.

What to Expect:
The museum is very well-organized and showcases the history of Madrid from prehistoric times to the semi-modern era. It’s filled with fascinating artifacts, including:

Impressive Fossils: Large-scale displays that bring ancient history to life.

Archaeological Finds: Beautifully preserved pottery, tools, and sculptures.

Interactive Exhibits: Plenty of high-quality, interactive elements that make the history engaging for all ages.

Why It’s a Great Visit:
Bilingual Displays: One of the best parts is that all the signage and information are in both Spanish and English, making it very accessible for international visitors.

Quiet & Peaceful: Unlike the major galleries in Madrid, this museum is not crowded, allowing you to slow down and really absorb the details.

Welcoming Atmosphere: The staff is extremely helpful and welcoming from the moment you walk in.

Bonus – Free Entry: Admission is completely FREE, which is incredible given the quality of the exhibits.

Final Thoughts:
Located in the charming Plaza de San Andrés, this museum is a "must" for history buffs or anyone wanting to understand the origins of Madrid. Whether you are admiring the beautiful courtyard or the ancient mammoth fossils, you won't be disappointed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD4JZ891c15EjgOGHvfwluC&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-1-25"},{"Name":"PG G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-KZn3KzHQeyJSRbIkb3rLdzUVXN_dKo03beVE4Nscw7tU7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's free!
Friendly staff.
Lovely courtyard garden.
Fascinating history of Madrid and lots and lots of relics, fossils, ancient tools and artefacts.
We stumbled across this museum after a disappointing attempt to see the nearby Cathedral (closed indefinitely for renovations). We ended up in there for nearly 2 hours!
Highlights:
Mammoth tusk fossil
Stone age tools and flints
Bronze urns
Roman mosaic floor
3-d model of old Madrid walled city
Statues
And it's free!
Please buy a fridge magnet or souvenir to show support!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD_89Y-hArWf1UTpnU6V3iP&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Kevin Yeung","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfr8VfoLnrWAatiNzspZuHOsAYPQ0rQ8QcqYSnL_8Cqky7zGB38w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very informative. Having stopped for a drink on the terrace opposite the museum, we then stopped in as we’re interested in prehistoric history. The museum is great, the people welcoming and the exhibits are very well done. Add in that the signs are in both Spanish and English and the admission is FREE, and you’ve got yourself a wonderful way to spend an hour out of the hot sun in the air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAl9ZcABAnCAaTrnm8Yc553&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2025-6-27"},{"Name":"Tracey Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwskhnIlnEeo-n_0YSv-LxRrghFV-m3Br-gt01qKASRTdlV9o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A charming and insightful museum that delves into the fascinating origins of Madrid! Museo de San Isidro offers a thoughtful journey through the city’s early history - from prehistoric times to the foundation of the modern capital. The exhibits are well-organized, and the building itself, with its lovely courtyard and peaceful atmosphere, adds to the experience.
It’s not a huge museum, but it’s full of interesting artifacts and stories that bring Madrid’s beginnings to life. Entry is free, which makes it even more worthwhile. Some displays could benefit from more English translations, but overall it’s a great cultural stop for anyone interested in history. A quiet gem tucked away in the heart of old Madrid - definitely worth visiting!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXj0TgkTNAuGwMesWnHLHh3gZP_MQOoaZdzbuIwp4tG_MvQdIP7w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A local walking tour guide recommended this quaint, charming museum for a visit. After or before this visit, go to the local mercado (public market) nearby for a stroll and a bite & drink. Entry is free, a beautiful museum preserving history. The museum's got the history of the Spanish. Loads of cool old stuff on display, all laid out really clearly so easy to follow.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBExZULt-3HCI6cxndrKaK2&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-6"},{"Name":"Naveen Perera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo1EOUBZmqNnX9TxAh0YA9pddlpk1dAqohyuijF1dv5BR7eDNB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very impressive small museum that clearly presents the naval history of Spain. Entry is free, which makes it a great stop even if you have limited time. The museum has multiple detailed models of historic ships, maps, and naval instruments that give a good picture of how Spain became a major maritime power.
There is a nice souvenir shop, but no café, so plan accordingly. The museum is well organised and easy to walk through, and you can comfortably visit it in about 45–60 minutes.
Overall, a worthwhile and informative visit, especially if you are interested in history, exploration, or ship models.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAVglefhROliQ0VvDWRhDkC&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-12-24"},{"Name":"Linda Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV13-ZLd5E8gbX7oVINRLkobXoW7aOrzbAdXFra4V8v2e0Q96I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum is located in a gorgeous building with an amazing staircase leading to the museum. Entrance is free with a suggestion donation of 3 euros. There are no lockers and backpacks are not allowed.

The is an audioguide available to use on your phone. Well curated, lovely displays and most placard translated in English describe the incredible history of the Spanish Navy. A must see if interested in all things naval and Spanish  history.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD7vsS9qt10cynH-8O4MnjY&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-28"},{"Name":"Denisa Boar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUz4j1MccDX5a9w1bCEZ9L9NF0O4fR0gp_RcR6pqDA-cwFMYfY2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great museum for passionates and noobies. There is something for everyone: from navigation instruments and tools, to boats, accessories, equipments and uniforms, flags, guns, and fabulous paintings.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCH0O4RAFE3SHslWcmA_aCE&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-1"},{"Name":"Jeremy Lim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUrc1Ud0khZyXU0C0tbctyuaBLhUoJQiepCDtbzj8M2mEPjfP8i=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free museum with beautiful ship models and detailed history of the Spanish navy and the journey to the new world. With a visit if you’re interested. Takes about 45min-1hr to walk through.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCU5xl-OCuKupPM3RHkOjbq&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-8"},{"Name":"Ctgb2","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocItC9iDa9r9g_651odkZll6vFcFOS4APKRO34THBDIzmKIUQQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum in Madrid feels like a hidden historical archive rather than a crowded tourist attraction. Located along the Paseo del Prado, it’s easy to overlook — but once inside, you realize it contains centuries of exploration, warfare, and global expansion.

The highlight is the collection of ship models. They’re incredibly detailed, almost architectural in precision, and they help you understand how naval technology evolved over time. You don’t just read history — you see engineering progress in miniature form.

One of the most fascinating pieces is the early world map attributed to Juan de la Cosa. Seeing the Americas represented in one of the first European maps gives a powerful sense of how people once understood the world.

What I appreciated most was the atmosphere. The museum is quiet and organized chronologically, making it easy to follow Spain’s maritime story from exploration to empire to modern navy. It feels educational without being overwhelming.

Overall, the Naval Museum rewards curiosity. It’s not flashy, but it’s deeply informative — a place where history unfolds patiently rather than dramatically.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Highly recommend for those interested in the topic/theme of the exhibition, which I understand changes from time to time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Juan P Obregon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUTqoYB1dCcA-rFkIjp2Muu-Z58BHjF8fn9IL2pV-EnvSgRp9kLwg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"While small and hence short, the ICO museum is a great visit for people interested in architecture and or photography. Very interesting tour and it was incredible to see the writer’s actual house and all the different rooms, as well as hearing about his life (which was very eventful).Alba was a fantastic tour guide.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDhyXrqSKgw1pdtFqg_95mD&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2026-2-19"},{"Name":"Fray Q","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWjAvpC7kFCp0Xuw6g8w26fl9nOyMKKNvAKSopm3iY4pz-z5uzB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is an excellent opportunity to learn more about one of the biggest writers of all times. Although the furniture is not the same used by Lope, all of it is original from that time. Incredibly, even the books are original copies from XVI and XVII century, which is amazing. I consider Madrid my second home and I highly recommend this visit; the guides are exceptional (at least my guide Laura was!).
Just book it; it’s free.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marta (MartaMoon)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLhCsthabnmP7Cex--6L-j_c1YIFDGHaO33vm4A7XPXGczIYg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Incredible little gem, very close to El Retiro park, and a short distance from Puerta del Sol. The visit and tour is free but you need to book in advance. Knowledgeable and friendly staff. The house is beautiful, and they have tried to restore the look/items as they were when the writer lived there. Highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_hILlyAE&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2025-1-21"},{"Name":"Rawandee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWe5zOuvNsPODJdIRy2KNdK87QbIIsyCNR_X1RZt5jwCTay1kgx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beyond beautiful and impressive. Although you have to take the guided tour and cannot wander on your own, it was still enjoyable.  Guide is in Spanish,  but the lady offered some translation.  I was hoping to find an English version of the detailed museum guide. Maybe this is something to consider. I would come again to see the garden,  it was crowded during my visit. And it is free. Reservation is required.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRyvKqYQ&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2023-4-22"},{"Name":"ismet konuralp (Izzy)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNmmxx-gfhLzECBdXnVlcis0BIh3_9_YFnBRzI4HkDN3xLxltw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best quick free tours you can take. You just drop them an email, and they book a 30 min slot to tour the house. Its a really cool trip. The house is in original condition, the old books on all the shelves are amazing, you get a glimpse into the past. Really recommend it !!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICNmtyE7gE&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2024-1-13"},{"Name":"Rachel Banks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKEGcN_Wz4Vr9XlqFf2nAjPQw3gD5oex92Xx_30v9Yd69hJWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fantastic museum and tour can’t believe it’s totally free we loved it and our guide definitely recommend this to anyone visiting Madrid","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAztpm9345ErRw3lkSUWIDf&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2025-7-31"},{"Name":"Giedre Dubauskiene","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJWKmrOc1dqaDqXRCFTX0pojKxCejY8w_VLwjirVg-c1kFL0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Visiting the Casa-Museo Lope de Vega in Madrid was a captivating journey into the life and times of one of Spain's greatest literary figures. The guided tour offered a glimpse into the world of Lope de Vega, showcasing the restored house of the writer filled with authentic furniture, mirrors, books, and other artefacts. Our guide was exceptionally knowledgeable and brought the history of the house to life with engaging stories about Lope de Vega’s personal life and his impact on Spanish literature. The tour was both educational and enjoyable, the guide’s enthusiasm truly enhanced the visit, making the Lope de Vega House a highlight of my trip to Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICbqLermgE&fid=0x0:0x7e0ad7b5f230117e"],"When":"2024-7-22"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Duncan Parsons","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWWmuOuhbw11S11o3_hEuVi4SoYoTgqQhzbOO1Nw41tYFod3gA96A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free admission. Apart from a remarkable current exhibition....details in the attached photos...there are permanent exhibitions which are themselves well worth seeing. The current temporary exhibition is called  'So many worlds in this one"   It runs until 24 January 2926. If in Madrid don't miss it. It is very special. The whole museum is special and should be on anyone's must see list.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBre-lJP_i0JevFp0RPNa2-&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2025-11-5"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This museum was founded in 1875 by Pedro González de Velasco and is recognized as Spain’s first museum dedicated specifically to anthropology. It is relatively small in scale and follows a traditional museum display approach. The collections focus on cultural anthropology, presenting materials related to clothing, religion, rituals, daily life, and leisure from Asia, Africa, and the Americas.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDsGnHJ3EcZsrMLJd_JR9gI&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2026-1-10"},{"Name":"Emily L","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIT2mX1D5qRGqic4rzLN5KPD-I-HcTVL6tDeweRX1zvDe3DbcE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free after 2pm on Saturday and on sundays, lovely quiet museum with interesting exhibits. The museum had bag storage which required a euro which is refunded after collection. Staff were very helpful and friendly!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nicole","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXtbugfc-OtDQXZC_h7jgjMlSWHpueywVSc-4ez5Zs-h1enWXQp=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free entrance on Sundays and with youth card. 3 floors. One per continent: America, Asia, Africa. Pretty small, you only need 2 hours. Lockers and restrooms available!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID__7PN2wE&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2025-1-28"},{"Name":"Paul Eddy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXU3pcF5e3FsuSWi92wNNW4HFR6KKrGTlxhl5SdJt6yT_U-BlyJ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We decided that we should walk Calle Serrano and came across the Archaeology museum by chance. What a pleasant surprise, very well laid out and filled with great, many from Spain, artefacts. You end up with a great sense of Spain's long history. We loved the Moorish sections with the arches, tiles and an incredible ceiling display. I recommend adding this to your visit and with the entrance fee at 3 Euros it is a great bargain.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID76c3BYA&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2024-8-25"},{"Name":"Carl Flores","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV5-UvVuQJWolN1JyF00AcESD4pizDr4hTKETyJtFOFgYEXy_OPJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I visited the museum on a Sunday 12/22/24 and admission was free. It normally is € 3 for general admission. A great place to view ancient history that one does not get to see in the USA. It was an honor and treat to see ancient history and artifacts in person. Plus you are allowed to take photos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDv44rNBg&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2024-12-23"},{"Name":"david howells","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXv00dm90sqOsiG9tuO5orrJOqUEDS-SlSH6CFfk8dmCBLrELi0Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Nice museum spread over three floors with friendly staff and just a €3 entry fee. Nicely laid out but the content is quite basic. The exhibits are just informative generally. This is a high school level exhibition really but good enough to pass away an hour or so.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChmqjJMw&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2023-2-10"},{"Name":"Bret Bowman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVGD8UU6n0pSe3DnnoMF9LV7M8K2xYwXPGsIE1BfmUYZUf70Ir6=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I arrived this morning right at about 11am and was let in free. But the cost of €3 (read in another review below) is will worth your time to see the exhibits on display here.

Wow! Spain has definitely made their historical mark on the world. Ground floor is Asia (mostly Philippines). First floor is Africa. Second floor is Americas. This museum is a great reminder how much influence Spain has had throughout the years of history following their glory years of exploration and colonization.

You can probably get through this museum within an hour if you really rush. Two hours is more appropriate to see and appreciate everything. I went through in about 90 minutes or so and really feel enlightened by everything I saw and read.

I snapped some pictures, but they don't really do justice, so make sure you go if you can carve out time. It's well worth it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID2mK-_Ug&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2022-5-5"}]

The exhibitions were absolutely stunning, they are very impressive and informative. This is a great place for groups of all ages, lots to see and do. Very clean, staff is very friendly and knowledgeable, lots of places to eat and very nice gift shop. The price was very reasonable, would most definitely recommend this place to family and friends. We had a wonderful time and were glad we stopped by!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC5nLutSg&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2023-10-11"},{"Name":"Dalila Mesta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHTDyQeyc2dn57Z7UrayDN2Y0oZVxuMSL4Qcth4eWoQSu3Zrjd=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great museum! And free
All the staff was great and very friendly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDFZMsVrcyOEFBkKeNygclt&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2026-1-30"},{"Name":"lm c","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWISjXvaWyNRz6ehSH7UZuvO9goelI8qsATNy1SgZ6nwZETrl7w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stumble upon this museum not knowing what to expect. This is worthwhile if you have time in Madrid. Most displays have English. The gift shop provides first day cover for coins and some stamps.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDdpf2r6AE&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2024-2-25"},{"Name":"Fahad Butt","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUwUTQwiBUM0rOwhvhcAjPsICNs5orUnpOKJWsr8lqTujUh0gBT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I write a review using google maps website which is not showing here.

[{"Name":"Jason Allen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUUhLijp2eBzhzQCOL96vMfN8YxmQjlBZ5gqel7gvQfNtR67x5k=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My 14 year old daughter enjoyed our trip to the museum on Saturday 11th November. It's less than a ten minute walk from Chamartín station and we used Google Maps to find our way there. On arrival there's an App you can download and can listen to all the info inside the museum in English (possibly other languages too). You can enter two of the buses inside the exhibition. All in all you're there for around an hour and it's good value for money.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDF94jDNA&fid=0x0:0x805d8dabc5d1cdd7"],"When":"2023-11-11"},{"Name":"Manuel Gonzalez Vazquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7DEtHZo8-9cH9OphhBsTFwF_pw91tcJyp1d4OGjmYWJ_mo1KK2w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The Museo de la EMT in Madrid is dedicated to the history of the city's municipal transport company. It features a collection of vintage buses and offers virtual visits and audio guides. The museum is open on weekends.
It is a nice collection of the different models that were seen in Madrid over the years as well as maintenance and operational material
For people like me, it is a nostalgic journey back in time to some of the coaches we rode as children.
You need to buy your tickets in advance on the museum's EMT website

Be aware that the museum only opens on weekends during some months. For 2025, the opening times are:

Opening calendar for 2025
Every Saturday, Sunday and public holiday from March 1 to July 13 and from September 6 to November 16
Visiting hours: 10:00 – 11:30 – 13:00 – 17:00 hours.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Wai Kin Vinson Kong","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJxRNLlIIivUE1fHYHzwjU5pINcTjC85kcQ1nBwVdVzAEearttB=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went to the museum without buying a ticket beforehand. The overall experience was extremely unfriendly for tourists, and I left feeling greatly disappointed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICToofKbQ&fid=0x0:0x805d8dabc5d1cdd7"],"When":"2024-5-12"},{"Name":"Yaraslau Zauharodni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLKgK_E46dUv9VJihj1cdxhKAHIlLFGMzgRUu8v7oRb9kAAWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was closed on Wednesday. Easiest to take the elevator down.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDrsJmxzgE&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2024-7-13"},{"Name":"Andrew Kram","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWTriD2J6Q11TFDLlyBL-s_erfIEPkdRh_17lpT655psOD2Pbj-7g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not call that a museum.😭😭","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Catalin Pascu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIa5EROUBV6pTPLGBll6MmRpyKADQCFzERhxsvzn0Y2XMbOcw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best museum!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Trachemysta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm05Blgf4TlryacKfxQE8ry9uK6kIOqqVl9lSDqnmtfdUrfSTocQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Parte de los museos del metro de Madrid, situado en la planta -2 de la estación de gran vía (antes de pasar los tornos, por lo que se puede ver gratis). Está justo donde el ascensor, pudiéndose acceder desde la calle directamente. Se trata de una exposición con unos cuantos paneles en la pared de la historia del metro y de la estación de gran vía, con carteles informativos y objetos encontrados durante la construcción, así como algunas cosas históricas del metro. Está bastante chulo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCAkcP-Rg&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2025-2-1"},{"Name":"Aran A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfH4gTdkqeLJoNdPTUdr4gPj6MGWSVPQd-CSuAwybErQs_eJQxFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"No hace falta pagar billete para verlo son unas tres o cuatro vitrinas donde se pueden ver cosas encontradas y antiguas del metro. Depende de lo interesado que estés pero se ve en menos de 5 minutos. Lo más interesante para mí es el emblema- escudo de Madrid","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRy8uJKA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2023-5-3"},{"Name":"Jordi Fajula","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJbvMkNWXEHot-zzw0mI3KEF1Ch8NJprx_I6RSrSHOzsdoFvA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Me ha parecido una forma interesante de conocer cómo fue una parte de la reforma de lo que es hoy Gran Vía. Puedes ver un emblema de madrid","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Raúl Fernández López","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX607swNaQjjzE_mhMfXHDElPsOGK5mKK6UpWfARpRFuleEVsKylg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una parte importante de la historia de Madrid es la historia del metro de Madrid.

Metro a través de este pequeñísimo museo muestra todo lo que se encontraron tras la remodelación de la estación de metro de Gran Vía. De los restos a destacar fue el emblema de Madrid.

Museo de entrada gratuita ya que se sitúa fuera de los tornos del metro y se halla en la segunda planta.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDW8p7fHA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2022-3-20"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo en el que se exponen diferentes piezas procedentes de las excavaciones arqueológicas realizadas en el año 2019 durante las obras de ampliación y remodelación de la estación. Entre ellas se encuentran restos del antiguo ascensor que daba acceso al metro, escalones con publicidad, un escudo de la ciudad, y numerosos enseres y objetos cotidianos con más de cien años","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICRy7OQOw&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2023-4-10"}]

Arts change frequently (it's a private collection) from bizant art to contemporary","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alexander Lipovetskiy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWAXHD6NVrwuD-Q8_6OuD3zViGIEb3NYE_tST12dAhLGUYzcwbo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I came to see works of Salvador Dali as it was advertised online, but found nothing. A stuff member confirmed there is no such at this time. That made me very sad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"María del Mar Fernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX-uXJ0rxzjyDAumGDWr7TxBqGeXc_jRWk6wHxBWYeWgfANeFo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"La visita a las exposiciones temporales ofrece una experiencia correcta. La selección de temas es variada, abarcando diferentes periodos y artistas, lo que permite tener una visión general de distintas corrientes.

La organización de las salas es funcional y el recorrido está estructurado de una manera lógica. Sin embargo, en ocasiones, la cantidad de visitantes puede dificultar la observación detallada de las obras, especialmente en las muestras más populares, por lo que se recomienda comprar la entrada con antelación para asegurar el acceso en la hora deseada.

La información que acompaña a las obras es adecuada, aunque a veces podría ser más extensa para proporcionar un contexto más profundo. En general, es una opción a considerar para complementar la visita a la colección permanente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marga Corchero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVlWyxEwOZiFHq03iR3sWkk2iGO95iBcTWB8VMHtbu7Y-PoexzTYg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Maravillosa exposición de Gabrielle Münter. El museo no solo vale por su gran colección de arte sino también por las exposiciones temporales.
Esta concretamente merece mucho la pena y está hasta febrero","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID_mfqENw&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2025-1-25"},{"Name":"Sara Sáez (Viña)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLaQkzvq_KUP1rQ3jxoAhpwmnL9tbsunfSGD6oxVueQTHg0b61=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una exposición increíble que aún puedes disfrutar. "La máquina Magritte” aún estará en este espacio hasta el 30 de enero. El Thyssen es un museo muy especial para mí. Disfruto con la selección de exposiciones temporales que realizan. Esta de Magritte ha sido la última, deseando conocer las próximas obras que se podrán disfrutar.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDmhM61mgE&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2022-1-18"},{"Name":"Jimena Amor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0t77PfCMG8QPjhOf62EKoGK6omfUh4mdhLCAozQjqQXqt7DOd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nosotras visitamos Maestras. Fue arte en toda su esencia, dieron voz a artistas femeninas que también revolucionaron y aportaron a la historia del arte. La obra expuesta, fantástica y muy bien seleccionada desde mi punto de vista (aparecen artistas como Artemisia Gentileschi, Maruja Mallo, Tamara Lempicka, Frida Kahlo...).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDt5PqqSQ&fid=0x0:0x9e3319c847b19fdc"],"When":"2024-2-2"},{"Name":"fernandonajera19","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU18Uk_JwcFouWUrDWav4h9vFfKY6pCasSNfrbMM2eJucy2joKr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"La exposicion "la Reina Roja de Palenque" es simplemente un timo. Te hacen pagar la entrada al museo para luego ver unos pocos objetos en una habitación del tamaño de mi salón. Se supone que estas acciones van a salvar la maltrecha relación politica con Mexico.... No se yo....","Images":null,"When":""}]

It is a timed entry as this is somehow comparable to the Prado lol","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCbuyqm336NkHDpOCGaREyB&fid=0x0:0x647d32fb7c26d0c3"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Forever 21","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXeydQtCkbieSH3Jfg5kGr9DHxgf2cfwEIwZJGMcFzg0Baayj_cEA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Ignored at reception until I proceeded to enter the tour on my own, denied the first time because my reservation was for a later time so returned later - was told an English tour was no issue but when I returned later I needed to wait for an English guide eventhough supposedly it had been prearranged weeks prior when my booking was made. The museum itself is very small with maybe 20 exhibits targeted to children.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD1KzamxfmVsl9O0K533_bg&fid=0x0:0x647d32fb7c26d0c3"],"When":"2025-11-27"},{"Name":"Amera Althobaity","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-WSzW1-yKo4-t9z4wElyCejKqyAdPTJpiklMp69GSMYSq_g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Museum of Light (Museo de la Luz) – Madrid
